> And the priest shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar of sweet incense before the LORD, which is in the tabernacle of the congregation; and shall pour all the blood of the bullock at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering, which is at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

**Leviticus 4:13** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And if the whole congregation of Israel sin through ignorance, and the thing be hid from the eyes of the assembly, and they have done somewhat against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which should not be done, and are guilty;

**Leviticus 4:15** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock before the LORD: and the bullock shall be killed before the LORD.

**Leviticus 4:17** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall dip his finger in some of the blood, and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD, even before the vail.

**Leviticus 4:18** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar which is before the LORD, that is in the tabernacle of the congregation, and shall pour out all the blood at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering, which is at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

**Leviticus 4:22** — `יְהוָ֨ה` (YHWH)
> When a ruler hath sinned, and done somewhat through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD his God concerning things which should not be done, and is guilty;

**Leviticus 4:24** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the goat, and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the LORD: it is a sin offering.

**Leviticus 4:27** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And if any one of the common people sin through ignorance, while he doeth somewhat against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done, and be guilty;

**Leviticus 4:31** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat is taken away from off the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall burn it upon the altar for a sweet savour unto the LORD; and the priest shall make an atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him.

**Leviticus 4:35** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat of the lamb is taken away from the sacrifice of the peace offerings; and the priest shall burn them upon the altar, according to the offerings made by fire unto the LORD: and the priest shall make an atonement for his sin that he hath committed, and it shall be forgiven him.

**Leviticus 5:6** — `יהוָ֡ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the LORD for his sin which he hath sinned, a female from the flock, a lamb or a kid of the goats, for a sin offering; and the priest shall make an atonement for him concerning his sin.

**Leviticus 5:7** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And if he be not able to bring a lamb, then he shall bring for his trespass, which he hath committed, two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, unto the LORD; one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering.

**Leviticus 5:12** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Then shall he bring it to the priest, and the priest shall take his handful of it, even a memorial thereof, and burn it on the altar, according to the offerings made by fire unto the LORD: it is a sin offering.

**Leviticus 5:14** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 5:15** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> If a soul commit a trespass, and sin through ignorance, in the holy things of the LORD; then he shall bring for his trespass unto the LORD a ram without blemish out of the flocks, with thy estimation by shekels of silver, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for a trespass offering:

**Leviticus 5:19** — `יהוָֽה׃ פ` (YHWH)
> It is a trespass offering: he hath certainly trespassed against the LORD.

**Leviticus 6:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 6:2** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> If a soul sin, and commit a trespass against the LORD, and lie unto his neighbour in that which was delivered him to keep, or in fellowship, or in a thing taken away by violence, or hath deceived his neighbour;

**Leviticus 6:6** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the LORD, a ram without blemish out of the flock, with thy estimation, for a trespass offering, unto the priest:

**Leviticus 6:7** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall make an atonement for him before the LORD: and it shall be forgiven him for any thing of all that he hath done in trespassing therein.

**Leviticus 6:8** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 6:14** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And this is the law of the meat offering: the sons of Aaron shall offer it before the LORD, before the altar.

**Leviticus 6:15** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And he shall take of it his handful, of the flour of the meat offering, and of the oil thereof, and all the frankincense which is upon the meat offering, and shall burn it upon the altar for a sweet savour, even the memorial of it, unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 6:18** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> All the males among the children of Aaron shall eat of it. It shall be a statute for ever in your generations concerning the offerings of the LORD made by fire: every one that toucheth them shall be holy.

**Leviticus 6:20** — `יהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> This is the offering of Aaron and of his sons, which they shall offer unto the LORD in the day when he is anointed; the tenth part of an ephah of fine flour for a meat offering perpetual, half of it in the morning, and half thereof at night.

**Leviticus 6:21** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> In a pan it shall be made with oil; and when it is baken, thou shalt bring it in: and the baken pieces of the meat offering shalt thou offer for a sweet savour unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 6:22** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest of his sons that is anointed in his stead shall offer it: it is a statute for ever unto the LORD; it shall be wholly burnt.

**Leviticus 6:24** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 6:25** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, saying, This is the law of the sin offering: In the place where the burnt offering is killed shall the sin offering be killed before the LORD: it is most holy.

**Leviticus 7:5** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall burn them upon the altar for an offering made by fire unto the LORD: it is a trespass offering.

**Leviticus 7:11** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which he shall offer unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 7:14** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And of it he shall offer one out of the whole oblation for an heave offering unto the LORD, and it shall be the priest's that sprinkleth the blood of the peace offerings.

**Leviticus 7:20** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> But the soul that eateth of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings, that pertain unto the LORD, having his uncleanness upon him, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.

**Leviticus 7:21** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Moreover the soul that shall touch any unclean thing, as the uncleanness of man, or any unclean beast, or any abominable unclean thing, and eat of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which pertain unto the LORD, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.

**Leviticus 7:25** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For whosoever eateth the fat of the beast, of which men offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD, even the soul that eateth it shall be cut off from his people.

**Leviticus 7:28** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 7:29** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, He that offereth the sacrifice of his peace offerings unto the LORD shall bring his oblation unto the LORD of the sacrifice of his peace offerings.

**Leviticus 7:30** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> His own hands shall bring the offerings of the LORD made by fire, the fat with the breast, it shall he bring, that the breast may be waved for a wave offering before the LORD.

**Leviticus 7:35** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> This is the portion of the anointing of Aaron, and of the anointing of his sons, out of the offerings of the LORD made by fire, in the day when he presented them to minister unto the LORD in the priest's office;

**Leviticus 7:36** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> Which the LORD commanded to be given them of the children of Israel, in the day that he anointed them, by a statute for ever throughout their generations.

**Leviticus 7:38** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> Which the LORD commanded Moses in mount Sinai, in the day that he commanded the children of Israel to offer their oblations unto the LORD, in the wilderness of Sinai.

**Leviticus 8:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 8:4** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses did as the LORD commanded him; and the assembly was gathered together unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

**Leviticus 8:5** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto the congregation, This is the thing which the LORD commanded to be done.

**Leviticus 8:9** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And he put the mitre upon his head; also upon the mitre, even upon his forefront, did he put the golden plate, the holy crown; as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Leviticus 8:13** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses brought Aaron's sons, and put coats upon them, and girded them with girdles, and put bonnets upon them; as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Leviticus 8:17** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> But the bullock, and his hide, his flesh, and his dung, he burnt with fire without the camp; as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Leviticus 8:26** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And out of the basket of unleavened bread, that was before the LORD, he took one unleavened cake, and a cake of oiled bread, and one wafer, and put them on the fat, and upon the right shoulder:

**Leviticus 8:27** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And he put all upon Aaron's hands, and upon his sons' hands, and waved them for a wave offering before the LORD.

**Leviticus 8:28** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And Moses took them from off their hands, and burnt them on the altar upon the burnt offering: they were consecrations for a sweet savour: it is an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 8:29** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses took the breast, and waved it for a wave offering before the LORD: for of the ram of consecration it was Moses' part; as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Leviticus 8:34** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> As he hath done this day, so the LORD hath commanded to do, to make an atonement for you.

**Leviticus 8:35** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Therefore shall ye abide at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation day and night seven days, and keep the charge of the LORD, that ye die not: for so I am commanded.

**Leviticus 8:36** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> So Aaron and his sons did all things which the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses.

**Leviticus 9:2** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And he said unto Aaron, Take thee a young calf for a sin offering, and a ram for a burnt offering, without blemish, and offer them before the LORD.

**Leviticus 9:4** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Also a bullock and a ram for peace offerings, to sacrifice before the LORD; and a meat offering mingled with oil: for to day the LORD will appear unto you.

**Leviticus 9:5** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And they brought that which Moses commanded before the tabernacle of the congregation: and all the congregation drew near and stood before the LORD.

**Leviticus 9:6** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said, This is the thing which the LORD commanded that ye should do: and the glory of the LORD shall appear unto you.

**Leviticus 9:7** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto Aaron, Go unto the altar, and offer thy sin offering, and thy burnt offering, and make an atonement for thyself, and for the people: and offer the offering of the people, and make an atonement for them; as the LORD commanded.

**Leviticus 9:10** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> But the fat, and the kidneys, and the caul above the liver of the sin offering, he burnt upon the altar; as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Leviticus 9:23** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses and Aaron went into the tabernacle of the congregation, and came out, and blessed the people: and the glory of the LORD appeared unto all the people.

**Leviticus 9:24** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And there came a fire out from before the LORD, and consumed upon the altar the burnt offering and the fat: which when all the people saw, they shouted, and fell on their faces.

**Leviticus 10:1** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And Nadab and Abihu, the sons of Aaron, took either of them his censer, and put fire therein, and put incense thereon, and offered strange fire before the LORD, which he commanded them not.

**Leviticus 10:2** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And there went out fire from the LORD, and devoured them, and they died before the LORD.

**Leviticus 10:3** — `יְהוָ֤ה׀` (YHWH)
> Then Moses said unto Aaron, This is it that the LORD spake, saying, I will be sanctified in them that come nigh me, and before all the people I will be glorified. And Aaron held his peace.

**Leviticus 10:6** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons, Uncover not your heads, neither rend your clothes; lest ye die, and lest wrath come upon all the people: but let your brethren, the whole house of Israel, bewail the burning which the LORD hath kindled.

**Leviticus 10:7** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall not go out from the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, lest ye die: for the anointing oil of the LORD is upon you. And they did according to the word of Moses.

**Leviticus 10:11** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And that ye may teach the children of Israel all the statutes which the LORD hath spoken unto them by the hand of Moses.

**Leviticus 10:12** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses spake unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons that were left, Take the meat offering that remaineth of the offerings of the LORD made by fire, and eat it without leaven beside the altar: for it is most holy:

**Leviticus 10:13** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall eat it in the holy place, because it is thy due, and thy sons' due, of the sacrifices of the LORD made by fire: for so I am commanded.

**Leviticus 10:15** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The heave shoulder and the wave breast shall they bring with the offerings made by fire of the fat, to wave it for a wave offering before the LORD; and it shall be thine, and thy sons' with thee, by a statute for ever; as the LORD hath commanded.

**Leviticus 10:17** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Wherefore have ye not eaten the sin offering in the holy place, seeing it is most holy, and God hath given it you to bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD?

**Leviticus 10:19** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And Aaron said unto Moses, Behold, this day have they offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD; and such things have befallen me: and if I had eaten the sin offering to day, should it have been accepted in the sight of the LORD?

**Leviticus 11:1** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses and to Aaron, saying unto them,

**Leviticus 11:44** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> For I am the LORD your God: ye shall therefore sanctify yourselves, and ye shall be holy; for I am holy: neither shall ye defile yourselves with any manner of creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.

**Leviticus 11:45** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> For I am the LORD that bringeth you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: ye shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.

**Leviticus 12:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 14:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 14:12** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall take one he lamb, and offer him for a trespass offering, and the log of oil, and wave them for a wave offering before the LORD:

**Leviticus 14:16** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall dip his right finger in the oil that is in his left hand, and shall sprinkle of the oil with his finger seven times before the LORD:

**Leviticus 14:18** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And the remnant of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall pour upon the head of him that is to be cleansed: and the priest shall make an atonement for him before the LORD.

**Leviticus 14:23** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And he shall bring them on the eighth day for his cleansing unto the priest, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, before the LORD.

**Leviticus 14:24** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall take the lamb of the trespass offering, and the log of oil, and the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the LORD:

**Leviticus 14:27** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left hand seven times before the LORD:

**Leviticus 14:29** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And the rest of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall put upon the head of him that is to be cleansed, to make an atonement for him before the LORD.

**Leviticus 14:31** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Even such as he is able to get, the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering, with the meat offering: and the priest shall make an atonement for him that is to be cleansed before the LORD.

**Leviticus 14:33** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

**Leviticus 15:1** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses and to Aaron, saying,

**Leviticus 15:14** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And on the eighth day he shall take to him two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, and come before the LORD unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and give them unto the priest:

**Leviticus 15:15** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall offer them, the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering; and the priest shall make an atonement for him before the LORD for his issue.

**Leviticus 16:1** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron, when they offered before the LORD, and died;

**Leviticus 16:2** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Moses, Speak unto Aaron thy brother, that he come not at all times into the holy place within the vail before the mercy seat, which is upon the ark; that he die not: for I will appear in the cloud upon the mercy seat.

**Leviticus 16:7** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall take the two goats, and present them before the LORD at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

**Leviticus 16:8** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And Aaron shall cast lots upon the two goats; one lot for the LORD, and the other lot for the scapegoat.

**Leviticus 16:9** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Aaron shall bring the goat upon which the LORD'S lot fell, and offer him for a sin offering.

**Leviticus 16:10** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> But the goat, on which the lot fell to be the scapegoat, shall be presented alive before the LORD, to make an atonement with him, and to let him go for a scapegoat into the wilderness.

**Leviticus 16:12** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall take a censer full of burning coals of fire from off the altar before the LORD, and his hands full of sweet incense beaten small, and bring it within the vail:

**Leviticus 16:13** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall put the incense upon the fire before the LORD, that the cloud of the incense may cover the mercy seat that is upon the testimony, that he die not:

**Leviticus 16:30** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> For on that day shall the priest make an atonement for you, to cleanse you, that ye may be clean from all your sins before the LORD.

**Leviticus 16:34** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And this shall be an everlasting statute unto you, to make an atonement for the children of Israel for all their sins once a year. And he did as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Leviticus 17:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 17:2** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons, and unto all the children of Israel, and say unto them; This is the thing which the LORD hath commanded, saying,

**Leviticus 17:4** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And bringeth it not unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, to offer an offering unto the LORD before the tabernacle of the LORD; blood shall be imputed unto that man; he hath shed blood; and that man shall be cut off from among his people:

**Leviticus 17:5** — `יהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> To the end that the children of Israel may bring their sacrifices, which they offer in the open field, even that they may bring them unto the LORD, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, unto the priest, and offer them for peace offerings unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 17:6** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall sprinkle the blood upon the altar of the LORD at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and burn the fat for a sweet savour unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 17:9** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And bringeth it not unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, to offer it unto the LORD; even that man shall be cut off from among his people.

**Leviticus 18:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 18:2** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 18:4** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Ye shall do my judgments, and keep mine ordinances, to walk therein: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 18:5** — `יְהוָֽה׃ ס` (YHWH)
> Ye shall therefore keep my statutes, and my judgments: which if a man do, he shall live in them: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 18:21** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And thou shalt not let any of thy seed pass through the fire to Molech, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 18:30** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Therefore shall ye keep mine ordinance, that ye commit not any one of these abominable customs, which were committed before you, and that ye defile not yourselves therein: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 19:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 19:2** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Speak unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, and say unto them, Ye shall be holy: for I the LORD your God am holy.

**Leviticus 19:3** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Ye shall fear every man his mother, and his father, and keep my sabbaths: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 19:4** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Turn ye not unto idols, nor make to yourselves molten gods: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 19:5** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And if ye offer a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the LORD, ye shall offer it at your own will.

**Leviticus 19:8** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Therefore every one that eateth it shall bear his iniquity, because he hath profaned the hallowed thing of the LORD: and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

**Leviticus 19:12** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And ye shall not swear by my name falsely, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 19:14** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Thou shalt not curse the deaf, nor put a stumblingblock before the blind, but shalt fear thy God: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 19:16** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Thou shalt not go up and down as a talebearer among thy people: neither shalt thou stand against the blood of thy neighbour: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 19:18** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 19:21** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the LORD, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, even a ram for a trespass offering.

**Leviticus 19:22** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall make an atonement for him with the ram of the trespass offering before the LORD for his sin which he hath done: and the sin which he hath done shall be forgiven him.

**Leviticus 19:24** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> But in the fourth year all the fruit thereof shall be holy to praise the LORD withal.

**Leviticus 19:25** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And in the fifth year shall ye eat of the fruit thereof, that it may yield unto you the increase thereof: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 19:28** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Ye shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead, nor print any marks upon you: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 19:30** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Ye shall keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 19:31** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Regard not them that have familiar spirits, neither seek after wizards, to be defiled by them: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 19:32** — `יְהוָֽה׃ פ` (YHWH)
> Thou shalt rise up before the hoary head, and honour the face of the old man, and fear thy God: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 19:34** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> But the stranger that dwelleth with you shall be unto you as one born among you, and thou shalt love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 19:36** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin, shall ye have: I am the LORD your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt.

**Leviticus 19:37** — `יְהוָֽה׃ פ` (YHWH)
> Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes, and all my judgments, and do them: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 20:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 20:8** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall keep my statutes, and do them: I am the LORD which sanctify you.

**Leviticus 20:24** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> But I have said unto you, Ye shall inherit their land, and I will give it unto you to possess it, a land that floweth with milk and honey: I am the LORD your God, which have separated you from other people.

**Leviticus 20:26** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall be holy unto me: for I the LORD am holy, and have severed you from other people, that ye should be mine.

**Leviticus 21:1** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Moses, Speak unto the priests the sons of Aaron, and say unto them, There shall none be defiled for the dead among his people:

**Leviticus 21:6** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> They shall be holy unto their God, and not profane the name of their God: for the offerings of the LORD made by fire, and the bread of their God, they do offer: therefore they shall be holy.

**Leviticus 21:8** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Thou shalt sanctify him therefore; for he offereth the bread of thy God: he shall be holy unto thee: for I the LORD, which sanctify you, am holy.

**Leviticus 21:12** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Neither shall he go out of the sanctuary, nor profane the sanctuary of his God; for the crown of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 21:16** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 21:21** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> No man that hath a blemish of the seed of Aaron the priest shall come nigh to offer the offerings of the LORD made by fire: he hath a blemish; he shall not come nigh to offer the bread of his God.

**Leviticus 21:23** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Only he shall not go in unto the vail, nor come nigh unto the altar, because he hath a blemish; that he profane not my sanctuaries: for I the LORD do sanctify them.

**Leviticus 22:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 22:2** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, that they separate themselves from the holy things of the children of Israel, and that they profane not my holy name in those things which they hallow unto me: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 22:3** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Say unto them, Whosoever he be of all your seed among your generations, that goeth unto the holy things, which the children of Israel hallow unto the LORD, having his uncleanness upon him, that soul shall be cut off from my presence: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 22:8** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> That which dieth of itself, or is torn with beasts, he shall not eat to defile himself therewith: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 22:9** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> They shall therefore keep mine ordinance, lest they bear sin for it, and die therefore, if they profane it: I the LORD do sanctify them.

**Leviticus 22:15** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And they shall not profane the holy things of the children of Israel, which they offer unto the LORD;

**Leviticus 22:16** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Or suffer them to bear the iniquity of trespass, when they eat their holy things: for I the LORD do sanctify them.

**Leviticus 22:17** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 22:18** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Speak unto Aaron, and to his sons, and unto all the children of Israel, and say unto them, Whatsoever he be of the house of Israel, or of the strangers in Israel, that will offer his oblation for all his vows, and for all his freewill offerings, which they will offer unto the LORD for a burnt offering;

**Leviticus 22:22** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Blind, or broken, or maimed, or having a wen, or scurvy, or scabbed, ye shall not offer these unto the LORD, nor make an offering by fire of them upon the altar unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 22:24** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Ye shall not offer unto the LORD that which is bruised, or crushed, or broken, or cut; neither shall ye make any offering thereof in your land.

**Leviticus 22:26** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 22:27** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> When a bullock, or a sheep, or a goat, is brought forth, then it shall be seven days under the dam; and from the eighth day and thenceforth it shall be accepted for an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 22:29** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And when ye will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto the LORD, offer it at your own will.

**Leviticus 22:30** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> On the same day it shall be eaten up; ye shall leave none of it until the morrow: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 22:31** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Therefore shall ye keep my commandments, and do them: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 22:32** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Neither shall ye profane my holy name; but I will be hallowed among the children of Israel: I am the LORD which hallow you,

**Leviticus 22:33** — `יְהוָֽה׃ פ` (YHWH)
> That brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 23:2** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, Concerning the feasts of the LORD, which ye shall proclaim to be holy convocations, even these are my feasts.

**Leviticus 23:3** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Six days shall work be done: but the seventh day is the sabbath of rest, an holy convocation; ye shall do no work therein: it is the sabbath of the LORD in all your dwellings.

**Leviticus 23:4** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> These are the feasts of the LORD, even holy convocations, which ye shall proclaim in their seasons.

**Leviticus 23:6** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the feast of unleavened bread unto the LORD: seven days ye must eat unleavened bread.

**Leviticus 23:8** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> But ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD seven days: in the seventh day is an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.

**Leviticus 23:9** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 23:11** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall wave the sheaf before the LORD, to be accepted for you: on the morrow after the sabbath the priest shall wave it.

**Leviticus 23:12** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And ye shall offer that day when ye wave the sheaf an he lamb without blemish of the first year for a burnt offering unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:13** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the meat offering thereof shall be two tenth deals of fine flour mingled with oil, an offering made by fire unto the LORD for a sweet savour: and the drink offering thereof shall be of wine, the fourth part of an hin.

**Leviticus 23:16** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Even unto the morrow after the seventh sabbath shall ye number fifty days; and ye shall offer a new meat offering unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:17** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Ye shall bring out of your habitations two wave loaves of two tenth deals: they shall be of fine flour; they shall be baken with leaven; they are the firstfruits unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:18** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall offer with the bread seven lambs without blemish of the first year, and one young bullock, and two rams: they shall be for a burnt offering unto the LORD, with their meat offering, and their drink offerings, even an offering made by fire, of sweet savour unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:20** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits for a wave offering before the LORD, with the two lambs: they shall be holy to the LORD for the priest.

**Leviticus 23:22** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not make clean riddance of the corners of thy field when thou reapest, neither shalt thou gather any gleaning of thy harvest: thou shalt leave them unto the poor, and to the stranger: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 23:23** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 23:25** — `יהוָֽה׃ ס` (YHWH)
> Ye shall do no servile work therein: but ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:26** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 23:27** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Also on the tenth day of this seventh month there shall be a day of atonement: it shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall afflict your souls, and offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:28** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall do no work in that same day: for it is a day of atonement, to make an atonement for you before the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 23:33** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 23:34** — `יהֹוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, The fifteenth day of this seventh month shall be the feast of tabernacles for seven days unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:36** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Seven days ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD: on the eighth day shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD: it is a solemn assembly; and ye shall do no servile work therein.

**Leviticus 23:37** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> These are the feasts of the LORD, which ye shall proclaim to be holy convocations, to offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD, a burnt offering, and a meat offering, a sacrifice, and drink offerings, every thing upon his day:

**Leviticus 23:38** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Beside the sabbaths of the LORD, and beside your gifts, and beside all your vows, and beside all your freewill offerings, which ye give unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:39** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Also in the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when ye have gathered in the fruit of the land, ye shall keep a feast unto the LORD seven days: on the first day shall be a sabbath, and on the eighth day shall be a sabbath.

**Leviticus 23:40** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall take you on the first day the boughs of goodly trees, branches of palm trees, and the boughs of thick trees, and willows of the brook; and ye shall rejoice before the LORD your God seven days.

**Leviticus 23:41** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall keep it a feast unto the LORD seven days in the year. It shall be a statute for ever in your generations: ye shall celebrate it in the seventh month.

**Leviticus 23:43** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> That your generations may know that I made the children of Israel to dwell in booths, when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 23:44** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses declared unto the children of Israel the feasts of the LORD.

**Leviticus 24:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 24:3** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Without the vail of the testimony, in the tabernacle of the congregation, shall Aaron order it from the evening unto the morning before the LORD continually: it shall be a statute for ever in your generations.

**Leviticus 24:4** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> He shall order the lamps upon the pure candlestick before the LORD continually.

**Leviticus 24:6** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And thou shalt set them in two rows, six on a row, upon the pure table before the LORD.

**Leviticus 24:7** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And thou shalt put pure frankincense upon each row, that it may be on the bread for a memorial, even an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 24:8** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Every sabbath he shall set it in order before the LORD continually, being taken from the children of Israel by an everlasting covenant.

**Leviticus 24:9** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And it shall be Aaron's and his sons'; and they shall eat it in the holy place: for it is most holy unto him of the offerings of the LORD made by fire by a perpetual statute.

**Leviticus 24:13** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 24:16** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And he that blasphemeth the name of the LORD, he shall surely be put to death, and all the congregation shall certainly stone him: as well the stranger, as he that is born in the land, when he blasphemeth the name of the LORD, shall be put to death.

**Leviticus 24:22** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Ye shall have one manner of law, as well for the stranger, as for one of your own country: for I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 24:23** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses spake to the children of Israel, that they should bring forth him that had cursed out of the camp, and stone him with stones. And the children of Israel did as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Leviticus 25:1** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses in mount Sinai, saying,

**Leviticus 25:2** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land which I give you, then shall the land keep a sabbath unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 25:4** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> But in the seventh year shall be a sabbath of rest unto the land, a sabbath for the LORD: thou shalt neither sow thy field, nor prune thy vineyard.

**Leviticus 25:17** — `יְהֹוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Ye shall not therefore oppress one another; but thou shalt fear thy God: for I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 25:38** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> I am the LORD your God, which brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, to give you the land of Canaan, and to be your God.

**Leviticus 25:55** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> For unto me the children of Israel are servants; they are my servants whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 26:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Ye shall make you no idols nor graven image, neither rear you up a standing image, neither shall ye set up any image of stone in your land, to bow down unto it: for I am the LORD your God.

**Leviticus 26:2** — `יְהוָֽה׃ ס` (YHWH)
> Ye shall keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 26:13** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I am the LORD your God, which brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, that ye should not be their bondmen; and I have broken the bands of your yoke, and made you go upright.

**Leviticus 26:44** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And yet for all that, when they be in the land of their enemies, I will not cast them away, neither will I abhor them, to destroy them utterly, and to break my covenant with them: for I am the LORD their God.

**Leviticus 26:45** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> But I will for their sakes remember the covenant of their ancestors, whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt in the sight of the heathen, that I might be their God: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 26:46** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> These are the statutes and judgments and laws, which the LORD made between him and the children of Israel in mount Sinai by the hand of Moses.

**Leviticus 27:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Leviticus 27:2** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When a man shall make a singular vow, the persons shall be for the LORD by thy estimation.

**Leviticus 27:9** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And if it be a beast, whereof men bring an offering unto the LORD, all that any man giveth of such unto the LORD shall be holy.

**Leviticus 27:11** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And if it be any unclean beast, of which they do not offer a sacrifice unto the LORD, then he shall present the beast before the priest:

**Leviticus 27:14** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And when a man shall sanctify his house to be holy unto the LORD, then the priest shall estimate it, whether it be good or bad: as the priest shall estimate it, so shall it stand.

**Leviticus 27:16** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And if a man shall sanctify unto the LORD some part of a field of his possession, then thy estimation shall be according to the seed thereof: an homer of barley seed shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.

**Leviticus 27:21** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> But the field, when it goeth out in the jubile, shall be holy unto the LORD, as a field devoted; the possession thereof shall be the priest's.

**Leviticus 27:22** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And if a man sanctify unto the LORD a field which he hath bought, which is not of the fields of his possession;

**Leviticus 27:23** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Then the priest shall reckon unto him the worth of thy estimation, even unto the year of the jubile: and he shall give thine estimation in that day, as a holy thing unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 27:26** — `יהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> Only the firstling of the beasts, which should be the LORD'S firstling, no man shall sanctify it; whether it be ox, or sheep: it is the LORD'S.

**Leviticus 27:30** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And all the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land, or of the fruit of the tree, is the LORD'S: it is holy unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 27:32** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And concerning the tithe of the herd, or of the flock, even of whatsoever passeth under the rod, the tenth shall be holy unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 27:34** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> These are the commandments, which the LORD commanded Moses for the children of Israel in mount Sinai.

### Numbers (328 verses)

**Numbers 1:1** — `יְהוָ֧ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the tabernacle of the congregation, on the first day of the second month, in the second year after they were come out of the land of Egypt, saying,

**Numbers 1:19** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> As the LORD commanded Moses, so he numbered them in the wilderness of Sinai.

**Numbers 1:48** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD had spoken unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 1:54** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And the children of Israel did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so did they.

**Numbers 2:1** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

**Numbers 2:34** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the children of Israel did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses: so they pitched by their standards, and so they set forward, every one after their families, according to the house of their fathers.

**Numbers 3:1** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> These also are the generations of Aaron and Moses in the day that the LORD spake with Moses in mount Sinai.

**Numbers 3:4** — `יְהוָ֡ה` (YHWH)
> And Nadab and Abihu died before the LORD, when they offered strange fire before the LORD, in the wilderness of Sinai, and they had no children: and Eleazar and Ithamar ministered in the priest's office in the sight of Aaron their father.

**Numbers 3:5** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 3:13** — `יְהוָֽה׃ ס` (YHWH)
> Because all the firstborn are mine; for on the day that I smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt I hallowed unto me all the firstborn in Israel, both man and beast: mine shall they be: I am the LORD.

**Numbers 3:14** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, saying,

**Numbers 3:16** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses numbered them according to the word of the LORD, as he was commanded.

**Numbers 3:39** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> All that were numbered of the Levites, which Moses and Aaron numbered at the commandment of the LORD, throughout their families, all the males from a month old and upward, were twenty and two thousand.

**Numbers 3:40** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Moses, Number all the firstborn of the males of the children of Israel from a month old and upward, and take the number of their names.

**Numbers 3:41** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And thou shalt take the Levites for me (I am the LORD) instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel; and the cattle of the Levites instead of all the firstlings among the cattle of the children of Israel.

**Numbers 3:42** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses numbered, as the LORD commanded him, all the firstborn among the children of Israel.

**Numbers 3:44** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 3:45** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel, and the cattle of the Levites instead of their cattle; and the Levites shall be mine: I am the LORD.

**Numbers 3:51** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses gave the money of them that were redeemed unto Aaron and to his sons, according to the word of the LORD, as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Numbers 4:1** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

**Numbers 4:21** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 4:37** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> These were they that were numbered of the families of the Kohathites, all that might do service in the tabernacle of the congregation, which Moses and Aaron did number according to the commandment of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

**Numbers 4:41** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> These are they that were numbered of the families of the sons of Gershon, of all that might do service in the tabernacle of the congregation, whom Moses and Aaron did number according to the commandment of the LORD.

**Numbers 4:45** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> These be those that were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the word of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

**Numbers 4:49** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> According to the commandment of the LORD they were numbered by the hand of Moses, every one according to his service, and according to his burden: thus were they numbered of him, as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Numbers 5:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 5:4** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the children of Israel did so, and put them out without the camp: as the LORD spake unto Moses, so did the children of Israel.

**Numbers 5:5** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 5:6** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, When a man or woman shall commit any sin that men commit, to do a trespass against the LORD, and that person be guilty;

**Numbers 5:8** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> But if the man have no kinsman to recompense the trespass unto, let the trespass be recompensed unto the LORD, even to the priest; beside the ram of the atonement, whereby an atonement shall be made for him.

**Numbers 5:11** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 5:18** — `יְהוָה֒` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall set the woman before the LORD, and uncover the woman's head, and put the offering of memorial in her hands, which is the jealousy offering: and the priest shall have in his hand the bitter water that causeth the curse:

**Numbers 5:21** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Then the priest shall charge the woman with an oath of cursing, and the priest shall say unto the woman, The LORD make thee a curse and an oath among thy people, when the LORD doth make thy thigh to rot, and thy belly to swell;

**Numbers 5:25** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Then the priest shall take the jealousy offering out of the woman's hand, and shall wave the offering before the LORD, and offer it upon the altar:

**Numbers 5:30** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Or when the spirit of jealousy cometh upon him, and he be jealous over his wife, and shall set the woman before the LORD, and the priest shall execute upon her all this law.

**Numbers 6:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 6:5** — `יהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> All the days of the vow of his separation there shall no razor come upon his head: until the days be fulfilled, in the which he separateth himself unto the LORD, he shall be holy, and shall let the locks of the hair of his head grow.

**Numbers 6:6** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> All the days that he separateth himself unto the LORD he shall come at no dead body.

**Numbers 6:8** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> All the days of his separation he is holy unto the LORD.

**Numbers 6:12** — `יהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And he shall consecrate unto the LORD the days of his separation, and shall bring a lamb of the first year for a trespass offering: but the days that were before shall be lost, because his separation was defiled.

**Numbers 6:14** — `יהוָ֡ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall offer his offering unto the LORD, one he lamb of the first year without blemish for a burnt offering, and one ewe lamb of the first year without blemish for a sin offering, and one ram without blemish for peace offerings,

**Numbers 6:16** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall bring them before the LORD, and shall offer his sin offering, and his burnt offering:

**Numbers 6:17** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall offer the ram for a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the LORD, with the basket of unleavened bread: the priest shall offer also his meat offering, and his drink offering.

**Numbers 6:20** — `יְהוָה֒` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the LORD: this is holy for the priest, with the wave breast and heave shoulder: and after that the Nazarite may drink wine.

**Numbers 6:21** — `יהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> This is the law of the Nazarite who hath vowed, and of his offering unto the LORD for his separation, beside that that his hand shall get: according to the vow which he vowed, so he must do after the law of his separation.

**Numbers 6:22** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 6:24** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD bless thee, and keep thee:

**Numbers 6:25** — `יְהוָ֧ה׀` (YHWH)
> The LORD make his face shine upon thee, and be gracious unto thee:

**Numbers 6:26** — `יְהוָ֤ה׀` (YHWH)
> The LORD lift up his countenance upon thee, and give thee peace.

**Numbers 7:3** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And they brought their offering before the LORD, six covered wagons, and twelve oxen; a wagon for two of the princes, and for each one an ox: and they brought them before the tabernacle.

**Numbers 7:4** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 8:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 8:3** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Aaron did so; he lighted the lamps thereof over against the candlestick, as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Numbers 8:4** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And this work of the candlestick was of beaten gold, unto the shaft thereof, unto the flowers thereof, was beaten work: according unto the pattern which the LORD had shewed Moses, so he made the candlestick.

**Numbers 8:5** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 8:11** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And Aaron shall offer the Levites before the LORD for an offering of the children of Israel, that they may execute the service of the LORD.

**Numbers 8:12** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the Levites shall lay their hands upon the heads of the bullocks: and thou shalt offer the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering, unto the LORD, to make an atonement for the Levites.

**Numbers 8:13** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And thou shalt set the Levites before Aaron, and before his sons, and offer them for an offering unto the LORD.

**Numbers 8:20** — `יְהוָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses, and Aaron, and all the congregation of the children of Israel, did to the Levites according unto all that the LORD commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so did the children of Israel unto them.

**Numbers 8:21** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And the Levites were purified, and they washed their clothes; and Aaron offered them as an offering before the LORD; and Aaron made an atonement for them to cleanse them.

**Numbers 8:22** — `יְהוָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> And after that went the Levites in to do their service in the tabernacle of the congregation before Aaron, and before his sons: as the LORD had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so did they unto them.

**Numbers 8:23** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 9:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after they were come out of the land of Egypt, saying,

**Numbers 9:5** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And they kept the passover on the fourteenth day of the first month at even in the wilderness of Sinai: according to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so did the children of Israel.

**Numbers 9:7** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And those men said unto him, We are defiled by the dead body of a man: wherefore are we kept back, that we may not offer an offering of the LORD in his appointed season among the children of Israel?

**Numbers 9:8** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto them, Stand still, and I will hear what the LORD will command concerning you.

**Numbers 9:9** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 9:10** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If any man of you or of your posterity shall be unclean by reason of a dead body, or be in a journey afar off, yet he shall keep the passover unto the LORD.

**Numbers 9:13** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> But the man that is clean, and is not in a journey, and forbeareth to keep the passover, even the same soul shall be cut off from among his people: because he brought not the offering of the LORD in his appointed season, that man shall bear his sin.

**Numbers 9:14** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And if a stranger shall sojourn among you, and will keep the passover unto the LORD; according to the ordinance of the passover, and according to the manner thereof, so shall he do: ye shall have one ordinance, both for the stranger, and for him that was born in the land.

**Numbers 9:18** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> At the commandment of the LORD the children of Israel journeyed, and at the commandment of the LORD they pitched: as long as the cloud abode upon the tabernacle they rested in their tents.

**Numbers 9:19** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And when the cloud tarried long upon the tabernacle many days, then the children of Israel kept the charge of the LORD, and journeyed not.

**Numbers 9:20** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And so it was, when the cloud was a few days upon the tabernacle; according to the commandment of the LORD they abode in their tents, and according to the commandment of the LORD they journeyed.

**Numbers 9:23** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> At the commandment of the LORD they rested in the tents, and at the commandment of the LORD they journeyed: they kept the charge of the LORD, at the commandment of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

**Numbers 10:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 10:9** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And if ye go to war in your land against the enemy that oppresseth you, then ye shall blow an alarm with the trumpets; and ye shall be remembered before the LORD your God, and ye shall be saved from your enemies.

**Numbers 10:10** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Also in the day of your gladness, and in your solemn days, and in the beginnings of your months, ye shall blow with the trumpets over your burnt offerings, and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings; that they may be to you for a memorial before your God: I am the LORD your God.

**Numbers 10:13** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And they first took their journey according to the commandment of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

**Numbers 10:29** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto Hobab, the son of Raguel the Midianite, Moses' father in law, We are journeying unto the place of which the LORD said, I will give it you: come thou with us, and we will do thee good: for the LORD hath spoken good concerning Israel.

**Numbers 10:32** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And it shall be, if thou go with us, yea, it shall be, that what goodness the LORD shall do unto us, the same will we do unto thee.

**Numbers 10:33** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And they departed from the mount of the LORD three days' journey: and the ark of the covenant of the LORD went before them in the three days' journey, to search out a resting place for them.

**Numbers 10:34** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And the cloud of the LORD was upon them by day, when they went out of the camp.

**Numbers 10:35** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And it came to pass, when the ark set forward, that Moses said, Rise up, LORD, and let thine enemies be scattered; and let them that hate thee flee before thee.

**Numbers 10:36** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And when it rested, he said, Return, O LORD, unto the many thousands of Israel.

**Numbers 11:1** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And when the people complained, it displeased the LORD: and the LORD heard it; and his anger was kindled; and the fire of the LORD burnt among them, and consumed them that were in the uttermost parts of the camp.

**Numbers 11:2** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the people cried unto Moses; and when Moses prayed unto the LORD, the fire was quenched.

**Numbers 11:3** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And he called the name of the place Taberah: because the fire of the LORD burnt among them.

**Numbers 11:11** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto the LORD, Wherefore hast thou afflicted thy servant? and wherefore have I not found favour in thy sight, that thou layest the burden of all this people upon me?

**Numbers 11:16** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Moses, Gather unto me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom thou knowest to be the elders of the people, and officers over them; and bring them unto the tabernacle of the congregation, that they may stand there with thee.

**Numbers 11:18** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And say thou unto the people, Sanctify yourselves against to morrow, and ye shall eat flesh: for ye have wept in the ears of the LORD, saying, Who shall give us flesh to eat? for it was well with us in Egypt: therefore the LORD will give you flesh, and ye shall eat.

**Numbers 11:20** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> But even a whole month, until it come out at your nostrils, and it be loathsome unto you: because that ye have despised the LORD which is among you, and have wept before him, saying, Why came we forth out of Egypt?

**Numbers 11:23** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Moses, Is the LORD'S hand waxed short? thou shalt see now whether my word shall come to pass unto thee or not.

**Numbers 11:24** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses went out, and told the people the words of the LORD, and gathered the seventy men of the elders of the people, and set them round about the tabernacle.

**Numbers 11:25** — `יְהוָ֥ה׀` (YHWH)
> And the LORD came down in a cloud, and spake unto him, and took of the spirit that was upon him, and gave it unto the seventy elders: and it came to pass, that, when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied, and did not cease.

**Numbers 11:29** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto him, Enviest thou for my sake? would God that all the LORD'S people were prophets, and that the LORD would put his spirit upon them!

**Numbers 11:31** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And there went forth a wind from the LORD, and brought quails from the sea, and let them fall by the camp, as it were a day's journey on this side, and as it were a day's journey on the other side, round about the camp, and as it were two cubits high upon the face of the earth.

**Numbers 11:33** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And while the flesh was yet between their teeth, ere it was chewed, the wrath of the LORD was kindled against the people, and the LORD smote the people with a very great plague.

**Numbers 12:2** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And they said, Hath the LORD indeed spoken only by Moses? hath he not spoken also by us? And the LORD heard it.

**Numbers 12:4** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake suddenly unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto Miriam, Come out ye three unto the tabernacle of the congregation. And they three came out.

**Numbers 12:5** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD came down in the pillar of the cloud, and stood in the door of the tabernacle, and called Aaron and Miriam: and they both came forth.

**Numbers 12:6** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And he said, Hear now my words: If there be a prophet among you, I the LORD will make myself known unto him in a vision, and will speak unto him in a dream.

**Numbers 12:8** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> With him will I speak mouth to mouth, even apparently, and not in dark speeches; and the similitude of the LORD shall he behold: wherefore then were ye not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?

**Numbers 12:13** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses cried unto the LORD, saying, Heal her now, O God, I beseech thee.

**Numbers 12:14** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Moses, If her father had but spit in her face, should she not be ashamed seven days? let her be shut out from the camp seven days, and after that let her be received in again.

**Numbers 13:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 13:3** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses by the commandment of the LORD sent them from the wilderness of Paran: all those men were heads of the children of Israel.

**Numbers 14:3** — `יְ֠הוָה` (YHWH)
> And wherefore hath the LORD brought us unto this land, to fall by the sword, that our wives and our children should be a prey? were it not better for us to return into Egypt?

**Numbers 14:8** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> If the LORD delight in us, then he will bring us into this land, and give it us; a land which floweth with milk and honey.

**Numbers 14:9** — `יהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> Only rebel not ye against the LORD, neither fear ye the people of the land; for they are bread for us: their defence is departed from them, and the LORD is with us: fear them not.

**Numbers 14:10** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> But all the congregation bade stone them with stones. And the glory of the LORD appeared in the tabernacle of the congregation before all the children of Israel.

**Numbers 14:11** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Moses, How long will this people provoke me? and how long will it be ere they believe me, for all the signs which I have shewed among them?

**Numbers 14:13** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto the LORD, Then the Egyptians shall hear it, (for thou broughtest up this people in thy might from among them;)

**Numbers 14:14** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And they will tell it to the inhabitants of this land: for they have heard that thou LORD art among this people, that thou LORD art seen face to face, and that thy cloud standeth over them, and that thou goest before them, by day time in a pillar of a cloud, and in a pillar of fire by night.

**Numbers 14:16** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Because the LORD was not able to bring this people into the land which he sware unto them, therefore he hath slain them in the wilderness.

**Numbers 14:18** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is longsuffering, and of great mercy, forgiving iniquity and transgression, and by no means clearing the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation.

**Numbers 14:20** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said, I have pardoned according to thy word:

**Numbers 14:21** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> But as truly as I live, all the earth shall be filled with the glory of the LORD.

**Numbers 14:26** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

**Numbers 14:28** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Say unto them, As truly as I live, saith the LORD, as ye have spoken in mine ears, so will I do to you:

**Numbers 14:35** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> I the LORD have said, I will surely do it unto all this evil congregation, that are gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall be consumed, and there they shall die.

**Numbers 14:40** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And they rose up early in the morning, and gat them up into the top of the mountain, saying, Lo, we be here, and will go up unto the place which the LORD hath promised: for we have sinned.

**Numbers 14:41** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said, Wherefore now do ye transgress the commandment of the LORD? but it shall not prosper.

**Numbers 14:42** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Go not up, for the LORD is not among you; that ye be not smitten before your enemies.

**Numbers 14:43** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> For the Amalekites and the Canaanites are there before you, and ye shall fall by the sword: because ye are turned away from the LORD, therefore the LORD will not be with you.

**Numbers 14:44** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> But they presumed to go up unto the hill top: nevertheless the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and Moses, departed not out of the camp.

**Numbers 15:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 15:3** — `יהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And will make an offering by fire unto the LORD, a burnt offering, or a sacrifice in performing a vow, or in a freewill offering, or in your solemn feasts, to make a sweet savour unto the LORD, of the herd, or of the flock:

**Numbers 15:4** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Then shall he that offereth his offering unto the LORD bring a meat offering of a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of oil.

**Numbers 15:7** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And for a drink offering thou shalt offer the third part of an hin of wine, for a sweet savour unto the LORD.

**Numbers 15:8** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And when thou preparest a bullock for a burnt offering, or for a sacrifice in performing a vow, or peace offerings unto the LORD:

**Numbers 15:10** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And thou shalt bring for a drink offering half an hin of wine, for an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.

**Numbers 15:13** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> All that are born of the country shall do these things after this manner, in offering an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.

**Numbers 15:14** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And if a stranger sojourn with you, or whosoever be among you in your generations, and will offer an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD; as ye do, so he shall do.

**Numbers 15:15** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> One ordinance shall be both for you of the congregation, and also for the stranger that sojourneth with you, an ordinance for ever in your generations: as ye are, so shall the stranger be before the LORD.

**Numbers 15:17** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 15:19** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Then it shall be, that, when ye eat of the bread of the land, ye shall offer up an heave offering unto the LORD.

**Numbers 15:21** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Of the first of your dough ye shall give unto the LORD an heave offering in your generations.

**Numbers 15:22** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And if ye have erred, and not observed all these commandments, which the LORD hath spoken unto Moses,

**Numbers 15:23** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> Even all that the LORD hath commanded you by the hand of Moses, from the day that the LORD commanded Moses, and henceforward among your generations;

**Numbers 15:24** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Then it shall be, if ought be committed by ignorance without the knowledge of the congregation, that all the congregation shall offer one young bullock for a burnt offering, for a sweet savour unto the LORD, with his meat offering, and his drink offering, according to the manner, and one kid of the goats for a sin offering.

**Numbers 15:25** — `יהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall make an atonement for all the congregation of the children of Israel, and it shall be forgiven them; for it is ignorance: and they shall bring their offering, a sacrifice made by fire unto the LORD, and their sin offering before the LORD, for their ignorance:

**Numbers 15:28** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And the priest shall make an atonement for the soul that sinneth ignorantly, when he sinneth by ignorance before the LORD, to make an atonement for him; and it shall be forgiven him.

**Numbers 15:30** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> But the soul that doeth ought presumptuously, whether he be born in the land, or a stranger, the same reproacheth the LORD; and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

**Numbers 15:31** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> Because he hath despised the word of the LORD, and hath broken his commandment, that soul shall utterly be cut off; his iniquity shall be upon him.

**Numbers 15:36** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And all the congregation brought him without the camp, and stoned him with stones, and he died; as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Numbers 15:37** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 15:39** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And it shall be unto you for a fringe, that ye may look upon it, and remember all the commandments of the LORD, and do them; and that ye seek not after your own heart and your own eyes, after which ye use to go a whoring:

**Numbers 15:41** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I am the LORD your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the LORD your God.

**Numbers 16:3** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And they gathered themselves together against Moses and against Aaron, and said unto them, Ye take too much upon you, seeing all the congregation are holy, every one of them, and the LORD is among them: wherefore then lift ye up yourselves above the congregation of the LORD?

**Numbers 16:5** — `יְהוָ֧ה` (YHWH)
> And he spake unto Korah and unto all his company, saying, Even to morrow the LORD will shew who are his, and who is holy; and will cause him to come near unto him: even him whom he hath chosen will he cause to come near unto him.

**Numbers 16:7** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And put fire therein, and put incense in them before the LORD to morrow: and it shall be that the man whom the LORD doth choose, he shall be holy: ye take too much upon you, ye sons of Levi.

**Numbers 16:9** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Seemeth it but a small thing unto you, that the God of Israel hath separated you from the congregation of Israel, to bring you near to himself to do the service of the tabernacle of the LORD, and to stand before the congregation to minister unto them?

**Numbers 16:11** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For which cause both thou and all thy company are gathered together against the LORD: and what is Aaron, that ye murmur against him?

**Numbers 16:15** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses was very wroth, and said unto the LORD, Respect not thou their offering: I have not taken one ass from them, neither have I hurt one of them.

**Numbers 16:16** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto Korah, Be thou and all thy company before the LORD, thou, and they, and Aaron, to morrow:

**Numbers 16:17** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And take every man his censer, and put incense in them, and bring ye before the LORD every man his censer, two hundred and fifty censers; thou also, and Aaron, each of you his censer.

**Numbers 16:19** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Korah gathered all the congregation against them unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and the glory of the LORD appeared unto all the congregation.

**Numbers 16:20** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

**Numbers 16:23** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 16:28** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said, Hereby ye shall know that the LORD hath sent me to do all these works; for I have not done them of mine own mind.

**Numbers 16:29** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> If these men die the common death of all men, or if they be visited after the visitation of all men; then the LORD hath not sent me.

**Numbers 16:30** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> But if the LORD make a new thing, and the earth open her mouth, and swallow them up, with all that appertain unto them, and they go down quick into the pit; then ye shall understand that these men have provoked the LORD.

**Numbers 16:35** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And there came out a fire from the LORD, and consumed the two hundred and fifty men that offered incense.

**Numbers 16:36** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 16:38** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> The censers of these sinners against their own souls, let them make them broad plates for a covering of the altar: for they offered them before the LORD, therefore they are hallowed: and they shall be a sign unto the children of Israel.

**Numbers 16:40** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> To be a memorial unto the children of Israel, that no stranger, which is not of the seed of Aaron, come near to offer incense before the LORD; that he be not as Korah, and as his company: as the LORD said to him by the hand of Moses.

**Numbers 16:41** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> But on the morrow all the congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron, saying, Ye have killed the people of the LORD.

**Numbers 16:42** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And it came to pass, when the congregation was gathered against Moses and against Aaron, that they looked toward the tabernacle of the congregation: and, behold, the cloud covered it, and the glory of the LORD appeared.

**Numbers 16:44** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 16:46** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses said unto Aaron, Take a censer, and put fire therein from off the altar, and put on incense, and go quickly unto the congregation, and make an atonement for them: for there is wrath gone out from the LORD; the plague is begun.

**Numbers 17:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 17:7** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses laid up the rods before the LORD in the tabernacle of witness.

**Numbers 17:10** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Moses, Bring Aaron's rod again before the testimony, to be kept for a token against the rebels; and thou shalt quite take away their murmurings from me, that they die not.

**Numbers 17:11** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses did so: as the LORD commanded him, so did he.

**Numbers 17:13** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Whosoever cometh any thing near unto the tabernacle of the LORD shall die: shall we be consumed with dying?

**Numbers 18:1** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Aaron, Thou and thy sons and thy father's house with thee shall bear the iniquity of the sanctuary: and thou and thy sons with thee shall bear the iniquity of your priesthood.

**Numbers 18:6** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And I, behold, I have taken your brethren the Levites from among the children of Israel: to you they are given as a gift for the LORD, to do the service of the tabernacle of the congregation.

**Numbers 18:8** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Aaron, Behold, I also have given thee the charge of mine heave offerings of all the hallowed things of the children of Israel; unto thee have I given them by reason of the anointing, and to thy sons, by an ordinance for ever.

**Numbers 18:12** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> All the best of the oil, and all the best of the wine, and of the wheat, the firstfruits of them which they shall offer unto the LORD, them have I given thee.

**Numbers 18:13** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And whatsoever is first ripe in the land, which they shall bring unto the LORD, shall be thine; every one that is clean in thine house shall eat of it.

**Numbers 18:15** — `יהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> Every thing that openeth the matrix in all flesh, which they bring unto the LORD, whether it be of men or beasts, shall be thine: nevertheless the firstborn of man shalt thou surely redeem, and the firstling of unclean beasts shalt thou redeem.

**Numbers 18:17** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> But the firstling of a cow, or the firstling of a sheep, or the firstling of a goat, thou shalt not redeem; they are holy: thou shalt sprinkle their blood upon the altar, and shalt burn their fat for an offering made by fire, for a sweet savour unto the LORD.

**Numbers 18:19** — `יהוָה֒` (YHWH)
> All the heave offerings of the holy things, which the children of Israel offer unto the LORD, have I given thee, and thy sons and thy daughters with thee, by a statute for ever: it is a covenant of salt for ever before the LORD unto thee and to thy seed with thee.

**Numbers 18:20** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Aaron, Thou shalt have no inheritance in their land, neither shalt thou have any part among them: I am thy part and thine inheritance among the children of Israel.

**Numbers 18:24** — `יהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> But the tithes of the children of Israel, which they offer as an heave offering unto the LORD, I have given to the Levites to inherit: therefore I have said unto them, Among the children of Israel they shall have no inheritance.

**Numbers 18:25** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 18:26** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Thus speak unto the Levites, and say unto them, When ye take of the children of Israel the tithes which I have given you from them for your inheritance, then ye shall offer up an heave offering of it for the LORD, even a tenth part of the tithe.

**Numbers 18:28** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Thus ye also shall offer an heave offering unto the LORD of all your tithes, which ye receive of the children of Israel; and ye shall give thereof the LORD'S heave offering to Aaron the priest.

**Numbers 18:29** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Out of all your gifts ye shall offer every heave offering of the LORD, of all the best thereof, even the hallowed part thereof out of it.

**Numbers 19:1** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

**Numbers 19:2** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> This is the ordinance of the law which the LORD hath commanded, saying, Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring thee a red heifer without spot, wherein is no blemish, and upon which never came yoke:

**Numbers 19:20** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> But the man that shall be unclean, and shall not purify himself, that soul shall be cut off from among the congregation, because he hath defiled the sanctuary of the LORD: the water of separation hath not been sprinkled upon him; he is unclean.

**Numbers 20:3** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And the people chode with Moses, and spake, saying, Would God that we had died when our brethren died before the LORD!

**Numbers 20:4** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And why have ye brought up the congregation of the LORD into this wilderness, that we and our cattle should die there?

**Numbers 20:6** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Moses and Aaron went from the presence of the assembly unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and they fell upon their faces: and the glory of the LORD appeared unto them.

**Numbers 20:7** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

**Numbers 20:9** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
