> Thus saith Cyrus king of Persia, All the kingdoms of the earth hath the LORD God of heaven given me; and he hath charged me to build him an house in Jerusalem, which is in Judah. Who is there among you of all his people? The LORD his God be with him, and let him go up.

### Ezra (23 verses)

**Ezra 1:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Now in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that the word of the LORD by the mouth of Jeremiah might be fulfilled, the LORD stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, that he made a proclamation throughout all his kingdom, and put it also in writing, saying,

**Ezra 1:2** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Thus saith Cyrus king of Persia, The LORD God of heaven hath given me all the kingdoms of the earth; and he hath charged me to build him an house at Jerusalem, which is in Judah.

**Ezra 1:3** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> Who is there among you of all his people? his God be with him, and let him go up to Jerusalem, which is in Judah, and build the house of the LORD God of Israel, (he is the God,) which is in Jerusalem.

**Ezra 1:5** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Then rose up the chief of the fathers of Judah and Benjamin, and the priests, and the Levites, with all them whose spirit God had raised, to go up to build the house of the LORD which is in Jerusalem.

**Ezra 2:68** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And some of the chief of the fathers, when they came to the house of the LORD which is at Jerusalem, offered freely for the house of God to set it up in his place:

**Ezra 3:3** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And they set the altar upon his bases; for fear was upon them because of the people of those countries: and they offered burnt offerings thereon unto the LORD, even burnt offerings morning and evening.

**Ezra 3:5** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And afterward offered the continual burnt offering, both of the new moons, and of all the set feasts of the LORD that were consecrated, and of every one that willingly offered a freewill offering unto the LORD.

**Ezra 3:8** — `יְהוָֽה׃ פ` (YHWH)
> Now in the second year of their coming unto the house of God at Jerusalem, in the second month, began Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua the son of Jozadak, and the remnant of their brethren the priests and the Levites, and all they that were come out of the captivity unto Jerusalem; and appointed the Levites, from twenty years old and upward, to set forward the work of the house of the LORD.

**Ezra 3:10** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And when the builders laid the foundation of the temple of the LORD, they set the priests in their apparel with trumpets, and the Levites the sons of Asaph with cymbals, to praise the LORD, after the ordinance of David king of Israel.

**Ezra 3:11** — `יהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And they sang together by course in praising and giving thanks unto the LORD; because he is good, for his mercy endureth for ever toward Israel. And all the people shouted with a great shout, when they praised the LORD, because the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid.

**Ezra 4:1** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Now when the adversaries of Judah and Benjamin heard that the children of the captivity builded the temple unto the LORD God of Israel;

**Ezra 4:3** — `יהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> But Zerubbabel, and Jeshua, and the rest of the chief of the fathers of Israel, said unto them, Ye have nothing to do with us to build an house unto our God; but we ourselves together will build unto the LORD God of Israel, as king Cyrus the king of Persia hath commanded us.

**Ezra 6:22** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And kept the feast of unleavened bread seven days with joy: for the LORD had made them joyful, and turned the heart of the king of Assyria unto them, to strengthen their hands in the work of the house of God, the God of Israel.

**Ezra 7:6** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> This Ezra went up from Babylon; and he was a ready scribe in the law of Moses, which the LORD God of Israel had given: and the king granted him all his request, according to the hand of the LORD his God upon him.

**Ezra 7:11** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> Now this is the copy of the letter that the king Artaxerxes gave unto Ezra the priest, the scribe, even a scribe of the words of the commandments of the LORD, and of his statutes to Israel.

**Ezra 7:27** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed be the LORD God of our fathers, which hath put such a thing as this in the king's heart, to beautify the house of the LORD which is in Jerusalem:

**Ezra 7:28** — `יְהוָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> And hath extended mercy unto me before the king, and his counsellors, and before all the king's mighty princes. And I was strengthened as the hand of the LORD my God was upon me, and I gathered together out of Israel chief men to go up with me.

**Ezra 8:28** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And I said unto them, Ye are holy unto the LORD; the vessels are holy also; and the silver and the gold are a freewill offering unto the LORD God of your fathers.

**Ezra 8:29** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Watch ye, and keep them, until ye weigh them before the chief of the priests and the Levites, and chief of the fathers of Israel, at Jerusalem, in the chambers of the house of the LORD.

**Ezra 8:35** — `יהוָֽה׃ פ` (YHWH)
> Also the children of those that had been carried away, which were come out of the captivity, offered burnt offerings unto the God of Israel, twelve bullocks for all Israel, ninety and six rams, seventy and seven lambs, twelve he goats for a sin offering: all this was a burnt offering unto the LORD.

**Ezra 9:5** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And at the evening sacrifice I arose up from my heaviness; and having rent my garment and my mantle, I fell upon my knees, and spread out my hands unto the LORD my God,

**Ezra 9:15** — `יְהוָ֞ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD God of Israel, thou art righteous: for we remain yet escaped, as it is this day: behold, we are before thee in our trespasses: for we cannot stand before thee because of this.

**Ezra 10:11** — `יהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Now therefore make confession unto the LORD God of your fathers, and do his pleasure: and separate yourselves from the people of the land, and from the strange wives.

### Nehemiah (13 verses)

**Nehemiah 8:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And all the people gathered themselves together as one man into the street that was before the water gate; and they spake unto Ezra the scribe to bring the book of the law of Moses, which the LORD had commanded to Israel.

**Nehemiah 8:6** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And Ezra blessed the LORD, the great God. And all the people answered, Amen, Amen, with lifting up their hands: and they bowed their heads, and worshipped the LORD with their faces to the ground.

**Nehemiah 8:9** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And Nehemiah, which is the Tirshatha, and Ezra the priest the scribe, and the Levites that taught the people, said unto all the people, This day is holy unto the LORD your God; mourn not, nor weep. For all the people wept, when they heard the words of the law.

**Nehemiah 8:10** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Then he said unto them, Go your way, eat the fat, and drink the sweet, and send portions unto them for whom nothing is prepared: for this day is holy unto our Lord: neither be ye sorry; for the joy of the LORD is your strength.

**Nehemiah 8:14** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And they found written in the law which the LORD had commanded by Moses, that the children of Israel should dwell in booths in the feast of the seventh month:

**Nehemiah 9:3** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And they stood up in their place, and read in the book of the law of the LORD their God one fourth part of the day; and another fourth part they confessed, and worshipped the LORD their God.

**Nehemiah 9:4** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Then stood up upon the stairs, of the Levites, Jeshua, and Bani, Kadmiel, Shebaniah, Bunni, Sherebiah, Bani, and Chenani, and cried with a loud voice unto the LORD their God.

**Nehemiah 9:5** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Then the Levites, Jeshua, and Kadmiel, Bani, Hashabniah, Sherebiah, Hodijah, Shebaniah, and Pethahiah, said, Stand up and bless the LORD your God for ever and ever: and blessed be thy glorious name, which is exalted above all blessing and praise.

**Nehemiah 9:6** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> Thou, even thou, art LORD alone; thou hast made heaven, the heaven of heavens, with all their host, the earth, and all things that are therein, the seas, and all that is therein, and thou preservest them all; and the host of heaven worshippeth thee.

**Nehemiah 9:7** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Thou art the LORD the God, who didst choose Abram, and broughtest him forth out of Ur of the Chaldees, and gavest him the name of Abraham;

**Nehemiah 10:29** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> They clave to their brethren, their nobles, and entered into a curse, and into an oath, to walk in God's law, which was given by Moses the servant of God, and to observe and do all the commandments of the LORD our Lord, and his judgments and his statutes;

**Nehemiah 10:34** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And we cast the lots among the priests, the Levites, and the people, for the wood offering, to bring it into the house of our God, after the houses of our fathers, at times appointed year by year, to burn upon the altar of the LORD our God, as it is written in the law:

**Nehemiah 10:35** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And to bring the firstfruits of our ground, and the firstfruits of all fruit of all trees, year by year, unto the house of the LORD:

### Job (20 verses)

**Job 1:6** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan came also among them.

**Job 1:8** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil?

**Job 1:9** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Then Satan answered the LORD, and said, Doth Job fear God for nought?

**Job 1:12** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Satan, Behold, all that he hath is in thy power; only upon himself put not forth thine hand. So Satan went forth from the presence of the LORD.

**Job 1:21** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And said, Naked came I out of my mother's womb, and naked shall I return thither: the LORD gave, and the LORD hath taken away; blessed be the name of the LORD.

**Job 2:1** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Again there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan came also among them to present himself before the LORD.

**Job 2:2** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Satan, From whence comest thou? And Satan answered the LORD, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.

**Job 2:3** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil? and still he holdeth fast his integrity, although thou movedst me against him, to destroy him without cause.

**Job 2:4** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And Satan answered the LORD, and said, Skin for skin, yea, all that a man hath will he give for his life.

**Job 2:7** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> So went Satan forth from the presence of the LORD, and smote Job with sore boils from the sole of his foot unto his crown.

**Job 38:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind, and said,

**Job 40:1** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Moreover the LORD answered Job, and said,

**Job 40:3** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Then Job answered the LORD, and said,

**Job 40:6** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Then answered the LORD unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,

**Job 42:1** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Then Job answered the LORD, and said,

**Job 42:7** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And it was so, that after the LORD had spoken these words unto Job, the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite, My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends: for ye have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant Job hath.

**Job 42:9** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the LORD commanded them: the LORD also accepted Job.

**Job 42:10** — `יהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends: also the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.

**Job 42:11** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Then came there unto him all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all they that had been of his acquaintance before, and did eat bread with him in his house: and they bemoaned him, and comforted him over all the evil that the LORD had brought upon him: every man also gave him a piece of money, and every one an earring of gold.

**Job 42:12** — `יהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> So the LORD blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning: for he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she asses.

### Psalms (583 verses)

**Psalms 1:6** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish.

**Psalms 2:2** — `יְ֝הוָה` (YHWH)
> The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD, and against his anointed, saying,

**Psalms 2:11** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.

**Psalms 3:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David, when he fled from Absalom his son. LORD, how are they increased that trouble me! many are they that rise up against me.

**Psalms 3:3** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> But thou, O LORD, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.

**Psalms 3:5** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the LORD sustained me.

**Psalms 3:7** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Arise, O LORD; save me, O my God: for thou hast smitten all mine enemies upon the cheek bone; thou hast broken the teeth of the ungodly.

**Psalms 3:8** — `יהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Salvation belongeth unto the LORD: thy blessing is upon thy people. Selah.

**Psalms 4:3** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> But know that the LORD hath set apart him that is godly for himself: the LORD will hear when I call unto him.

**Psalms 4:5** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the LORD.

**Psalms 4:6** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> There be many that say, Who will shew us any good? LORD, lift thou up the light of thy countenance upon us.

**Psalms 4:8** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, LORD, only makest me dwell in safety.

**Psalms 5:1** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician upon Nehiloth, A Psalm of David. Give ear to my words, O LORD, consider my meditation.

**Psalms 5:3** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O LORD; in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up.

**Psalms 5:6** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Thou shalt destroy them that speak leasing: the LORD will abhor the bloody and deceitful man.

**Psalms 5:8** — `יְהוָ֤ה׀` (YHWH)
> Lead me, O LORD, in thy righteousness because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.

**Psalms 5:12** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For thou, LORD, wilt bless the righteous; with favour wilt thou compass him as with a shield.

**Psalms 6:1** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician on Neginoth upon Sheminith, A Psalm of David. O LORD, rebuke me not in thine anger, neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.

**Psalms 6:3** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> My soul is also sore vexed: but thou, O LORD, how long?

**Psalms 6:4** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Return, O LORD, deliver my soul: oh save me for thy mercies' sake.

**Psalms 6:8** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity; for the LORD hath heard the voice of my weeping.

**Psalms 6:9** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD hath heard my supplication; the LORD will receive my prayer.

**Psalms 7:1** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Shiggaion of David, which he sang unto the LORD, concerning the words of Cush the Benjamite. O LORD my God, in thee do I put my trust: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me:

**Psalms 7:3** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD my God, if I have done this; if there be iniquity in my hands;

**Psalms 7:6** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Arise, O LORD, in thine anger, lift up thyself because of the rage of mine enemies: and awake for me to the judgment that thou hast commanded.

**Psalms 7:8** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> The LORD shall judge the people: judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity that is in me.

**Psalms 7:17** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I will praise the LORD according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the LORD most high.

**Psalms 8:1** — `יְהוָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician upon Gittith, A Psalm of David. O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.

**Psalms 8:9** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!

**Psalms 9:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician upon Muthlabben, A Psalm of David. I will praise thee, O LORD, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.

**Psalms 9:7** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> But the LORD shall endure for ever: he hath prepared his throne for judgment.

**Psalms 9:9** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD also will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.

**Psalms 9:10** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And they that know thy name will put their trust in thee: for thou, LORD, hast not forsaken them that seek thee.

**Psalms 9:11** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> Sing praises to the LORD, which dwelleth in Zion: declare among the people his doings.

**Psalms 9:13** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Have mercy upon me, O LORD; consider my trouble which I suffer of them that hate me, thou that liftest me up from the gates of death:

**Psalms 9:16** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> The LORD is known by the judgment which he executeth: the wicked is snared in the work of his own hands. Higgaion. Selah.

**Psalms 10:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Why standest thou afar off, O LORD? why hidest thou thyself in times of trouble?

**Psalms 10:3** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> For the wicked boasteth of his heart's desire, and blesseth the covetous, whom the LORD abhorreth.

**Psalms 10:12** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Arise, O LORD; O God, lift up thine hand: forget not the humble.

**Psalms 10:16** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is King for ever and ever: the heathen are perished out of his land.

**Psalms 10:17** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> LORD, thou hast heard the desire of the humble: thou wilt prepare their heart, thou wilt cause thine ear to hear:

**Psalms 11:1** — `יהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. In the LORD put I my trust: how say ye to my soul, Flee as a bird to your mountain?

**Psalms 11:4** — `יְהוָ֤ה׀` (YHWH)
> The LORD is in his holy temple, the LORD'S throne is in heaven: his eyes behold, his eyelids try, the children of men.

**Psalms 11:5** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> The LORD trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loveth violence his soul hateth.

**Psalms 11:7** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> For the righteous LORD loveth righteousness; his countenance doth behold the upright.

**Psalms 12:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician upon Sheminith, A Psalm of David. Help, LORD; for the godly man ceaseth; for the faithful fail from among the children of men.

**Psalms 12:3** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD shall cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that speaketh proud things:

**Psalms 12:5** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For the oppression of the poor, for the sighing of the needy, now will I arise, saith the LORD; I will set him in safety from him that puffeth at him.

**Psalms 12:6** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> The words of the LORD are pure words: as silver tried in a furnace of earth, purified seven times.

**Psalms 12:7** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Thou shalt keep them, O LORD, thou shalt preserve them from this generation for ever.

**Psalms 13:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. How long wilt thou forget me, O LORD? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?

**Psalms 13:3** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Consider and hear me, O LORD my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;

**Psalms 13:6** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> I will sing unto the LORD, because he hath dealt bountifully with me.

**Psalms 14:2** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, and seek God.

**Psalms 14:4** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people as they eat bread, and call not upon the LORD.

**Psalms 14:6** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Ye have shamed the counsel of the poor, because the LORD is his refuge.

**Psalms 14:7** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! when the LORD bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

**Psalms 15:1** — `יְ֭הֹוָה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. LORD, who shall abide in thy tabernacle? who shall dwell in thy holy hill?

**Psalms 15:4** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> In whose eyes a vile person is contemned; but he honoureth them that fear the LORD. He that sweareth to his own hurt, and changeth not.

**Psalms 16:2** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> O my soul, thou hast said unto the LORD, Thou art my Lord: my goodness extendeth not to thee;

**Psalms 16:5** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is the portion of mine inheritance and of my cup: thou maintainest my lot.

**Psalms 16:7** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> I will bless the LORD, who hath given me counsel: my reins also instruct me in the night seasons.

**Psalms 16:8** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I have set the LORD always before me: because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.

**Psalms 17:1** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> A Prayer of David. Hear the right, O LORD, attend unto my cry, give ear unto my prayer, that goeth not out of feigned lips.

**Psalms 17:13** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Arise, O LORD, disappoint him, cast him down: deliver my soul from the wicked, which is thy sword:

**Psalms 17:14** — `יְהוָ֡ה` (YHWH)
> From men which are thy hand, O LORD, from men of the world, which have their portion in this life, and whose belly thou fillest with thy hid treasure: they are full of children, and leave the rest of their substance to their babes.

**Psalms 18:1** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David, the servant of the LORD, who spake unto the LORD the words of this song in the day that the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies, and from the hand of Saul: And he said, I will love thee, O LORD, my strength.

**Psalms 18:2** — `יְהוָ֤ה׀` (YHWH)
> The LORD is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust; my buckler, and the horn of my salvation, and my high tower.

**Psalms 18:3** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> I will call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies.

**Psalms 18:6** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> In my distress I called upon the LORD, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.

**Psalms 18:13** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD also thundered in the heavens, and the Highest gave his voice; hail stones and coals of fire.

**Psalms 18:15** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Then the channels of waters were seen, and the foundations of the world were discovered at thy rebuke, O LORD, at the blast of the breath of thy nostrils.

**Psalms 18:18** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the LORD was my stay.

**Psalms 18:20** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.

**Psalms 18:21** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God.

**Psalms 18:24** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Therefore hath the LORD recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyesight.

**Psalms 18:28** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> For thou wilt light my candle: the LORD my God will enlighten my darkness.

**Psalms 18:30** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> As for God, his way is perfect: the word of the LORD is tried: he is a buckler to all those that trust in him.

**Psalms 18:31** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For who is God save the LORD? or who is a rock save our God?

**Psalms 18:41** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> They cried, but there was none to save them: even unto the LORD, but he answered them not.

**Psalms 18:46** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD liveth; and blessed be my rock; and let the God of my salvation be exalted.

**Psalms 18:49** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Therefore will I give thanks unto thee, O LORD, among the heathen, and sing praises unto thy name.

**Psalms 19:7** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The law of the LORD is perfect, converting the soul: the testimony of the LORD is sure, making wise the simple.

**Psalms 19:8** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The statutes of the LORD are right, rejoicing the heart: the commandment of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes.

**Psalms 19:9** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> The fear of the LORD is clean, enduring for ever: the judgments of the LORD are true and righteous altogether.

**Psalms 19:14** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Let the words of my mouth, and the meditation of my heart, be acceptable in thy sight, O LORD, my strength, and my redeemer.

**Psalms 20:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. The LORD hear thee in the day of trouble; the name of the God of Jacob defend thee;

**Psalms 20:5** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> We will rejoice in thy salvation, and in the name of our God we will set up our banners: the LORD fulfil all thy petitions.

**Psalms 20:6** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Now know I that the LORD saveth his anointed; he will hear him from his holy heaven with the saving strength of his right hand.

**Psalms 20:7** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Some trust in chariots, and some in horses: but we will remember the name of the LORD our God.

**Psalms 20:9** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Save, LORD: let the king hear us when we call.

**Psalms 21:1** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. The king shall joy in thy strength, O LORD; and in thy salvation how greatly shall he rejoice!

**Psalms 21:7** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For the king trusteth in the LORD, and through the mercy of the most High he shall not be moved.

**Psalms 21:9** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Thou shalt make them as a fiery oven in the time of thine anger: the LORD shall swallow them up in his wrath, and the fire shall devour them.

**Psalms 21:13** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Be thou exalted, LORD, in thine own strength: so will we sing and praise thy power.

**Psalms 22:8** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> He trusted on the LORD that he would deliver him: let him deliver him, seeing he delighted in him.

**Psalms 22:19** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> But be not thou far from me, O LORD: O my strength, haste thee to help me.

**Psalms 22:23** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Ye that fear the LORD, praise him; all ye the seed of Jacob, glorify him; and fear him, all ye the seed of Israel.

**Psalms 22:26** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The meek shall eat and be satisfied: they shall praise the LORD that seek him: your heart shall live for ever.

**Psalms 22:27** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> All the ends of the world shall remember and turn unto the LORD: and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before thee.

**Psalms 22:28** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> For the kingdom is the LORD'S: and he is the governor among the nations.

**Psalms 23:1** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.

**Psalms 23:6** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life: and I will dwell in the house of the LORD for ever.

**Psalms 24:1** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. The earth is the LORD'S, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.

**Psalms 24:3** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Who shall ascend into the hill of the LORD? or who shall stand in his holy place?

**Psalms 24:5** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> He shall receive the blessing from the LORD, and righteousness from the God of his salvation.

**Psalms 24:8** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Who is this King of glory? The LORD strong and mighty, the LORD mighty in battle.

**Psalms 24:10** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Who is this King of glory? The LORD of hosts, he is the King of glory. Selah.

**Psalms 25:1** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. Unto thee, O LORD, do I lift up my soul.

**Psalms 25:4** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Shew me thy ways, O LORD; teach me thy paths.

**Psalms 25:6** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Remember, O LORD, thy tender mercies and thy lovingkindnesses; for they have been ever of old.

**Psalms 25:7** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Remember not the sins of my youth, nor my transgressions: according to thy mercy remember thou me for thy goodness' sake, O LORD.

**Psalms 25:8** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Good and upright is the LORD: therefore will he teach sinners in the way.

**Psalms 25:10** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> All the paths of the LORD are mercy and truth unto such as keep his covenant and his testimonies.

**Psalms 25:11** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For thy name's sake, O LORD, pardon mine iniquity; for it is great.

**Psalms 25:12** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> What man is he that feareth the LORD? him shall he teach in the way that he shall choose.

**Psalms 25:14** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The secret of the LORD is with them that fear him; and he will shew them his covenant.

**Psalms 25:15** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Mine eyes are ever toward the LORD; for he shall pluck my feet out of the net.

**Psalms 26:1** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. Judge me, O LORD; for I have walked in mine integrity: I have trusted also in the LORD; therefore I shall not slide.

**Psalms 26:2** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Examine me, O LORD, and prove me; try my reins and my heart.

**Psalms 26:6** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> I will wash mine hands in innocency: so will I compass thine altar, O LORD:

**Psalms 26:8** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> LORD, I have loved the habitation of thy house, and the place where thine honour dwelleth.

**Psalms 26:12** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> My foot standeth in an even place: in the congregations will I bless the LORD.

**Psalms 27:1** — `יְהוָ֤ה׀` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the LORD is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?

**Psalms 27:4** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> One thing have I desired of the LORD, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the LORD, and to enquire in his temple.

**Psalms 27:6** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And now shall mine head be lifted up above mine enemies round about me: therefore will I offer in his tabernacle sacrifices of joy; I will sing, yea, I will sing praises unto the LORD.

**Psalms 27:7** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Hear, O LORD, when I cry with my voice: have mercy also upon me, and answer me.

**Psalms 27:8** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> When thou saidst, Seek ye my face; my heart said unto thee, Thy face, LORD, will I seek.

**Psalms 27:10** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> When my father and my mother forsake me, then the LORD will take me up.

**Psalms 27:11** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Teach me thy way, O LORD, and lead me in a plain path, because of mine enemies.

**Psalms 27:13** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> I had fainted, unless I had believed to see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living.

**Psalms 27:14** — `יְה֫וָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Wait on the LORD: be of good courage, and he shall strengthen thine heart: wait, I say, on the LORD.

**Psalms 28:1** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. Unto thee will I cry, O LORD my rock; be not silent to me: lest, if thou be silent to me, I become like them that go down into the pit.

**Psalms 28:5** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Because they regard not the works of the LORD, nor the operation of his hands, he shall destroy them, and not build them up.

**Psalms 28:6** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed be the LORD, because he hath heard the voice of my supplications.

**Psalms 28:7** — `יְהוָ֤ה׀` (YHWH)
> The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart greatly rejoiceth; and with my song will I praise him.

**Psalms 28:8** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is their strength, and he is the saving strength of his anointed.

**Psalms 29:1** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. Give unto the LORD, O ye mighty, give unto the LORD glory and strength.

**Psalms 29:2** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> Give unto the LORD the glory due unto his name; worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness.

**Psalms 29:3** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The voice of the LORD is upon the waters: the God of glory thundereth: the LORD is upon many waters.

**Psalms 29:4** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> The voice of the LORD is powerful; the voice of the LORD is full of majesty.

**Psalms 29:5** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The voice of the LORD breaketh the cedars; yea, the LORD breaketh the cedars of Lebanon.

**Psalms 29:7** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> The voice of the LORD divideth the flames of fire.

**Psalms 29:8** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The voice of the LORD shaketh the wilderness; the LORD shaketh the wilderness of Kadesh.

**Psalms 29:9** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> The voice of the LORD maketh the hinds to calve, and discovereth the forests: and in his temple doth every one speak of his glory.

**Psalms 29:10** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD sitteth upon the flood; yea, the LORD sitteth King for ever.

**Psalms 29:11** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD will give strength unto his people; the LORD will bless his people with peace.

**Psalms 30:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm and Song at the dedication of the house of David. I will extol thee, O LORD; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.

**Psalms 30:2** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD my God, I cried unto thee, and thou hast healed me.

**Psalms 30:3** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD, thou hast brought up my soul from the grave: thou hast kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.

**Psalms 30:4** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Sing unto the LORD, O ye saints of his, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.

**Psalms 30:7** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> LORD, by thy favour thou hast made my mountain to stand strong: thou didst hide thy face, and I was troubled.

**Psalms 30:8** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I cried to thee, O LORD; and unto the LORD I made supplication.

**Psalms 30:10** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Hear, O LORD, and have mercy upon me: LORD, be thou my helper.

**Psalms 30:12** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> To the end that my glory may sing praise to thee, and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever.

**Psalms 31:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. In thee, O LORD, do I put my trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.

**Psalms 31:5** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Into thine hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, O LORD God of truth.

**Psalms 31:6** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> I have hated them that regard lying vanities: but I trust in the LORD.

**Psalms 31:9** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> Have mercy upon me, O LORD, for I am in trouble: mine eye is consumed with grief, yea, my soul and my belly.

**Psalms 31:14** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> But I trusted in thee, O LORD: I said, Thou art my God.

**Psalms 31:17** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Let me not be ashamed, O LORD; for I have called upon thee: let the wicked be ashamed, and let them be silent in the grave.

**Psalms 31:23** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> O love the LORD, all ye his saints: for the LORD preserveth the faithful, and plentifully rewardeth the proud doer.

**Psalms 31:24** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Be of good courage, and he shall strengthen your heart, all ye that hope in the LORD.

**Psalms 32:2** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed is the man unto whom the LORD imputeth not iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no guile.

**Psalms 32:5** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> I acknowledged my sin unto thee, and mine iniquity have I not hid. I said, I will confess my transgressions unto the LORD; and thou forgavest the iniquity of my sin. Selah.

**Psalms 32:10** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Many sorrows shall be to the wicked: but he that trusteth in the LORD, mercy shall compass him about.

**Psalms 32:11** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Be glad in the LORD, and rejoice, ye righteous: and shout for joy, all ye that are upright in heart.

**Psalms 33:1** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Rejoice in the LORD, O ye righteous: for praise is comely for the upright.

**Psalms 33:2** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Praise the LORD with harp: sing unto him with the psaltery and an instrument of ten strings.

**Psalms 33:4** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For the word of the LORD is right; and all his works are done in truth.

**Psalms 33:5** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the goodness of the LORD.

**Psalms 33:6** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> By the word of the LORD were the heavens made; and all the host of them by the breath of his mouth.

**Psalms 33:8** — `יְהוָה` (YHWH)
> Let all the earth fear the LORD: let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.

**Psalms 33:10** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD bringeth the counsel of the heathen to nought: he maketh the devices of the people of none effect.

**Psalms 33:11** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The counsel of the LORD standeth for ever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.

**Psalms 33:12** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD; and the people whom he hath chosen for his own inheritance.

**Psalms 33:13** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD looketh from heaven; he beholdeth all the sons of men.

**Psalms 33:18** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Behold, the eye of the LORD is upon them that fear him, upon them that hope in his mercy;

**Psalms 33:20** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Our soul waiteth for the LORD: he is our help and our shield.

**Psalms 33:22** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Let thy mercy, O LORD, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.

**Psalms 34:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David, when he changed his behaviour before Abimelech; who drove him away, and he departed. I will bless the LORD at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth.

**Psalms 34:2** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> My soul shall make her boast in the LORD: the humble shall hear thereof, and be glad.

**Psalms 34:3** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> O magnify the LORD with me, and let us exalt his name together.

**Psalms 34:4** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I sought the LORD, and he heard me, and delivered me from all my fears.

**Psalms 34:6** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> This poor man cried, and the LORD heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles.

**Psalms 34:7** — `יְהוָ֓ה` (YHWH)
> The angel of the LORD encampeth round about them that fear him, and delivereth them.

**Psalms 34:8** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> O taste and see that the LORD is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.

**Psalms 34:9** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> O fear the LORD, ye his saints: for there is no want to them that fear him.

**Psalms 34:10** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The young lions do lack, and suffer hunger: but they that seek the LORD shall not want any good thing.

**Psalms 34:11** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Come, ye children, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the LORD.

**Psalms 34:15** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The eyes of the LORD are upon the righteous, and his ears are open unto their cry.

**Psalms 34:16** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The face of the LORD is against them that do evil, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.

**Psalms 34:17** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The righteous cry, and the LORD heareth, and delivereth them out of all their troubles.

**Psalms 34:18** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.

**Psalms 34:19** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Many are the afflictions of the righteous: but the LORD delivereth him out of them all.

**Psalms 34:22** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD redeemeth the soul of his servants: and none of them that trust in him shall be desolate.

**Psalms 35:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. Plead my cause, O LORD, with them that strive with me: fight against them that fight against me.

**Psalms 35:5** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Let them be as chaff before the wind: and let the angel of the LORD chase them.

**Psalms 35:6** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Let their way be dark and slippery: and let the angel of the LORD persecute them.

**Psalms 35:9** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And my soul shall be joyful in the LORD: it shall rejoice in his salvation.

**Psalms 35:10** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> All my bones shall say, LORD, who is like unto thee, which deliverest the poor from him that is too strong for him, yea, the poor and the needy from him that spoileth him?

**Psalms 35:22** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> This thou hast seen, O LORD: keep not silence: O Lord, be not far from me.

**Psalms 35:24** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Judge me, O LORD my God, according to thy righteousness; and let them not rejoice over me.

**Psalms 35:27** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Let them shout for joy, and be glad, that favour my righteous cause: yea, let them say continually, Let the LORD be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of his servant.

**Psalms 36:1** — `יְהוָ֬ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David the servant of the LORD. The transgression of the wicked saith within my heart, that there is no fear of God before his eyes.

**Psalms 36:5** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Thy mercy, O LORD, is in the heavens; and thy faithfulness reacheth unto the clouds.

**Psalms 36:6** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Thy righteousness is like the great mountains; thy judgments are a great deep: O LORD, thou preservest man and beast.

**Psalms 37:3** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> Trust in the LORD, and do good; so shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.

**Psalms 37:4** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.

**Psalms 37:5** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Commit thy way unto the LORD; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.

**Psalms 37:7** — `יהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> Rest in the LORD, and wait patiently for him: fret not thyself because of him who prospereth in his way, because of the man who bringeth wicked devices to pass.

**Psalms 37:9** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> For evildoers shall be cut off: but those that wait upon the LORD, they shall inherit the earth.

**Psalms 37:17** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> For the arms of the wicked shall be broken: but the LORD upholdeth the righteous.

**Psalms 37:18** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD knoweth the days of the upright: and their inheritance shall be for ever.

**Psalms 37:20** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> But the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the LORD shall be as the fat of lambs: they shall consume; into smoke shall they consume away.

**Psalms 37:23** — `יְהוָה` (YHWH)
> The steps of a good man are ordered by the LORD: and he delighteth in his way.

**Psalms 37:24** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the LORD upholdeth him with his hand.

**Psalms 37:28** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> For the LORD loveth judgment, and forsaketh not his saints; they are preserved for ever: but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.

**Psalms 37:33** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.

**Psalms 37:34** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Wait on the LORD, and keep his way, and he shall exalt thee to inherit the land: when the wicked are cut off, thou shalt see it.

**Psalms 37:39** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> But the salvation of the righteous is of the LORD: he is their strength in the time of trouble.

**Psalms 37:40** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD shall help them, and deliver them: he shall deliver them from the wicked, and save them, because they trust in him.

**Psalms 38:1** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David, to bring to remembrance. O LORD, rebuke me not in thy wrath: neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.

**Psalms 38:21** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Forsake me not, O LORD: O my God, be not far from me.

**Psalms 39:4** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> LORD, make me to know mine end, and the measure of my days, what it is; that I may know how frail I am.

**Psalms 39:12** — `יְהוָ֡ה` (YHWH)
> Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear unto my cry; hold not thy peace at my tears: for I am a stranger with thee, and a sojourner, as all my fathers were.

**Psalms 40:1** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. I waited patiently for the LORD; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.

**Psalms 40:3** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> And he hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the LORD.

**Psalms 40:4** — `יְ֭הֹוָה` (YHWH)
> Blessed is that man that maketh the LORD his trust, and respecteth not the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.

**Psalms 40:5** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Many, O LORD my God, are thy wonderful works which thou hast done, and thy thoughts which are to us-ward: they cannot be reckoned up in order unto thee: if I would declare and speak of them, they are more than can be numbered.

**Psalms 40:9** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> I have preached righteousness in the great congregation: lo, I have not refrained my lips, O LORD, thou knowest.

**Psalms 40:11** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Withhold not thou thy tender mercies from me, O LORD: let thy lovingkindness and thy truth continually preserve me.

**Psalms 40:13** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me: O LORD, make haste to help me.

**Psalms 40:16** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: let such as love thy salvation say continually, The LORD be magnified.

**Psalms 41:1** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. Blessed is he that considereth the poor: the LORD will deliver him in time of trouble.

**Psalms 41:2** — `יְהוָ֤ה׀` (YHWH)
> The LORD will preserve him, and keep him alive; and he shall be blessed upon the earth: and thou wilt not deliver him unto the will of his enemies.

**Psalms 41:3** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD will strengthen him upon the bed of languishing: thou wilt make all his bed in his sickness.

**Psalms 41:4** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I said, LORD, be merciful unto me: heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee.

**Psalms 41:13** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting, and to everlasting. Amen, and Amen.

**Psalms 42:8** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Yet the LORD will command his lovingkindness in the daytime, and in the night his song shall be with me, and my prayer unto the God of my life.

**Psalms 46:7** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

**Psalms 46:8** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Come, behold the works of the LORD, what desolations he hath made in the earth.

**Psalms 46:11** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

**Psalms 47:2** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD most high is terrible; he is a great King over all the earth.

**Psalms 47:5** — `יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> God is gone up with a shout, the LORD with the sound of a trumpet.

**Psalms 48:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> A Song and Psalm for the sons of Korah. Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, in the mountain of his holiness.

**Psalms 50:1** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of Asaph. The mighty God, even the LORD, hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof.

**Psalms 54:6** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I will freely sacrifice unto thee: I will praise thy name, O LORD; for it is good.

**Psalms 55:16** — `יהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> As for me, I will call upon God; and the LORD shall save me.

**Psalms 55:22** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.

**Psalms 56:10** — `יהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> In God will I praise his word: in the LORD will I praise his word.

**Psalms 58:6** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Break their teeth, O God, in their mouth: break out the great teeth of the young lions, O LORD.

**Psalms 59:3** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> For, lo, they lie in wait for my soul: the mighty are gathered against me; not for my transgression, nor for my sin, O LORD.

**Psalms 59:5** — `יְהוָֽה־` (YHWH)
> Thou therefore, O LORD God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.

**Psalms 59:8** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> But thou, O LORD, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt have all the heathen in derision.

**Psalms 68:16** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Why leap ye, ye high hills? this is the hill which God desireth to dwell in; yea, the LORD will dwell in it for ever.

**Psalms 68:26** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Bless ye God in the congregations, even the Lord, from the fountain of Israel.

**Psalms 69:13** — `יְהוָ֡ה` (YHWH)
> But as for me, my prayer is unto thee, O LORD, in an acceptable time: O God, in the multitude of thy mercy hear me, in the truth of thy salvation.

**Psalms 69:16** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Hear me, O LORD; for thy lovingkindness is good: turn unto me according to the multitude of thy tender mercies.

**Psalms 69:31** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> This also shall please the LORD better than an ox or bullock that hath horns and hoofs.

**Psalms 69:33** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD heareth the poor, and despiseth not his prisoners.

**Psalms 70:1** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David, to bring to remembrance. Make haste, O God, to deliver me; make haste to help me, O LORD.

**Psalms 70:5** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> But I am poor and needy: make haste unto me, O God: thou art my help and my deliverer; O LORD, make no tarrying.

**Psalms 71:1** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> In thee, O LORD, do I put my trust: let me never be put to confusion.

**Psalms 71:5** — `יְ֝הוִ֗ה` (YHWH)
> For thou art my hope, O Lord GOD: thou art my trust from my youth.

**Psalms 72:18** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel, who only doeth wondrous things.

**Psalms 74:18** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Remember this, that the enemy hath reproached, O LORD, and that the foolish people have blasphemed thy name.

**Psalms 75:8** — `יְהוָ֡ה` (YHWH)
> For in the hand of the LORD there is a cup, and the wine is red; it is full of mixture; and he poureth out of the same: but the dregs thereof, all the wicked of the earth shall wring them out, and drink them.

**Psalms 76:11** — `יהוָ֪ה` (YHWH)
> Vow, and pay unto the LORD your God: let all that be round about him bring presents unto him that ought to be feared.

**Psalms 78:4** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> We will not hide them from their children, shewing to the generation to come the praises of the LORD, and his strength, and his wonderful works that he hath done.

**Psalms 78:21** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Therefore the LORD heard this, and was wroth: so a fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger also came up against Israel;

**Psalms 79:5** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> How long, LORD? wilt thou be angry for ever? shall thy jealousy burn like fire?

**Psalms 80:4** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD God of hosts, how long wilt thou be angry against the prayer of thy people?

**Psalms 80:19** — `יְה֘וָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> Turn us again, O LORD God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

**Psalms 81:10** — `יְה֘וָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> I am the LORD thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Egypt: open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.

**Psalms 81:15** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The haters of the LORD should have submitted themselves unto him: but their time should have endured for ever.

**Psalms 83:16** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Fill their faces with shame; that they may seek thy name, O LORD.

**Psalms 83:18** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> That men may know that thou, whose name alone is JEHOVAH, art the most high over all the earth.

**Psalms 84:1** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician upon Gittith, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. How amiable are thy tabernacles, O LORD of hosts!

**Psalms 84:2** — `יְה֫וָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> My soul longeth, yea, even fainteth for the courts of the LORD: my heart and my flesh crieth out for the living God.

**Psalms 84:3** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Yea, the sparrow hath found an house, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, even thine altars, O LORD of hosts, my King, and my God.

**Psalms 84:8** — `יְה֘וָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer: give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.

**Psalms 84:11** — `יְהוָ֪ה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD God is a sun and shield: the LORD will give grace and glory: no good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly.

**Psalms 84:12** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD of hosts, blessed is the man that trusteth in thee.

**Psalms 85:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. LORD, thou hast been favourable unto thy land: thou hast brought back the captivity of Jacob.

**Psalms 85:7** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Shew us thy mercy, O LORD, and grant us thy salvation.

**Psalms 85:8** — `יְה֫וָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> I will hear what God the LORD will speak: for he will speak peace unto his people, and to his saints: but let them not turn again to folly.

**Psalms 85:12** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Yea, the LORD shall give that which is good; and our land shall yield her increase.

**Psalms 86:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> A Prayer of David. Bow down thine ear, O LORD, hear me: for I am poor and needy.

**Psalms 86:6** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Give ear, O LORD, unto my prayer; and attend to the voice of my supplications.

**Psalms 86:11** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Teach me thy way, O LORD; I will walk in thy truth: unite my heart to fear thy name.

**Psalms 86:17** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Shew me a token for good; that they which hate me may see it, and be ashamed: because thou, LORD, hast holpen me, and comforted me.

**Psalms 87:2** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD loveth the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.

**Psalms 87:6** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD shall count, when he writeth up the people, that this man was born there. Selah.

**Psalms 88:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> A Song or Psalm for the sons of Korah, to the chief Musician upon Mahalath Leannoth, Maschil of Heman the Ezrahite. O LORD God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before thee:

**Psalms 88:9** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Mine eye mourneth by reason of affliction: LORD, I have called daily upon thee, I have stretched out my hands unto thee.

**Psalms 88:13** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> But unto thee have I cried, O LORD; and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.

**Psalms 88:14** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> LORD, why castest thou off my soul? why hidest thou thy face from me?

**Psalms 89:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Maschil of Ethan the Ezrahite. I will sing of the mercies of the LORD for ever: with my mouth will I make known thy faithfulness to all generations.

**Psalms 89:5** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And the heavens shall praise thy wonders, O LORD: thy faithfulness also in the congregation of the saints.

**Psalms 89:6** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For who in the heaven can be compared unto the LORD? who among the sons of the mighty can be likened unto the LORD?

**Psalms 89:8** — `יְהוָ֤ה׀` (YHWH)
> O LORD God of hosts, who is a strong LORD like unto thee? or to thy faithfulness round about thee?

**Psalms 89:15** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed is the people that know the joyful sound: they shall walk, O LORD, in the light of thy countenance.

**Psalms 89:18** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD is our defence; and the Holy One of Israel is our king.

**Psalms 89:46** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> How long, LORD? wilt thou hide thyself for ever? shall thy wrath burn like fire?

**Psalms 89:51** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Wherewith thine enemies have reproached, O LORD; wherewith they have reproached the footsteps of thine anointed.

**Psalms 89:52** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed be the LORD for evermore. Amen, and Amen.

**Psalms 90:13** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Return, O LORD, how long? and let it repent thee concerning thy servants.

**Psalms 91:2** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> I will say of the LORD, He is my refuge and my fortress: my God; in him will I trust.

**Psalms 91:9** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Because thou hast made the LORD, which is my refuge, even the most High, thy habitation;

**Psalms 92:1** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm or Song for the sabbath day. It is a good thing to give thanks unto the LORD, and to sing praises unto thy name, O most High:

**Psalms 92:4** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> For thou, LORD, hast made me glad through thy work: I will triumph in the works of thy hands.

**Psalms 92:5** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD, how great are thy works! and thy thoughts are very deep.

**Psalms 92:8** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> But thou, LORD, art most high for evermore.

**Psalms 92:9** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> For, lo, thine enemies, O LORD, for, lo, thine enemies shall perish; all the workers of iniquity shall be scattered.

**Psalms 92:13** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Those that be planted in the house of the LORD shall flourish in the courts of our God.

**Psalms 92:15** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> To shew that the LORD is upright: he is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him.

**Psalms 93:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD reigneth, he is clothed with majesty; the LORD is clothed with strength, wherewith he hath girded himself: the world also is stablished, that it cannot be moved.

**Psalms 93:3** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The floods have lifted up, O LORD, the floods have lifted up their voice; the floods lift up their waves.

**Psalms 93:4** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> The LORD on high is mightier than the noise of many waters, yea, than the mighty waves of the sea.

**Psalms 93:5** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Thy testimonies are very sure: holiness becometh thine house, O LORD, for ever.

**Psalms 94:1** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD God, to whom vengeance belongeth; O God, to whom vengeance belongeth, shew thyself.

**Psalms 94:3** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> LORD, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked triumph?

**Psalms 94:5** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> They break in pieces thy people, O LORD, and afflict thine heritage.

**Psalms 94:11** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity.

**Psalms 94:14** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD will not cast off his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.

**Psalms 94:17** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Unless the LORD had been my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.

**Psalms 94:18** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> When I said, My foot slippeth; thy mercy, O LORD, held me up.

**Psalms 94:22** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> But the LORD is my defence; and my God is the rock of my refuge.

**Psalms 94:23** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity, and shall cut them off in their own wickedness; yea, the LORD our God shall cut them off.

**Psalms 95:1** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> O come, let us sing unto the LORD: let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation.

**Psalms 95:3** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD is a great God, and a great King above all gods.

**Psalms 95:6** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> O come, let us worship and bow down: let us kneel before the LORD our maker.

**Psalms 96:1** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> O sing unto the LORD a new song: sing unto the LORD, all the earth.

**Psalms 96:2** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> Sing unto the LORD, bless his name; shew forth his salvation from day to day.

**Psalms 96:4** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD is great, and greatly to be praised: he is to be feared above all gods.

**Psalms 96:5** — `יהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> For all the gods of the nations are idols: but the LORD made the heavens.

**Psalms 96:7** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> Give unto the LORD, O ye kindreds of the people, give unto the LORD glory and strength.

**Psalms 96:8** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> Give unto the LORD the glory due unto his name: bring an offering, and come into his courts.

**Psalms 96:9** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> O worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness: fear before him, all the earth.

**Psalms 96:10** — `יְה֘וָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> Say among the heathen that the LORD reigneth: the world also shall be established that it shall not be moved: he shall judge the people righteously.

**Psalms 96:13** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Before the LORD: for he cometh, for he cometh to judge the earth: he shall judge the world with righteousness, and the people with his truth.

**Psalms 97:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD reigneth; let the earth rejoice; let the multitude of isles be glad thereof.

**Psalms 97:5** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The hills melted like wax at the presence of the LORD, at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.

**Psalms 97:8** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Zion heard, and was glad; and the daughters of Judah rejoiced because of thy judgments, O LORD.

**Psalms 97:9** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> For thou, LORD, art high above all the earth: thou art exalted far above all gods.

**Psalms 97:10** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Ye that love the LORD, hate evil: he preserveth the souls of his saints; he delivereth them out of the hand of the wicked.

**Psalms 97:12** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Rejoice in the LORD, ye righteous; and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.

**Psalms 98:1** — `יהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> A Psalm. O sing unto the LORD a new song; for he hath done marvellous things: his right hand, and his holy arm, hath gotten him the victory.

**Psalms 98:2** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD hath made known his salvation: his righteousness hath he openly shewed in the sight of the heathen.

**Psalms 98:4** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> Make a joyful noise unto the LORD, all the earth: make a loud noise, and rejoice, and sing praise.

**Psalms 98:5** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Sing unto the LORD with the harp; with the harp, and the voice of a psalm.

**Psalms 98:6** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> With trumpets and sound of cornet make a joyful noise before the LORD, the King.

**Psalms 98:9** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Before the LORD; for he cometh to judge the earth: with righteousness shall he judge the world, and the people with equity.

**Psalms 99:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD reigneth; let the people tremble: he sitteth between the cherubims; let the earth be moved.

**Psalms 99:2** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is great in Zion; and he is high above all the people.

**Psalms 99:5** — `יְה֘וָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> Exalt ye the LORD our God, and worship at his footstool; for he is holy.

**Psalms 99:6** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among them that call upon his name; they called upon the LORD, and he answered them.

**Psalms 99:9** — `יְה֘וָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> Exalt the LORD our God, and worship at his holy hill; for the LORD our God is holy.

**Psalms 100:1** — `יהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of praise. Make a joyful noise unto the LORD, all ye lands.

**Psalms 100:2** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Serve the LORD with gladness: come before his presence with singing.

**Psalms 100:3** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> Know ye that the LORD he is God: it is he that hath made us, and not we ourselves; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.

**Psalms 100:5** — `יְ֭הֹוָה` (YHWH)
> For the LORD is good; his mercy is everlasting; and his truth endureth to all generations.

**Psalms 101:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. I will sing of mercy and judgment: unto thee, O LORD, will I sing.

**Psalms 101:8** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> I will early destroy all the wicked of the land; that I may cut off all wicked doers from the city of the LORD.

**Psalms 102:1** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> A Prayer of the afflicted, when he is overwhelmed, and poureth out his complaint before the LORD. Hear my prayer, O LORD, and let my cry come unto thee.

**Psalms 102:12** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> But thou, O LORD, shalt endure for ever; and thy remembrance unto all generations.

**Psalms 102:15** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> So the heathen shall fear the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth thy glory.

**Psalms 102:16** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> When the LORD shall build up Zion, he shall appear in his glory.

**Psalms 102:19** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> For he hath looked down from the height of his sanctuary; from heaven did the LORD behold the earth;

**Psalms 102:21** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> To declare the name of the LORD in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem;

**Psalms 102:22** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> When the people are gathered together, and the kingdoms, to serve the LORD.

**Psalms 103:1** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. Bless the LORD, O my soul: and all that is within me, bless his holy name.

**Psalms 103:2** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits:

**Psalms 103:6** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD executeth righteousness and judgment for all that are oppressed.

**Psalms 103:8** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.

**Psalms 103:13** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Like as a father pitieth his children, so the LORD pitieth them that fear him.

**Psalms 103:17** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> But the mercy of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting upon them that fear him, and his righteousness unto children's children;

**Psalms 103:19** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD hath prepared his throne in the heavens; and his kingdom ruleth over all.

**Psalms 103:20** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Bless the LORD, ye his angels, that excel in strength, that do his commandments, hearkening unto the voice of his word.

**Psalms 103:21** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Bless ye the LORD, all ye his hosts; ye ministers of his, that do his pleasure.

**Psalms 103:22** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> Bless the LORD, all his works in all places of his dominion: bless the LORD, O my soul.

**Psalms 104:1** — `יְה֫וָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Bless the LORD, O my soul. O LORD my God, thou art very great; thou art clothed with honour and majesty.

**Psalms 104:16** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The trees of the LORD are full of sap; the cedars of Lebanon, which he hath planted;

**Psalms 104:24** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> O LORD, how manifold are thy works! in wisdom hast thou made them all: the earth is full of thy riches.

**Psalms 104:31** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The glory of the LORD shall endure for ever: the LORD shall rejoice in his works.

**Psalms 104:33** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I will sing unto the LORD as long as I live: I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.

**Psalms 104:34** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> My meditation of him shall be sweet: I will be glad in the LORD.

**Psalms 104:35** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Let the sinners be consumed out of the earth, and let the wicked be no more. Bless thou the LORD, O my soul. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 105:1** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> O give thanks unto the LORD; call upon his name: make known his deeds among the people.

**Psalms 105:3** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the LORD.

**Psalms 105:4** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Seek the LORD, and his strength: seek his face evermore.

**Psalms 105:7** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> He is the LORD our God: his judgments are in all the earth.

**Psalms 105:19** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Until the time that his word came: the word of the LORD tried him.

**Psalms 106:1** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Praise ye the LORD. O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 106:2** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD? who can shew forth all his praise?

**Psalms 106:4** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Remember me, O LORD, with the favour that thou bearest unto thy people: O visit me with thy salvation;

**Psalms 106:16** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> They envied Moses also in the camp, and Aaron the saint of the LORD.

**Psalms 106:25** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> But murmured in their tents, and hearkened not unto the voice of the LORD.

**Psalms 106:40** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Therefore was the wrath of the LORD kindled against his people, insomuch that he abhorred his own inheritance.

**Psalms 106:47** — `יְה֘וָ֤ה` (YHWH)
> Save us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the heathen, to give thanks unto thy holy name, and to triumph in thy praise.

**Psalms 106:48** — `יְהוָ֨ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting to everlasting: and let all the people say, Amen. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 107:1** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 107:2** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Let the redeemed of the LORD say so, whom he hath redeemed from the hand of the enemy;

**Psalms 107:8** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Oh that men would praise the LORD for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!

**Psalms 107:15** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Oh that men would praise the LORD for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!

**Psalms 107:21** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Oh that men would praise the LORD for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!

**Psalms 107:24** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> These see the works of the LORD, and his wonders in the deep.

**Psalms 107:31** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Oh that men would praise the LORD for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!

**Psalms 107:43** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Whoso is wise, and will observe these things, even they shall understand the lovingkindness of the LORD.

**Psalms 108:3** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> I will praise thee, O LORD, among the people: and I will sing praises unto thee among the nations.

**Psalms 109:14** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered with the LORD; and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.

**Psalms 109:15** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Let them be before the LORD continually, that he may cut off the memory of them from the earth.

**Psalms 109:20** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Let this be the reward of mine adversaries from the LORD, and of them that speak evil against my soul.

**Psalms 109:26** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Help me, O LORD my God: O save me according to thy mercy:

**Psalms 109:27** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> That they may know that this is thy hand; that thou, LORD, hast done it.

**Psalms 109:30** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I will greatly praise the LORD with my mouth; yea, I will praise him among the multitude.

**Psalms 110:1** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> A Psalm of David. The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.

**Psalms 110:2** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The LORD shall send the rod of thy strength out of Zion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies.

**Psalms 110:4** — `יְהוָ֨ה׀` (YHWH)
> The LORD hath sworn, and will not repent, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek.

**Psalms 111:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Praise ye the LORD. I will praise the LORD with my whole heart, in the assembly of the upright, and in the congregation.

**Psalms 111:2** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The works of the LORD are great, sought out of all them that have pleasure therein.

**Psalms 111:4** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> He hath made his wonderful works to be remembered: the LORD is gracious and full of compassion.

**Psalms 111:10** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments: his praise endureth for ever.

**Psalms 112:1** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Praise ye the LORD. Blessed is the man that feareth the LORD, that delighteth greatly in his commandments.

**Psalms 112:7** — `יהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> He shall not be afraid of evil tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the LORD.

**Psalms 113:1** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Praise ye the LORD. Praise, O ye servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD.

**Psalms 113:2** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed be the name of the LORD from this time forth and for evermore.

**Psalms 113:3** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> From the rising of the sun unto the going down of the same the LORD'S name is to be praised.

**Psalms 113:4** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.

**Psalms 113:5** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Who is like unto the LORD our God, who dwelleth on high,

**Psalms 115:1** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto thy name give glory, for thy mercy, and for thy truth's sake.

**Psalms 115:9** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> O Israel, trust thou in the LORD: he is their help and their shield.

**Psalms 115:10** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD: he is their help and their shield.

**Psalms 115:11** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Ye that fear the LORD, trust in the LORD: he is their help and their shield.

**Psalms 115:12** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> The LORD hath been mindful of us: he will bless us; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron.

**Psalms 115:13** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> He will bless them that fear the LORD, both small and great.

**Psalms 115:14** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD shall increase you more and more, you and your children.

**Psalms 115:15** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Ye are blessed of the LORD which made heaven and earth.

**Psalms 115:16** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The heaven, even the heavens, are the LORD'S: but the earth hath he given to the children of men.

**Psalms 116:1** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> I love the LORD, because he hath heard my voice and my supplications.

**Psalms 116:4** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Then called I upon the name of the LORD; O LORD, I beseech thee, deliver my soul.

**Psalms 116:5** — `יְהֹוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; yea, our God is merciful.

**Psalms 116:6** — `יְהֹוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD preserveth the simple: I was brought low, and he helped me.

**Psalms 116:7** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Return unto thy rest, O my soul; for the LORD hath dealt bountifully with thee.

**Psalms 116:9** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> I will walk before the LORD in the land of the living.

**Psalms 116:12** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> What shall I render unto the LORD for all his benefits toward me?

**Psalms 116:13** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I will take the cup of salvation, and call upon the name of the LORD.

**Psalms 116:14** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I will pay my vows unto the LORD now in the presence of all his people.

**Psalms 116:15** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Precious in the sight of the LORD is the death of his saints.

**Psalms 116:16** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> O LORD, truly I am thy servant; I am thy servant, and the son of thine handmaid: thou hast loosed my bonds.

**Psalms 116:17** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I will offer to thee the sacrifice of thanksgiving, and will call upon the name of the LORD.

**Psalms 116:18** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> I will pay my vows unto the LORD now in the presence of all his people,

**Psalms 116:19** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> In the courts of the LORD'S house, in the midst of thee, O Jerusalem. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 117:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> O praise the LORD, all ye nations: praise him, all ye people.

**Psalms 117:2** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> For his merciful kindness is great toward us: and the truth of the LORD endureth for ever. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 118:1** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: because his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 118:4** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Let them now that fear the LORD say, that his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 118:6** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is on my side; I will not fear: what can man do unto me?

**Psalms 118:7** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD taketh my part with them that help me: therefore shall I see my desire upon them that hate me.

**Psalms 118:8** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> It is better to trust in the LORD than to put confidence in man.

**Psalms 118:9** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> It is better to trust in the LORD than to put confidence in princes.

**Psalms 118:10** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> All nations compassed me about: but in the name of the LORD will I destroy them.

**Psalms 118:11** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> They compassed me about; yea, they compassed me about: but in the name of the LORD I will destroy them.

**Psalms 118:12** — `יְ֝הוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> They compassed me about like bees; they are quenched as the fire of thorns: for in the name of the LORD I will destroy them.

**Psalms 118:13** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Thou hast thrust sore at me that I might fall: but the LORD helped me.

**Psalms 118:15** — `יְ֝הוָה` (YHWH)
> The voice of rejoicing and salvation is in the tabernacles of the righteous: the right hand of the LORD doeth valiantly.

**Psalms 118:16** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> The right hand of the LORD is exalted: the right hand of the LORD doeth valiantly.

**Psalms 118:20** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> This gate of the LORD, into which the righteous shall enter.

**Psalms 118:23** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> This is the LORD'S doing; it is marvellous in our eyes.

**Psalms 118:24** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> This is the day which the LORD hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.

**Psalms 118:25** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> Save now, I beseech thee, O LORD: O LORD, I beseech thee, send now prosperity.

**Psalms 118:26** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed be he that cometh in the name of the LORD: we have blessed you out of the house of the LORD.

**Psalms 118:27** — `יְהוָה֮` (YHWH)
> God is the LORD, which hath shewed us light: bind the sacrifice with cords, even unto the horns of the altar.

**Psalms 118:29** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 120:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> A Song of degrees. In my distress I cried unto the LORD, and he heard me.

**Psalms 120:2** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> Deliver my soul, O LORD, from lying lips, and from a deceitful tongue.

**Psalms 121:2** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> My help cometh from the LORD, which made heaven and earth.

**Psalms 121:5** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD is thy keeper: the LORD is thy shade upon thy right hand.

**Psalms 121:7** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD shall preserve thee from all evil: he shall preserve thy soul.

**Psalms 121:8** — `יְֽהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD shall preserve thy going out and thy coming in from this time forth, and even for evermore.

**Psalms 122:1** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> A Song of degrees of David. I was glad when they said unto me, Let us go into the house of the LORD.

**Psalms 122:4** — `יְהוָֽה׃` (YHWH)
> Whither the tribes go up, the tribes of the LORD, unto the testimony of Israel, to give thanks unto the name of the LORD.

**Psalms 122:9** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Because of the house of the LORD our God I will seek thy good.

**Psalms 123:2** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hand of their masters, and as the eyes of a maiden unto the hand of her mistress; so our eyes wait upon the LORD our God, until that he have mercy upon us.

**Psalms 123:3** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Have mercy upon us, O LORD, have mercy upon us: for we are exceedingly filled with contempt.

**Psalms 124:1** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> A Song of degrees of David. If it had not been the LORD who was on our side, now may Israel say;

**Psalms 124:2** — `יְ֭הוָה` (YHWH)
> If it had not been the LORD who was on our side, when men rose up against us:

**Psalms 124:6** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Blessed be the LORD, who hath not given us as a prey to their teeth.

**Psalms 124:8** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Our help is in the name of the LORD, who made heaven and earth.

**Psalms 125:1** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> A Song of degrees. They that trust in the LORD shall be as mount Zion, which cannot be removed, but abideth for ever.

**Psalms 125:2** — `יהוָה` (YHWH)
> As the mountains are round about Jerusalem, so the LORD is round about his people from henceforth even for ever.
