> For I will no more pity the inhabitants of the land, saith the LORD: but, lo, I will deliver the men every one into his neighbour's hand, and into the hand of his king: and they shall smite the land, and out of their hand I will not deliver them.

**Zechariah 11:11** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And it was broken in that day: and so the poor of the flock that waited upon me knew that it was the word of the LORD.

**Zechariah 11:15** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD said unto me, Take unto thee yet the instruments of a foolish shepherd.

**Zechariah 12:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> The burden of the word of the LORD for Israel, saith the LORD, which stretcheth forth the heavens, and layeth the foundation of the earth, and formeth the spirit of man within him.

**Zechariah 12:4** — `יְהוָ֗ה` (YHWH)
> In that day, saith the LORD, I will smite every horse with astonishment, and his rider with madness: and I will open mine eyes upon the house of Judah, and will smite every horse of the people with blindness.

**Zechariah 12:5** — `יהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And the governors of Judah shall say in their heart, The inhabitants of Jerusalem shall be my strength in the LORD of hosts their God.

**Zechariah 12:7** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD also shall save the tents of Judah first, that the glory of the house of David and the glory of the inhabitants of Jerusalem do not magnify themselves against Judah.

**Zechariah 12:8** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> In that day shall the LORD defend the inhabitants of Jerusalem; and he that is feeble among them at that day shall be as David; and the house of David shall be as God, as the angel of the LORD before them.

**Zechariah 13:2** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the LORD of hosts, that I will cut off the names of the idols out of the land, and they shall no more be remembered: and also I will cause the prophets and the unclean spirit to pass out of the land.

**Zechariah 13:3** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> And it shall come to pass, that when any shall yet prophesy, then his father and his mother that begat him shall say unto him, Thou shalt not live; for thou speakest lies in the name of the LORD: and his father and his mother that begat him shall thrust him through when he prophesieth.

**Zechariah 13:8** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And it shall come to pass, that in all the land, saith the LORD, two parts therein shall be cut off and die; but the third shall be left therein.

**Zechariah 13:9** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And I will bring the third part through the fire, and will refine them as silver is refined, and will try them as gold is tried: they shall call on my name, and I will hear them: I will say, It is my people: and they shall say, The LORD is my God.

**Zechariah 14:1** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Behold, the day of the LORD cometh, and thy spoil shall be divided in the midst of thee.

**Zechariah 14:5** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall flee to the valley of the mountains; for the valley of the mountains shall reach unto Azal: yea, ye shall flee, like as ye fled from before the earthquake in the days of Uzziah king of Judah: and the LORD my God shall come, and all the saints with thee.

**Zechariah 14:7** — `יהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> But it shall be one day which shall be known to the LORD, not day, nor night: but it shall come to pass, that at evening time it shall be light.

**Zechariah 14:9** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And the LORD shall be king over all the earth: in that day shall there be one LORD, and his name one.

**Zechariah 14:12** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And this shall be the plague wherewith the LORD will smite all the people that have fought against Jerusalem; Their flesh shall consume away while they stand upon their feet, and their eyes shall consume away in their holes, and their tongue shall consume away in their mouth.

**Zechariah 14:13** — `יְהוָ֛ה` (YHWH)
> And it shall come to pass in that day, that a great tumult from the LORD shall be among them; and they shall lay hold every one on the hand of his neighbour, and his hand shall rise up against the hand of his neighbour.

**Zechariah 14:16** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And it shall come to pass, that every one that is left of all the nations which came against Jerusalem shall even go up from year to year to worship the King, the LORD of hosts, and to keep the feast of tabernacles.

**Zechariah 14:17** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And it shall be, that whoso will not come up of all the families of the earth unto Jerusalem to worship the King, the LORD of hosts, even upon them shall be no rain.

**Zechariah 14:18** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> And if the family of Egypt go not up, and come not, that have no rain; there shall be the plague, wherewith the LORD will smite the heathen that come not up to keep the feast of tabernacles.

**Zechariah 14:20** — `יהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> In that day shall there be upon the bells of the horses, HOLINESS UNTO THE LORD; and the pots in the LORD'S house shall be like the bowls before the altar.

**Zechariah 14:21** — `יהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Yea, every pot in Jerusalem and in Judah shall be holiness unto the LORD of hosts: and all they that sacrifice shall come and take of them, and seethe therein: and in that day there shall be no more the Canaanite in the house of the LORD of hosts.

### Malachi (36 verses)

**Malachi 1:1** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> The burden of the word of the LORD to Israel by Malachi.

**Malachi 1:2** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> I have loved you, saith the LORD. Yet ye say, Wherein hast thou loved us? Was not Esau Jacob's brother? saith the LORD: yet I loved Jacob,

**Malachi 1:4** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Whereas Edom saith, We are impoverished, but we will return and build the desolate places; thus saith the LORD of hosts, They shall build, but I will throw down; and they shall call them, The border of wickedness, and, The people against whom the LORD hath indignation for ever.

**Malachi 1:5** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And your eyes shall see, and ye shall say, The LORD will be magnified from the border of Israel.

**Malachi 1:6** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> A son honoureth his father, and a servant his master: if then I be a father, where is mine honour? and if I be a master, where is my fear? saith the LORD of hosts unto you, O priests, that despise my name. And ye say, Wherein have we despised thy name?

**Malachi 1:7** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Ye offer polluted bread upon mine altar; and ye say, Wherein have we polluted thee? In that ye say, The table of the LORD is contemptible.

**Malachi 1:8** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And if ye offer the blind for sacrifice, is it not evil? and if ye offer the lame and sick, is it not evil? offer it now unto thy governor; will he be pleased with thee, or accept thy person? saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 1:9** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And now, I pray you, beseech God that he will be gracious unto us: this hath been by your means: will he regard your persons? saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 1:10** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Who is there even among you that would shut the doors for nought? neither do ye kindle fire on mine altar for nought. I have no pleasure in you, saith the LORD of hosts, neither will I accept an offering at your hand.

**Malachi 1:11** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> For from the rising of the sun even unto the going down of the same my name shall be great among the Gentiles; and in every place incense shall be offered unto my name, and a pure offering: for my name shall be great among the heathen, saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 1:13** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Ye said also, Behold, what a weariness is it! and ye have snuffed at it, saith the LORD of hosts; and ye brought that which was torn, and the lame, and the sick; thus ye brought an offering: should I accept this of your hand? saith the LORD.

**Malachi 1:14** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> But cursed be the deceiver, which hath in his flock a male, and voweth, and sacrificeth unto the Lord a corrupt thing: for I am a great King, saith the LORD of hosts, and my name is dreadful among the heathen.

**Malachi 2:2** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> If ye will not hear, and if ye will not lay it to heart, to give glory unto my name, saith the LORD of hosts, I will even send a curse upon you, and I will curse your blessings: yea, I have cursed them already, because ye do not lay it to heart.

**Malachi 2:4** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall know that I have sent this commandment unto you, that my covenant might be with Levi, saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 2:7** — `יְהוָֽה־` (YHWH)
> For the priest's lips should keep knowledge, and they should seek the law at his mouth: for he is the messenger of the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 2:8** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> But ye are departed out of the way; ye have caused many to stumble at the law; ye have corrupted the covenant of Levi, saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 2:11** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> Judah hath dealt treacherously, and an abomination is committed in Israel and in Jerusalem; for Judah hath profaned the holiness of the LORD which he loved, and hath married the daughter of a strange god.

**Malachi 2:12** — `יְהוָ֜ה` (YHWH)
> The LORD will cut off the man that doeth this, the master and the scholar, out of the tabernacles of Jacob, and him that offereth an offering unto the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 2:13** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And this have ye done again, covering the altar of the LORD with tears, with weeping, and with crying out, insomuch that he regardeth not the offering any more, or receiveth it with good will at your hand.

**Malachi 2:14** — `יְהוָה֩` (YHWH)
> Yet ye say, Wherefore? Because the LORD hath been witness between thee and the wife of thy youth, against whom thou hast dealt treacherously: yet is she thy companion, and the wife of thy covenant.

**Malachi 2:16** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> For the LORD, the God of Israel, saith that he hateth putting away: for one covereth violence with his garment, saith the LORD of hosts: therefore take heed to your spirit, that ye deal not treacherously.

**Malachi 2:17** — `יְהוָה֙` (YHWH)
> Ye have wearied the LORD with your words. Yet ye say, Wherein have we wearied him? When ye say, Every one that doeth evil is good in the sight of the LORD, and he delighteth in them; or, Where is the God of judgment?

**Malachi 3:1** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> Behold, I will send my messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me: and the Lord, whom ye seek, shall suddenly come to his temple, even the messenger of the covenant, whom ye delight in: behold, he shall come, saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 3:3** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> And he shall sit as a refiner and purifier of silver: and he shall purify the sons of Levi, and purge them as gold and silver, that they may offer unto the LORD an offering in righteousness.

**Malachi 3:4** — `יהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Then shall the offering of Judah and Jerusalem be pleasant unto the LORD, as in the days of old, and as in former years.

**Malachi 3:5** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And I will come near to you to judgment; and I will be a swift witness against the sorcerers, and against the adulterers, and against false swearers, and against those that oppress the hireling in his wages, the widow, and the fatherless, and that turn aside the stranger from his right, and fear not me, saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 3:6** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> For I am the LORD, I change not; therefore ye sons of Jacob are not consumed.

**Malachi 3:7** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Even from the days of your fathers ye are gone away from mine ordinances, and have not kept them. Return unto me, and I will return unto you, saith the LORD of hosts. But ye said, Wherein shall we return?

**Malachi 3:10** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse, that there may be meat in mine house, and prove me now herewith, saith the LORD of hosts, if I will not open you the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that there shall not be room enough to receive it.

**Malachi 3:11** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And I will rebuke the devourer for your sakes, and he shall not destroy the fruits of your ground; neither shall your vine cast her fruit before the time in the field, saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 3:12** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And all nations shall call you blessed: for ye shall be a delightsome land, saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 3:13** — `יְהוָ֑ה` (YHWH)
> Your words have been stout against me, saith the LORD. Yet ye say, What have we spoken so much against thee?

**Malachi 3:16** — `יְהוָ֖ה` (YHWH)
> Then they that feared the LORD spake often one to another: and the LORD hearkened, and heard it, and a book of remembrance was written before him for them that feared the LORD, and that thought upon his name.

**Malachi 3:17** — `יְהוָ֣ה` (YHWH)
> And they shall be mine, saith the LORD of hosts, in that day when I make up my jewels; and I will spare them, as a man spareth his own son that serveth him.

**Malachi 4:3** — `יְהוָ֥ה` (YHWH)
> And ye shall tread down the wicked; for they shall be ashes under the soles of your feet in the day that I shall do this, saith the LORD of hosts.

**Malachi 4:5** — `יְהוָ֔ה` (YHWH)
> Behold, I will send you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful day of the LORD:

## `H3050` — Yah ("LORD / JAH")

> Shortened poetic form of YHWH, used in Hallelu-Yah and in poetic/prophetic contexts.

**45 verses found.**

### Exodus (2 verses)

**Exodus 15:2** — `יָ֔הּ` (the Lord)
> The LORD is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation: he is my God, and I will prepare him an habitation; my father's God, and I will exalt him.

**Exodus 17:16** — `יָ֔הּ` (the Lord)
> For he said, Because the LORD hath sworn that the LORD will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.

### Psalms (40 verses)

**Psalms 68:4** — `יָ֥הּ` (the Lord)
> Sing unto God, sing praises to his name: extol him that rideth upon the heavens by his name JAH, and rejoice before him.

**Psalms 68:18** — `יָ֬הּ` (the Lord)
> Thou hast ascended on high, thou hast led captivity captive: thou hast received gifts for men; yea, for the rebellious also, that the LORD God might dwell among them.

**Psalms 77:11** — `יָ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> I will remember the works of the LORD: surely I will remember thy wonders of old.

**Psalms 89:8** — `יָ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> O LORD God of hosts, who is a strong LORD like unto thee? or to thy faithfulness round about thee?

**Psalms 94:7** — `יָּ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> Yet they say, The LORD shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob regard it.

**Psalms 94:12** — `יָּ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> Blessed is the man whom thou chastenest, O LORD, and teachest him out of thy law;

**Psalms 102:18** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> This shall be written for the generation to come: and the people which shall be created shall praise the LORD.

**Psalms 104:35** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> Let the sinners be consumed out of the earth, and let the wicked be no more. Bless thou the LORD, O my soul. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 105:45** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> That they might observe his statutes, and keep his laws. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 106:1** — `יָ֨הּ׀` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD. O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 106:48** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting to everlasting: and let all the people say, Amen. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 111:1** — `יָ֨הּ׀` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD. I will praise the LORD with my whole heart, in the assembly of the upright, and in the congregation.

**Psalms 112:1** — `יָ֨הּ׀` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD. Blessed is the man that feareth the LORD, that delighteth greatly in his commandments.

**Psalms 113:1** — `יָ֨הּ׀` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD. Praise, O ye servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD.

**Psalms 113:9** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> He maketh the barren woman to keep house, and to be a joyful mother of children. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 115:17** — `יָ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> The dead praise not the LORD, neither any that go down into silence.

**Psalms 115:18** — `יָ֗הּ` (the Lord)
> But we will bless the LORD from this time forth and for evermore. Praise the LORD.

**Psalms 116:19** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> In the courts of the LORD'S house, in the midst of thee, O Jerusalem. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 117:2** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> For his merciful kindness is great toward us: and the truth of the LORD endureth for ever. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 118:5** — `יָּ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> I called upon the LORD in distress: the LORD answered me, and set me in a large place.

**Psalms 118:14** — `יָ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> The LORD is my strength and song, and is become my salvation.

**Psalms 118:17** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> I shall not die, but live, and declare the works of the LORD.

**Psalms 118:18** — `יָּ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> The LORD hath chastened me sore: but he hath not given me over unto death.

**Psalms 118:19** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> Open to me the gates of righteousness: I will go into them, and I will praise the LORD:

**Psalms 122:4** — `יָ֭הּ` (the Lord)
> Whither the tribes go up, the tribes of the LORD, unto the testimony of Israel, to give thanks unto the name of the LORD.

**Psalms 130:3** — `יָ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> If thou, LORD, shouldest mark iniquities, O Lord, who shall stand?

**Psalms 135:1** — `יָ֨הּ׀` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD. Praise ye the name of the LORD; praise him, O ye servants of the LORD.

**Psalms 135:3** — `יָהּ` (the Lord)
> Praise the LORD; for the LORD is good: sing praises unto his name; for it is pleasant.

**Psalms 135:4** — `יָ֑הּ` (the Lord)
> For the LORD hath chosen Jacob unto himself, and Israel for his peculiar treasure.

**Psalms 135:21** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> Blessed be the LORD out of Zion, which dwelleth at Jerusalem. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 146:1** — `יָ֡הּ` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD. Praise the LORD, O my soul.

**Psalms 146:10** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> The LORD shall reign for ever, even thy God, O Zion, unto all generations. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 147:1** — `יָ֨הּ׀` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD: for it is good to sing praises unto our God; for it is pleasant; and praise is comely.

**Psalms 147:20** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> He hath not dealt so with any nation: and as for his judgments, they have not known them. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 148:1** — `יָ֨הּ׀` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD. Praise ye the LORD from the heavens: praise him in the heights.

**Psalms 148:14** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> He also exalteth the horn of his people, the praise of all his saints; even of the children of Israel, a people near unto him. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 149:1** — `יָ֨הּ׀` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD. Sing unto the LORD a new song, and his praise in the congregation of saints.

**Psalms 149:9** — `יָֽהּ׃` (the Lord)
> To execute upon them the judgment written: this honour have all his saints. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 150:1** — `יָ֨הּ׀` (the Lord)
> Praise ye the LORD. Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his power.

**Psalms 150:6** — `יָ֗הּ` (the Lord)

## `H430` — Elohim ("God")

> And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.

**Genesis 1:7** — `אֱלֹהִים֮` (god(s))
> And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament: and it was so.

**Genesis 1:8** — `אֱלֹהִ֛ים` (god(s))
> And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

**Genesis 1:9** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))

**Genesis 1:10** — `אֱלֹהִ֤ים׀` (god(s))
> And God called the dry land Earth; and the gathering together of the waters called he Seas: and God saw that it was good.

> And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth,

### Joshua (65 verses)

**Joshua 1:9** — `אֱלֹהֶ֔יךָ` (god(s))
> Have not I commanded thee? Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.

**Joshua 1:11** — `אֱלֹֽהֵיכֶ֔ם` (god(s))
> Pass through the host, and command the people, saying, Prepare you victuals; for within three days ye shall pass over this Jordan, to go in to possess the land, which the LORD your God giveth you to possess it.

**Joshua 1:13** — `אֱלֹהֵיכֶם֙` (god(s))
> Remember the word which Moses the servant of the LORD commanded you, saying, The LORD your God hath given you rest, and hath given you this land.

**Joshua 1:15** — `אֱלֹֽהֵיכֶ֖ם` (god(s))
> Until the LORD have given your brethren rest, as he hath given you, and they also have possessed the land which the LORD your God giveth them: then ye shall return unto the land of your possession, and enjoy it, which Moses the LORD'S servant gave you on this side Jordan toward the sunrising.

**Joshua 1:17** — `אֱלֹהֶ֨יךָ֙` (god(s))
> According as we hearkened unto Moses in all things, so will we hearken unto thee: only the LORD thy God be with thee, as he was with Moses.

**Joshua 2:11** — `אֱלֹֽהֵיכֶ֔ם` (god(s))
> And as soon as we had heard these things, our hearts did melt, neither did there remain any more courage in any man, because of you: for the LORD your God, he is God in heaven above, and in earth beneath.

**Joshua 3:3** — `אֱלֹ֣הֵיכֶ֔ם` (god(s))
> And they commanded the people, saying, When ye see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God, and the priests the Levites bearing it, then ye shall remove from your place, and go after it.

**Joshua 3:9** — `אֱלֹֽהֵיכֶֽם׃` (god(s))
> And Joshua said unto the children of Israel, Come hither, and hear the words of the LORD your God.

**Joshua 4:5** — `אֱלֹֽהֵיכֶ֖ם` (god(s))
> And Joshua said unto them, Pass over before the ark of the LORD your God into the midst of Jordan, and take ye up every man of you a stone upon his shoulder, according unto the number of the tribes of the children of Israel:

**Joshua 4:23** — `אֱלֹהֵיכֶ֜ם` (god(s))
> For the LORD your God dried up the waters of Jordan from before you, until ye were passed over, as the LORD your God did to the Red sea, which he dried up from before us, until we were gone over:

**Joshua 4:24** — `אֱלֹהֵיכֶ֖ם` (god(s))
> That all the people of the earth might know the hand of the LORD, that it is mighty: that ye might fear the LORD your God for ever.

**Joshua 7:13** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> Up, sanctify the people, and say, Sanctify yourselves against to morrow: for thus saith the LORD God of Israel, There is an accursed thing in the midst of thee, O Israel: thou canst not stand before thine enemies, until ye take away the accursed thing from among you.

**Joshua 7:19** — `אֱלֹהֵ֥י` (god(s))

**Joshua 7:20** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> And Achan answered Joshua, and said, Indeed I have sinned against the LORD God of Israel, and thus and thus have I done:

> Then ye shall rise up from the ambush, and seize upon the city: for the LORD your God will deliver it into your hand.

> Then Joshua built an altar unto the LORD God of Israel in mount Ebal,

> But all the princes said unto all the congregation, We have sworn unto them by the LORD God of Israel: now therefore we may not touch them.

> Now therefore ye are cursed, and there shall none of you be freed from being bondmen, and hewers of wood and drawers of water for the house of my God.

> And they answered Joshua, and said, Because it was certainly told thy servants, how that the LORD thy God commanded his servant Moses to give you all the land, and to destroy all the inhabitants of the land from before you, therefore we were sore afraid of our lives because of you, and have done this thing.

### 2 Chronicles (167 verses)

**2 Chronicles 1:1** — `אֱלֹהָיו֙` (god(s))
> And Solomon the son of David was strengthened in his kingdom, and the LORD his God was with him, and magnified him exceedingly.

**2 Chronicles 1:3** — `אֱלֹהִ֔ים` (god(s))
> So Solomon, and all the congregation with him, went to the high place that was at Gibeon; for there was the tabernacle of the congregation of God, which Moses the servant of the LORD had made in the wilderness.

**2 Chronicles 1:4** — `אֱלֹהִים֙` (god(s))
> But the ark of God had David brought up from Kirjathjearim to the place which David had prepared for it: for he had pitched a tent for it at Jerusalem.

**2 Chronicles 1:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> In that night did God appear unto Solomon, and said unto him, Ask what I shall give thee.

**2 Chronicles 1:8** — `אלֹהִ֔ים` (god(s))
> And Solomon said unto God, Thou hast shewed great mercy unto David my father, and hast made me to reign in his stead.

**2 Chronicles 1:9** — `אֱלֹהִ֔ים` (god(s))
> Now, O LORD God, let thy promise unto David my father be established: for thou hast made me king over a people like the dust of the earth in multitude.

**2 Chronicles 1:11** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים׀` (god(s))
> And God said to Solomon, Because this was in thine heart, and thou hast not asked riches, wealth, or honour, nor the life of thine enemies, neither yet hast asked long life; but hast asked wisdom and knowledge for thyself, that thou mayest judge my people, over whom I have made thee king:

**2 Chronicles 2:4** — `אֱלֹהָ֗י` (god(s))
> Behold, I build an house to the name of the LORD my God, to dedicate it to him, and to burn before him sweet incense, and for the continual shewbread, and for the burnt offerings morning and evening, on the sabbaths, and on the new moons, and on the solemn feasts of the LORD our God. This is an ordinance for ever to Israel.

**2 Chronicles 2:5** — `אֱלֹהֵ֖ינוּ` (god(s))
> And the house which I build is great: for great is our God above all gods.

**2 Chronicles 2:12** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> Huram said moreover, Blessed be the LORD God of Israel, that made heaven and earth, who hath given to David the king a wise son, endued with prudence and understanding, that might build an house for the LORD, and an house for his kingdom.

**2 Chronicles 3:3** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Now these are the things wherein Solomon was instructed for the building of the house of God. The length by cubits after the first measure was threescore cubits, and the breadth twenty cubits.

**2 Chronicles 4:11** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> And Huram made the pots, and the shovels, and the basons. And Huram finished the work that he was to make for king Solomon for the house of God;

**2 Chronicles 4:19** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> And Solomon made all the vessels that were for the house of God, the golden altar also, and the tables whereon the shewbread was set;

> Thus all the work that Solomon made for the house of the LORD was finished: and Solomon brought in all the things that David his father had dedicated; and the silver, and the gold, and all the instruments, put he among the treasures of the house of God.

> The LORD therefore hath performed his word that he hath spoken: for I am risen up in the room of David my father, and am set on the throne of Israel, as the LORD promised, and have built the house for the name of the LORD God of Israel.

**2 Chronicles 6:14** — `אֱלֹהֵ֤י` (god(s))

> Now therefore, O LORD God of Israel, keep with thy servant David my father that which thou hast promised him, saying, There shall not fail thee a man in my sight to sit upon the throne of Israel; yet so that thy children take heed to their way to walk in my law, as thou hast walked before me.

**2 Chronicles 6:17** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))

> And now for a little space grace hath been shewed from the LORD our God, to leave us a remnant to escape, and to give us a nail in his holy place, that our God may lighten our eyes, and give us a little reviving in our bondage.

**Ezra 9:10** — `אֱלֹהֵ֖ינוּ` (god(s))
> And now, O our God, what shall we say after this? for we have forsaken thy commandments,

**Ezra 9:13** — `אֱלֹהֵ֗ינוּ` (god(s))
> And after all that is come upon us for our evil deeds, and for our great trespass, seeing that thou our God hast punished us less than our iniquities deserve, and hast given us such deliverance as this;

> O LORD God of Israel, thou art righteous: for we remain yet escaped, as it is this day: behold, we are before thee in our trespasses: for we cannot stand before thee because of this.

And the king granted me, according to the good hand of my God upon me.

> Stir up thyself, and awake to my judgment, even unto my cause, my God and my Lord.

**Psalms 35:24** — `אֱלֹהָ֗י` (god(s))
> Judge me, O LORD my God, according to thy righteousness; and let them not rejoice over me.

**Psalms 36:1** — `אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David the servant of the LORD. The transgression of the wicked saith within my heart, that there is no fear of God before his eyes.

**Psalms 36:7** — `אֱלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> How excellent is thy lovingkindness, O God! therefore the children of men put their trust under the shadow of thy wings.

**Psalms 37:31** — `אֱלֹהָ֣יו` (god(s))
> The law of his God is in his heart; none of his steps shall slide.

**Psalms 38:15** — `אֱלֹהָֽי׃` (god(s))
> For in thee, O LORD, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.

**Psalms 38:21** — `אֱ֝לֹהַ֗י` (god(s))
> Forsake me not, O LORD: O my God, be not far from me.

**Psalms 40:3** — `אלֹ֫הֵ֥ינוּ` (god(s))
> And he hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the LORD.

**Psalms 40:5** — `אֱלֹהַי֮` (god(s))
> Many, O LORD my God, are thy wonderful works which thou hast done, and thy thoughts which are to us-ward: they cannot be reckoned up in order unto thee: if I would declare and speak of them, they are more than can be numbered.

**Psalms 40:8** — `אֱלֹהַ֣י` (god(s))
> I delight to do thy will, O my God: yea, thy law is within my heart.

**Psalms 40:17** — `אֱ֝לֹהַ֗י` (god(s))
> But I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinketh upon me: thou art my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.

**Psalms 41:13** — `אֱלֹ֘הֵ֤י` (god(s))
> Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting, and to everlasting. Amen, and Amen.

**Psalms 42:1** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, Maschil, for the sons of Korah. As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.

**Psalms 42:2** — `אלֹהִים֮` (god(s))
> My soul thirsteth for God, for the living God: when shall I come and appear before God?

**Psalms 42:3** — `אֱלֹהֶֽיךָ׃` (god(s))
> My tears have been my meat day and night, while they continually say unto me, Where is thy God?

**Psalms 42:4** — `אֱלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> When I remember these things, I pour out my soul in me: for I had gone with the multitude, I went with them to the house of God, with the voice of joy and praise, with a multitude that kept holyday.

**Psalms 42:5** — `אלֹהִים` (god(s))
> Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted in me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him for the help of his countenance.

**Psalms 42:6** — `אֱֽלֹהַ֗י` (god(s))
> O my God, my soul is cast down within me: therefore will I remember thee from the land of Jordan, and of the Hermonites, from the hill Mizar.

**Psalms 42:10** — `אֱלֹהֶֽיךָ׃` (god(s))
> As with a sword in my bones, mine enemies reproach me; while they say daily unto me, Where is thy God?

**Psalms 42:11** — `אלֹהִים` (god(s))
> Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countenance, and my God.

**Psalms 43:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֨ים׀` (god(s))
> Judge me, O God, and plead my cause against an ungodly nation: O deliver me from the deceitful and unjust man.

**Psalms 43:2** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> For thou art the God of my strength: why dost thou cast me off? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?

**Psalms 43:4** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> Then will I go unto the altar of God, unto God my exceeding joy: yea, upon the harp will I praise thee, O God my God.

**Psalms 43:5** — `אלֹהִים` (god(s))
> Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope in God: for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countenance, and my God.

**Psalms 44:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֤ים׀` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician for the sons of Korah, Maschil. We have heard with our ears, O God, our fathers have told us, what work thou didst in their days, in the times of old.

**Psalms 44:4** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Thou art my King, O God: command deliverances for Jacob.

**Psalms 44:8** — `אלֹהִים` (god(s))
> In God we boast all the day long, and praise thy name for ever. Selah.

**Psalms 44:20** — `אֱלֹהֵ֑ינוּ` (god(s))
> If we have forgotten the name of our God, or stretched out our hands to a strange god;

**Psalms 44:21** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Shall not God search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.

**Psalms 45:2** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Thou art fairer than the children of men: grace is poured into thy lips: therefore God hath blessed thee for ever.

**Psalms 45:6** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever: the sceptre of thy kingdom is a right sceptre.

**Psalms 45:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Thou lovest righteousness, and hatest wickedness: therefore God, thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.

**Psalms 46:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician for the sons of Korah, A Song upon Alamoth. God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.

**Psalms 46:4** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> There is a river, the streams whereof shall make glad the city of God, the holy place of the tabernacles of the most High.

**Psalms 46:5** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> God is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved: God shall help her, and that right early.

**Psalms 46:7** — `אֱלֹהֵ֖י` (god(s))
> The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

**Psalms 46:10** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Be still, and know that I am God: I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.

**Psalms 46:11** — `אֱלֹהֵ֖י` (god(s))
> The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

**Psalms 47:1** — `אלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. O clap your hands, all ye people; shout unto God with the voice of triumph.

**Psalms 47:5** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> God is gone up with a shout, the LORD with the sound of a trumpet.

**Psalms 47:6** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Sing praises to God, sing praises: sing praises unto our King, sing praises.

**Psalms 47:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> For God is the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding.

**Psalms 47:8** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> God reigneth over the heathen: God sitteth upon the throne of his holiness.

**Psalms 47:9** — `אֱלֹהֵ֪י` (god(s))
> The princes of the people are gathered together, even the people of the God of Abraham: for the shields of the earth belong unto God: he is greatly exalted.

**Psalms 48:1** — `אֱ֝לֹהֵ֗ינוּ` (god(s))
> A Song and Psalm for the sons of Korah. Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, in the mountain of his holiness.

**Psalms 48:3** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> God is known in her palaces for a refuge.

**Psalms 48:8** — `אֱלֹהֵ֑ינוּ` (god(s))
> As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God: God will establish it for ever. Selah.

**Psalms 48:9** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> We have thought of thy lovingkindness, O God, in the midst of thy temple.

**Psalms 48:10** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> According to thy name, O God, so is thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy right hand is full of righteousness.

**Psalms 48:14** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> For this God is our God for ever and ever: he will be our guide even unto death.

**Psalms 49:7** — `אלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> None of them can by any means redeem his brother, nor give to God a ransom for him:

**Psalms 49:15** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> But God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave: for he shall receive me. Selah.

**Psalms 50:1** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֡ים` (god(s))
> A Psalm of Asaph. The mighty God, even the LORD, hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof.

**Psalms 50:2** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God hath shined.

**Psalms 50:3** — `אֱלֹהֵ֗ינוּ` (god(s))
> Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about him.

**Psalms 50:6** — `אֱלֹהִ֓ים׀` (god(s))
> And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God is judge himself. Selah.

**Psalms 50:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> Hear, O my people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify against thee: I am God, even thy God.

**Psalms 50:14** — `אלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Offer unto God thanksgiving; and pay thy vows unto the most High:

**Psalms 50:16** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> But unto the wicked God saith, What hast thou to do to declare my statutes, or that thou shouldest take my covenant in thy mouth?

**Psalms 50:23** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me: and to him that ordereth his conversation aright will I shew the salvation of God.

**Psalms 51:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David, when Nathan the prophet came unto him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba. Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness: according unto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.

**Psalms 51:10** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.

**Psalms 51:14** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, thou God of my salvation: and my tongue shall sing aloud of thy righteousness.

**Psalms 51:17** — `אֱלֹהִים֮` (god(s))
> The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise.

**Psalms 52:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> Lo, this is the man that made not God his strength; but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.

**Psalms 52:8** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God: I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.

**Psalms 53:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician upon Mahalath, Maschil, A Psalm of David. The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God. Corrupt are they, and have done abominable iniquity: there is none that doeth good.

**Psalms 53:2** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, that did seek God.

**Psalms 53:4** — `אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people as they eat bread: they have not called upon God.

**Psalms 53:5** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> There were they in great fear, where no fear was: for God hath scattered the bones of him that encampeth against thee: thou hast put them to shame, because God hath despised them.

**Psalms 53:6** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! When God bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

**Psalms 54:1** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician on Neginoth, Maschil, A Psalm of David, when the Ziphims came and said to Saul, Doth not David hide himself with us? Save me, O God, by thy name, and judge me by thy strength.

**Psalms 54:2** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Hear my prayer, O God; give ear to the words of my mouth.

**Psalms 54:3** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> For strangers are risen up against me, and oppressors seek after my soul: they have not set God before them. Selah.

**Psalms 54:4** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Behold, God is mine helper: the Lord is with them that uphold my soul.

**Psalms 55:1** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician on Neginoth, Maschil, A Psalm of David. Give ear to my prayer, O God; and hide not thyself from my supplication.

**Psalms 55:14** — `אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> We took sweet counsel together, and walked unto the house of God in company.

**Psalms 55:16** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> As for me, I will call upon God; and the LORD shall save me.

**Psalms 55:19** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> God shall hear, and afflict them, even he that abideth of old. Selah. Because they have no changes, therefore they fear not God.

**Psalms 55:23** — `אֱלֹהִ֨ים׀` (god(s))
> But thou, O God, shalt bring them down into the pit of destruction: bloody and deceitful men shall not live out half their days; but I will trust in thee.

**Psalms 56:1** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician upon Jonathelemrechokim, Michtam of David, when the Philistines took him in Gath. Be merciful unto me, O God: for man would swallow me up; he fighting daily oppresseth me.

**Psalms 56:4** — `אלֹהִים֮` (god(s))
> In God I will praise his word, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what flesh can do unto me.

**Psalms 56:7** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> Shall they escape by iniquity? in thine anger cast down the people, O God.

**Psalms 56:9** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> When I cry unto thee, then shall mine enemies turn back: this I know; for God is for me.

**Psalms 56:10** — `אלֹהִים` (god(s))
> In God will I praise his word: in the LORD will I praise his word.

**Psalms 56:11** — `אלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> In God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.

**Psalms 56:12** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Thy vows are upon me, O God: I will render praises unto thee.

**Psalms 56:13** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> For thou hast delivered my soul from death: wilt not thou deliver my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living?

**Psalms 57:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֨ים׀` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, Altaschith, Michtam of David, when he fled from Saul in the cave. Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me: for my soul trusteth in thee: yea, in the shadow of thy wings will I make my refuge, until these calamities be overpast.

**Psalms 57:2** — `אלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> I will cry unto God most high; unto God that performeth all things for me.

**Psalms 57:3** — `אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> He shall send from heaven, and save me from the reproach of him that would swallow me up. Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.

**Psalms 57:5** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens; let thy glory be above all the earth.

**Psalms 57:7** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed: I will sing and give praise.

**Psalms 57:11** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: let thy glory be above all the earth.

**Psalms 58:6** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> Break their teeth, O God, in their mouth: break out the great teeth of the young lions, O LORD.

**Psalms 58:11** — `אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> So that a man shall say, Verily there is a reward for the righteous: verily he is a God that judgeth in the earth.

**Psalms 59:1** — `אֱלֹהָ֑י` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, Altaschith, Michtam of David; when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill him. Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.

**Psalms 59:5** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים׀` (god(s))
> Thou therefore, O LORD God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.

**Psalms 59:9** — `אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> Because of his strength will I wait upon thee: for God is my defence.

**Psalms 59:10** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> The God of my mercy shall prevent me: God shall let me see my desire upon mine enemies.

**Psalms 59:13** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Consume them in wrath, consume them, that they may not be: and let them know that God ruleth in Jacob unto the ends of the earth. Selah.

**Psalms 59:17** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing: for God is my defence, and the God of my mercy.

**Psalms 60:1** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician upon Shushaneduth, Michtam of David, to teach; when he strove with Aramnaharaim and with Aramzobah, when Joab returned, and smote of Edom in the valley of salt twelve thousand. O God, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; O turn thyself to us again.

**Psalms 60:6** — `אֱלֹהִ֤ים׀` (god(s))
> God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.

**Psalms 60:10** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Wilt not thou, O God, which hadst cast us off? and thou, O God, which didst not go out with our armies?

**Psalms 60:12** — `אלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Through God we shall do valiantly: for he it is that shall tread down our enemies.

**Psalms 61:1** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician upon Neginah, A Psalm of David. Hear my cry, O God; attend unto my prayer.

**Psalms 61:5** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> For thou, O God, hast heard my vows: thou hast given me the heritage of those that fear thy name.

**Psalms 61:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> He shall abide before God for ever: O prepare mercy and truth, which may preserve him.

**Psalms 62:1** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, to Jeduthun, A Psalm of David. Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him cometh my salvation.

**Psalms 62:5** — `אלֹהִים` (god(s))
> My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him.

**Psalms 62:7** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> In God is my salvation and my glory: the rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.

**Psalms 62:8** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> Trust in him at all times; ye people, pour out your heart before him: God is a refuge for us. Selah.

**Psalms 62:11** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> God hath spoken once; twice have I heard this; that power belongeth unto God.

**Psalms 63:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֤ים׀` (god(s))
> A Psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah. O God, thou art my God; early will I seek thee: my soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for thee in a dry and thirsty land, where no water is;

**Psalms 63:11** — `אלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> But the king shall rejoice in God; every one that sweareth by him shall glory: but the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.

**Psalms 64:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. Hear my voice, O God, in my prayer: preserve my life from fear of the enemy.

**Psalms 64:7** — `אֱלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> But God shall shoot at them with an arrow; suddenly shall they be wounded.

**Psalms 64:9** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> And all men shall fear, and shall declare the work of God; for they shall wisely consider of his doing.

**Psalms 65:1** — `אֱלֹ֘הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm and Song of David. Praise waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion: and unto thee shall the vow be performed.

**Psalms 65:5** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> By terrible things in righteousness wilt thou answer us, O God of our salvation; who art the confidence of all the ends of the earth, and of them that are afar off upon the sea:

**Psalms 65:9** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Thou visitest the earth, and waterest it: thou greatly enrichest it with the river of God, which is full of water: thou preparest them corn, when thou hast so provided for it.

**Psalms 66:1** — `אלֹהִים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, A Song or Psalm. Make a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands:

**Psalms 66:3** — `אלֹהִים` (god(s))
> Say unto God, How terrible art thou in thy works! through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies submit themselves unto thee.

**Psalms 66:5** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Come and see the works of God: he is terrible in his doing toward the children of men.

**Psalms 66:8** — `אֱלֹהֵ֑ינוּ` (god(s))
> O bless our God, ye people, and make the voice of his praise to be heard:

**Psalms 66:10** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> For thou, O God, hast proved us: thou hast tried us, as silver is tried.

**Psalms 66:16** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Come and hear, all ye that fear God, and I will declare what he hath done for my soul.

**Psalms 66:19** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> But verily God hath heard me; he hath attended to the voice of my prayer.

**Psalms 66:20** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Blessed be God, which hath not turned away my prayer, nor his mercy from me.

**Psalms 67:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. God be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine upon us; Selah.

**Psalms 67:3** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.

**Psalms 67:5** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.

**Psalms 67:6** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Then shall the earth yield her increase; and God, even our own God, shall bless us.

**Psalms 67:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> God shall bless us; and all the ends of the earth shall fear him.

**Psalms 68:1** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm or Song of David. Let God arise, let his enemies be scattered: let them also that hate him flee before him.

**Psalms 68:2** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> As smoke is driven away, so drive them away: as wax melteth before the fire, so let the wicked perish at the presence of God.

**Psalms 68:3** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> But let the righteous be glad; let them rejoice before God: yea, let them exceedingly rejoice.

**Psalms 68:4** — `אלֹהִים֮` (god(s))
> Sing unto God, sing praises to his name: extol him that rideth upon the heavens by his name JAH, and rejoice before him.

**Psalms 68:5** — `אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> A father of the fatherless, and a judge of the widows, is God in his holy habitation.

**Psalms 68:6** — `אֱלֹהִ֤ים׀` (god(s))
> God setteth the solitary in families: he bringeth out those which are bound with chains: but the rebellious dwell in a dry land.

**Psalms 68:7** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> O God, when thou wentest forth before thy people, when thou didst march through the wilderness; Selah:

**Psalms 68:8** — `אֱלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> The earth shook, the heavens also dropped at the presence of God: even Sinai itself was moved at the presence of God, the God of Israel.

**Psalms 68:9** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Thou, O God, didst send a plentiful rain, whereby thou didst confirm thine inheritance, when it was weary.

**Psalms 68:10** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> Thy congregation hath dwelt therein: thou, O God, hast prepared of thy goodness for the poor.

**Psalms 68:15** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> The hill of God is as the hill of Bashan; an high hill as the hill of Bashan.

**Psalms 68:16** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Why leap ye, ye high hills? this is the hill which God desireth to dwell in; yea, the LORD will dwell in it for ever.

**Psalms 68:17** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> The chariots of God are twenty thousand, even thousands of angels: the Lord is among them, as in Sinai, in the holy place.

**Psalms 68:18** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> Thou hast ascended on high, thou hast led captivity captive: thou hast received gifts for men; yea, for the rebellious also, that the LORD God might dwell among them.

**Psalms 68:21** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> But God shall wound the head of his enemies, and the hairy scalp of such an one as goeth on still in his trespasses.

**Psalms 68:24** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> They have seen thy goings, O God; even the goings of my God, my King, in the sanctuary.

**Psalms 68:26** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Bless ye God in the congregations, even the Lord, from the fountain of Israel.

**Psalms 68:28** — `אֱלֹהֶ֗יךָ` (god(s))
> Thy God hath commanded thy strength: strengthen, O God, that which thou hast wrought for us.

**Psalms 68:31** — `אלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> Princes shall come out of Egypt; Ethiopia shall soon stretch out her hands unto God.

**Psalms 68:32** — `אלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Sing unto God, ye kingdoms of the earth; O sing praises unto the Lord; Selah:

**Psalms 68:34** — `אלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Ascribe ye strength unto God: his excellency is over Israel, and his strength is in the clouds.

**Psalms 68:35** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> O God, thou art terrible out of thy holy places: the God of Israel is he that giveth strength and power unto his people. Blessed be God.

**Psalms 69:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician upon Shoshannim, A Psalm of David. Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto my soul.

**Psalms 69:3** — `אלֹהָֽי׃` (god(s))
> I am weary of my crying: my throat is dried: mine eyes fail while I wait for my God.

**Psalms 69:5** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> O God, thou knowest my foolishness; and my sins are not hid from thee.

**Psalms 69:6** — `אֱ֝לֹהֵ֗י` (god(s))
> Let not them that wait on thee, O Lord GOD of hosts, be ashamed for my sake: let not those that seek thee be confounded for my sake, O God of Israel.

**Psalms 69:13** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> But as for me, my prayer is unto thee, O LORD, in an acceptable time: O God, in the multitude of thy mercy hear me, in the truth of thy salvation.

**Psalms 69:29** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> But I am poor and sorrowful: let thy salvation, O God, set me up on high.

**Psalms 69:30** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.

**Psalms 69:32** — `אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> The humble shall see this, and be glad: and your heart shall live that seek God.

**Psalms 69:35** — `אֱלֹהִ֨ים׀` (god(s))
> For God will save Zion, and will build the cities of Judah: that they may dwell there, and have it in possession.

**Psalms 70:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David, to bring to remembrance. Make haste, O God, to deliver me; make haste to help me, O LORD.

**Psalms 70:4** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: and let such as love thy salvation say continually, Let God be magnified.

**Psalms 70:5** — `אֱלֹהִ֪ים` (god(s))
> But I am poor and needy: make haste unto me, O God: thou art my help and my deliverer; O LORD, make no tarrying.

**Psalms 71:4** — `אֱֽלֹהַ֗י` (god(s))
> Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.

**Psalms 71:11** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Saying, God hath forsaken him: persecute and take him; for there is none to deliver him.

**Psalms 71:12** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> O God, be not far from me: O my God, make haste for my help.

**Psalms 71:17** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> O God, thou hast taught me from my youth: and hitherto have I declared thy wondrous works.

**Psalms 71:18** — `אֱלֹהִ֪ים` (god(s))
> Now also when I am old and grayheaded, O God, forsake me not; until I have shewed thy strength unto this generation, and thy power to every one that is to come.

**Psalms 71:19** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> Thy righteousness also, O God, is very high, who hast done great things: O God, who is like unto thee!

**Psalms 71:22** — `אֱלֹ֫הָ֥י` (god(s))
> I will also praise thee with the psaltery, even thy truth, O my God: unto thee will I sing with the harp, O thou Holy One of Israel.

**Psalms 72:1** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> A Psalm for Solomon. Give the king thy judgments, O God, and thy righteousness unto the king's son.

**Psalms 72:18** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel, who only doeth wondrous things.

**Psalms 73:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> A Psalm of Asaph. Truly God is good to Israel, even to such as are of a clean heart.

**Psalms 73:26** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.

**Psalms 73:28** — `אֱלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> But it is good for me to draw near to God: I have put my trust in the Lord GOD, that I may declare all thy works.

**Psalms 74:1** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Maschil of Asaph. O God, why hast thou cast us off for ever? why doth thine anger smoke against the sheep of thy pasture?

**Psalms 74:10** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> O God, how long shall the adversary reproach? shall the enemy blaspheme thy name for ever?

**Psalms 74:12** — `אלֹהִים` (god(s))
> For God is my King of old, working salvation in the midst of the earth.

**Psalms 74:22** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Arise, O God, plead thine own cause: remember how the foolish man reproacheth thee daily.

**Psalms 75:1** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, Altaschith, A Psalm or Song of Asaph. Unto thee, O God, do we give thanks, unto thee do we give thanks: for that thy name is near thy wondrous works declare.

**Psalms 75:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> But God is the judge: he putteth down one, and setteth up another.

**Psalms 75:9** — `אלֹהֵ֥י` (god(s))
> But I will declare for ever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.

**Psalms 76:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song of Asaph. In Judah is God known: his name is great in Israel.

**Psalms 76:6** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> At thy rebuke, O God of Jacob, both the chariot and horse are cast into a dead sleep.

**Psalms 76:9** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> When God arose to judgment, to save all the meek of the earth. Selah.

**Psalms 76:11** — `אֱֽלֹהֵ֫יכֶ֥ם` (god(s))
> Vow, and pay unto the LORD your God: let all that be round about him bring presents unto him that ought to be feared.

**Psalms 77:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, to Jeduthun, A Psalm of Asaph. I cried unto God with my voice, even unto God with my voice; and he gave ear unto me.

**Psalms 77:3** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> I remembered God, and was troubled: I complained, and my spirit was overwhelmed. Selah.

**Psalms 77:13** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Thy way, O God, is in the sanctuary: who is so great a God as our God?

**Psalms 77:16** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> The waters saw thee, O God, the waters saw thee; they were afraid: the depths also were troubled.

**Psalms 78:7** — `אלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> That they might set their hope in God, and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments:

**Psalms 78:10** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> They kept not the covenant of God, and refused to walk in his law;

**Psalms 78:19** — `אלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Yea, they spake against God; they said, Can God furnish a table in the wilderness?

**Psalms 78:22** — `אלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Because they believed not in God, and trusted not in his salvation:

**Psalms 78:31** — `אֱלֹהִ֨ים׀` (god(s))
> The wrath of God came upon them, and slew the fattest of them, and smote down the chosen men of Israel.

**Psalms 78:35** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> And they remembered that God was their rock, and the high God their redeemer.

**Psalms 78:56** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Yet they tempted and provoked the most high God, and kept not his testimonies:

**Psalms 78:59** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> When God heard this, he was wroth, and greatly abhorred Israel:

**Psalms 79:1** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֡ים` (god(s))
> A Psalm of Asaph. O God, the heathen are come into thine inheritance; thy holy temple have they defiled; they have laid Jerusalem on heaps.

**Psalms 79:9** — `אֱלֹ֘הֵ֤י` (god(s))
> Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of thy name: and deliver us, and purge away our sins, for thy name's sake.

**Psalms 79:10** — `אֱֽלֹהֵ֫יהֶ֥ם` (god(s))
> Wherefore should the heathen say, Where is their God? let him be known among the heathen in our sight by the revenging of the blood of thy servants which is shed.

**Psalms 80:3** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Turn us again, O God, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

**Psalms 80:4** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> O LORD God of hosts, how long wilt thou be angry against the prayer of thy people?

**Psalms 80:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Turn us again, O God of hosts, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

**Psalms 80:14** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Return, we beseech thee, O God of hosts: look down from heaven, and behold, and visit this vine;

**Psalms 80:19** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Turn us again, O LORD God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

**Psalms 81:1** — `אלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician upon Gittith, A Psalm of Asaph. Sing aloud unto God our strength: make a joyful noise unto the God of Jacob.

**Psalms 81:4** — `אלֹהֵ֥י` (god(s))
> For this was a statute for Israel, and a law of the God of Jacob.

**Psalms 81:10** — `אֱלֹהֶ֗יךָ` (god(s))
> I am the LORD thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Egypt: open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.

**Psalms 82:1** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> A Psalm of Asaph. God standeth in the congregation of the mighty; he judgeth among the gods.

**Psalms 82:6** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> I have said, Ye are gods; and all of you are children of the most High.

**Psalms 82:8** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))
> Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shalt inherit all nations.

**Psalms 83:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> A Song or Psalm of Asaph. Keep not thou silence, O God: hold not thy peace, and be not still, O God.

**Psalms 83:12** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> Who said, Let us take to ourselves the houses of God in possession.

**Psalms 83:13** — `אֱֽלֹהַ֗י` (god(s))
> O my God, make them like a wheel; as the stubble before the wind.

**Psalms 84:3** — `אלֹהָֽי׃` (god(s))
> Yea, the sparrow hath found an house, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, even thine altars, O LORD of hosts, my King, and my God.

**Psalms 84:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> They go from strength to strength, every one of them in Zion appeareth before God.

**Psalms 84:8** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer: give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.

**Psalms 84:9** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Behold, O God our shield, and look upon the face of thine anointed.

**Psalms 84:10** — `אֱלֹהַ֑י` (god(s))
> For a day in thy courts is better than a thousand. I had rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.

**Psalms 84:11** — `אֱלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> For the LORD God is a sun and shield: the LORD will give grace and glory: no good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly.

**Psalms 85:4** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> Turn us, O God of our salvation, and cause thine anger toward us to cease.

**Psalms 86:2** — `אֱלֹהַ֑י` (god(s))
> Preserve my soul; for I am holy: O thou my God, save thy servant that trusteth in thee.

**Psalms 86:8** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים׀` (god(s))
> Among the gods there is none like unto thee, O Lord; neither are there any works like unto thy works.

**Psalms 86:10** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> For thou art great, and doest wondrous things: thou art God alone.

**Psalms 86:12** — `אֱ֭לֹהַי` (god(s))
> I will praise thee, O Lord my God, with all my heart: and I will glorify thy name for evermore.

**Psalms 86:14** — `אֱלֹהִ֤ים׀` (god(s))
> O God, the proud are risen against me, and the assemblies of violent men have sought after my soul; and have not set thee before them.

**Psalms 87:3** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> Glorious things are spoken of thee, O city of God. Selah.

**Psalms 88:1** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> A Song or Psalm for the sons of Korah, to the chief Musician upon Mahalath Leannoth, Maschil of Heman the Ezrahite. O LORD God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before thee:

**Psalms 89:8** — `אֱלֹ֘הֵ֤י` (god(s))
> O LORD God of hosts, who is a strong LORD like unto thee? or to thy faithfulness round about thee?

**Psalms 90:1** — `אֱלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> A Prayer of Moses the man of God. Lord, thou hast been our dwelling place in all generations.

**Psalms 90:17** — `אֱלֹהֵ֗ינוּ` (god(s))
> And let the beauty of the LORD our God be upon us: and establish thou the work of our hands upon us; yea, the work of our hands establish thou it.

**Psalms 91:2** — `אֱ֝לֹהַ֗י` (god(s))
> I will say of the LORD, He is my refuge and my fortress: my God; in him will I trust.

**Psalms 92:13** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣ינוּ` (god(s))
> Those that be planted in the house of the LORD shall flourish in the courts of our God.

**Psalms 94:7** — `אֱלֹהֵ֥י` (god(s))
> Yet they say, The LORD shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob regard it.

**Psalms 94:22** — `אלֹהַ֗י` (god(s))
> But the LORD is my defence; and my God is the rock of my refuge.

**Psalms 94:23** — `אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ׃` (god(s))
> And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity, and shall cut them off in their own wickedness; yea, the LORD our God shall cut them off.

**Psalms 95:3** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> For the LORD is a great God, and a great King above all gods.

**Psalms 95:7** — `אֱלֹהֵ֗ינוּ` (god(s))
> For he is our God; and we are the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand. To day if ye will hear his voice,

**Psalms 96:4** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> For the LORD is great, and greatly to be praised: he is to be feared above all gods.

**Psalms 96:5** — `אֱלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> For all the gods of the nations are idols: but the LORD made the heavens.

**Psalms 97:7** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> Confounded be all they that serve graven images, that boast themselves of idols: worship him, all ye gods.

**Psalms 97:9** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> For thou, LORD, art high above all the earth: thou art exalted far above all gods.

**Psalms 98:3** — `אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ׃` (god(s))
> He hath remembered his mercy and his truth toward the house of Israel: all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.

**Psalms 99:5** — `אֱלֹהֵ֗ינוּ` (god(s))
> Exalt ye the LORD our God, and worship at his footstool; for he is holy.

**Psalms 99:8** — `אֱלֹהֵינוּ֮` (god(s))
> Thou answeredst them, O LORD our God: thou wast a God that forgavest them, though thou tookest vengeance of their inventions.

**Psalms 99:9** — `אֱלֹהֵ֗ינוּ` (god(s))
> Exalt the LORD our God, and worship at his holy hill; for the LORD our God is holy.

**Psalms 100:3** — `אֱלֹ֫הִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Know ye that the LORD he is God: it is he that hath made us, and not we ourselves; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.

**Psalms 104:1** — `אֱ֭לֹהַי` (god(s))
> Bless the LORD, O my soul. O LORD my God, thou art very great; thou art clothed with honour and majesty.

**Psalms 104:33** — `אלֹהַ֣י` (god(s))
> I will sing unto the LORD as long as I live: I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.

**Psalms 105:7** — `אֱלֹהֵ֑ינוּ` (god(s))
> He is the LORD our God: his judgments are in all the earth.

**Psalms 106:47** — `אֱלֹהֵ֗ינוּ` (god(s))
> Save us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the heathen, to give thanks unto thy holy name, and to triumph in thy praise.

**Psalms 106:48** — `אֱלֹהֵ֪י` (god(s))
> Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting to everlasting: and let all the people say, Amen. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 108:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> A Song or Psalm of David. O God, my heart is fixed; I will sing and give praise, even with my glory.

**Psalms 108:5** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: and thy glory above all the earth;

**Psalms 108:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֤ים׀` (god(s))
> God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.

**Psalms 108:11** — `אֱלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Wilt not thou, O God, who hast cast us off? and wilt not thou, O God, go forth with our hosts?

**Psalms 108:13** — `אלֹהִ֥ים` (god(s))
> Through God we shall do valiantly: for he it is that shall tread down our enemies.

**Psalms 109:1** — `אֱלֹהֵ֥י` (god(s))
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise;

**Psalms 109:26** — `אֱלֹהָ֑י` (god(s))
> Help me, O LORD my God: O save me according to thy mercy:

**Psalms 113:5** — `אֱלֹהֵ֑ינוּ` (god(s))
> Who is like unto the LORD our God, who dwelleth on high,

**Psalms 115:2** — `אֱלֹהֵיהֶֽם׃` (god(s))
> Wherefore should the heathen say, Where is now their God?

**Psalms 115:3** — `אלֹהֵ֥ינוּ` (god(s))
> But our God is in the heavens: he hath done whatsoever he hath pleased.

**Psalms 116:5** — `אלֹהֵ֣ינוּ` (god(s))
> Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; yea, our God is merciful.

**Psalms 118:28** — `אֱ֝לֹהַ֗י` (god(s))
> Thou art my God, and I will praise thee: thou art my God, I will exalt thee.

**Psalms 119:115** — `אֱלֹהָֽי׃` (god(s))
> Depart from me, ye evildoers: for I will keep the commandments of my God.

**Psalms 122:9** — `אֱלֹהֵ֑ינוּ` (god(s))
> Because of the house of the LORD our God I will seek thy good.

**Psalms 123:2** — `אֱלֹהֵ֑ינוּ` (god(s))
> Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hand of their masters, and as the eyes of a maiden unto the hand of her mistress; so our eyes wait upon the LORD our God, until that he have mercy upon us.

**Psalms 135:2** — `אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ׃` (god(s))
> Ye that stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God,

**Psalms 135:5** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> For I know that the LORD is great, and that our Lord is above all gods.

**Psalms 136:2** — `אלֹהֵ֣י` (god(s))
> O give thanks unto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 138:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֣ים` (god(s))
> A Psalm of David. I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise unto thee.

**Psalms 144:9** — `אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> I will sing a new song unto thee, O God: upon a psaltery and an instrument of ten strings will I sing praises unto thee.

**Psalms 144:15** — `אֱלֹהָֽיו׃` (god(s))
> Happy is that people, that is in such a case: yea, happy is that people, whose God is the LORD.

**Psalms 146:2** — `אלֹהַ֣י` (god(s))
> While I live will I praise the LORD: I will sing praises unto my God while I have any being.

**Psalms 146:5** — `אֱלֹהָֽיו׃` (god(s))
> Happy is he that hath the God of Jacob for his help, whose hope is in the LORD his God:

**Psalms 146:10** — `אֱלֹהַ֣יִךְ` (god(s))
> The LORD shall reign for ever, even thy God, O Zion, unto all generations. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 147:1** — `אֱלֹהֵ֑ינוּ` (god(s))
> Praise ye the LORD: for it is good to sing praises unto our God; for it is pleasant; and praise is comely.

**Psalms 147:7** — `אלֹהֵ֣ינוּ` (god(s))
> Sing unto the LORD with thanksgiving; sing praise upon the harp unto our God:

**Psalms 147:12** — `אֱלֹהַ֣יִךְ` (god(s))

**Proverbs 2:17** — `אֱלֹהֶ֣יהָ` (god(s))

**Proverbs 25:2** — `אֱ֭לֹהִים` (god(s))

### Ecclesiastes (36 verses)

**Ecclesiastes 1:13** — `אֱלֹהִ֛ים` (god(s))
> And I gave my heart to seek and search out by wisdom concerning all things that are done under heaven: this sore travail hath God given to the sons of man to be exercised therewith.

**Ecclesiastes 2:24** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> There is nothing better for a man, than that he should eat and drink, and that he should make his soul enjoy good in his labour. This also I saw, that it was from the hand of God.

**Ecclesiastes 2:26** — `אֱלֹהִ֔ים` (god(s))
> For God giveth to a man that is good in his sight wisdom, and knowledge, and joy: but to the sinner he giveth travail, to gather and to heap up, that he may give to him that is good before God. This also is vanity and vexation of spirit.

**Ecclesiastes 3:10** — `אֱלֹהִ֛ים` (god(s))
> I have seen the travail, which God hath given to the sons of men to be exercised in it.

**Ecclesiastes 3:11** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> He hath made every thing beautiful in his time: also he hath set the world in their heart, so that no man can find out the work that God maketh from the beginning to the end.

**Ecclesiastes 3:13** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> And also that every man should eat and drink, and enjoy the good of all his labour, it is the gift of God.

**Ecclesiastes 3:14** — `אֱלֹהִים֙` (god(s))
> I know that, whatsoever God doeth, it shall be for ever: nothing can be put to it, nor any thing taken from it: and God doeth it, that men should fear before him.

**Ecclesiastes 3:15** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> That which hath been is now; and that which is to be hath already been; and God requireth that which is past.

**Ecclesiastes 3:17** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> I said in mine heart, God shall judge the righteous and the wicked: for there is a time there for every purpose and for every work.

**Ecclesiastes 3:18** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> I said in mine heart concerning the estate of the sons of men, that God might manifest them, and that they might see that they themselves are beasts.

**Ecclesiastes 5:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֔ים` (god(s))
> Keep thy foot when thou goest to the house of God, and be more ready to hear, than to give the sacrifice of fools: for they consider not that they do evil.

**Ecclesiastes 5:2** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Be not rash with thy mouth, and let not thine heart be hasty to utter any thing before God: for God is in heaven, and thou upon earth: therefore let thy words be few.

**Ecclesiastes 5:4** — `אלֹהִ֗ים` (god(s))
> When thou vowest a vow unto God, defer not to pay it; for he hath no pleasure in fools: pay that which thou hast vowed.

**Ecclesiastes 5:6** — `אֱלֹהִים֙` (god(s))
> Suffer not thy mouth to cause thy flesh to sin; neither say thou before the angel, that it was an error: wherefore should God be angry at thy voice, and destroy the work of thine hands?

**Ecclesiastes 5:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> For in the multitude of dreams and many words there are also divers vanities: but fear thou God.

**Ecclesiastes 5:18** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> Behold that which I have seen: it is good and comely for one to eat and to drink, and to enjoy the good of all his labour that he taketh under the sun all the days of his life, which God giveth him: for it is his portion.

**Ecclesiastes 5:19** — `אֱלֹהִים֩` (god(s))
> Every man also to whom God hath given riches and wealth, and hath given him power to eat thereof, and to take his portion, and to rejoice in his labour; this is the gift of God.

**Ecclesiastes 5:20** — `אֱלֹהִ֛ים` (god(s))
> For he shall not much remember the days of his life; because God answereth him in the joy of his heart.

**Ecclesiastes 6:2** — `אֱלֹהִ֡ים` (god(s))
> A man to whom God hath given riches, wealth, and honour, so that he wanteth nothing for his soul of all that he desireth, yet God giveth him not power to eat thereof, but a stranger eateth it: this is vanity, and it is an evil disease.

**Ecclesiastes 7:13** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> Consider the work of God: for who can make that straight, which he hath made crooked?

**Ecclesiastes 7:14** — `אֱלֹהִ֔ים` (god(s))
> In the day of prosperity be joyful, but in the day of adversity consider: God also hath set the one over against the other, to the end that man should find nothing after him.

**Ecclesiastes 7:18** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> It is good that thou shouldest take hold of this; yea, also from this withdraw not thine hand: for he that feareth God shall come forth of them all.

**Ecclesiastes 7:26** — `אֱלֹהִים֙` (god(s))
> And I find more bitter than death the woman, whose heart is snares and nets, and her hands as bands: whoso pleaseth God shall escape from her; but the sinner shall be taken by her.

**Ecclesiastes 7:29** — `אֱלֹהִ֛ים` (god(s))
> Lo, this only have I found, that God hath made man upright; but they have sought out many inventions.

**Ecclesiastes 8:2** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> I counsel thee to keep the king's commandment, and that in regard of the oath of God.

**Ecclesiastes 8:12** — `אֱלֹהִ֔ים` (god(s))
> Though a sinner do evil an hundred times, and his days be prolonged, yet surely I know that it shall be well with them that fear God, which fear before him:

**Ecclesiastes 8:13** — `אֱלֹהִֽים׃` (god(s))
> But it shall not be well with the wicked, neither shall he prolong his days, which are as a shadow; because he feareth not before God.

**Ecclesiastes 8:15** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> Then I commended mirth, because a man hath no better thing under the sun, than to eat, and to drink, and to be merry: for that shall abide with him of his labour the days of his life, which God giveth him under the sun.

**Ecclesiastes 8:17** — `אֱלֹהִים֒` (god(s))
> Then I beheld all the work of God, that a man cannot find out the work that is done under the sun: because though a man labour to seek it out, yet he shall not find it; yea further; though a wise man think to know it, yet shall he not be able to find it.

**Ecclesiastes 9:1** — `אֱלֹהִ֑ים` (god(s))
> For all this I considered in my heart even to declare all this, that the righteous, and the wise, and their works, are in the hand of God: no man knoweth either love or hatred by all that is before them.

**Ecclesiastes 9:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> Go thy way, eat thy bread with joy, and drink thy wine with a merry heart; for God now accepteth thy works.

**Ecclesiastes 11:5** — `אֱלֹהִ֔ים` (god(s))
> As thou knowest not what is the way of the spirit, nor how the bones do grow in the womb of her that is with child: even so thou knowest not the works of God who maketh all.

**Ecclesiastes 11:9** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> Rejoice, O young man, in thy youth; and let thy heart cheer thee in the days of thy youth, and walk in the ways of thine heart, and in the sight of thine eyes: but know thou, that for all these things God will bring thee into judgment.

**Ecclesiastes 12:7** — `אֱלֹהִ֖ים` (god(s))
> Then shall the dust return to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall return unto God who gave it.

**Ecclesiastes 12:13** — `אֱלֹהִ֤ים` (god(s))
> Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep his commandments: for this is the whole duty of man.

**Ecclesiastes 12:14** — `אֱלֹהִ֛ים` (god(s))
> For God shall bring every work into judgment, with every secret thing, whether it be good, or whether it be evil.

> And now, saith the LORD that formed me from the womb to be his servant, to bring Jacob again to him, Though Israel be not gathered, yet shall I be glorious in the eyes of the LORD, and my God shall be my strength.

> But I am the LORD thy God, that divided the sea, whose waves roared: The LORD of hosts is his name.

> Thus saith thy Lord the LORD, and thy God that pleadeth the cause of his people, Behold, I have taken out of thine hand the cup of trembling, even the dregs of the cup of my fury; thou shalt no more drink it again:

> For thy Maker is thine husband; the LORD of hosts is his name; and thy Redeemer the Holy One of Israel; The God of the whole earth shall he be called.

> For the LORD hath called thee as a woman forsaken and grieved in spirit, and a wife of youth, when thou wast refused, saith thy God.

**Isaiah 55:5** — `אֱלֹהֶ֔יךָ` (god(s))
> Behold, thou shalt call a nation that thou knowest not, and nations that knew not thee shall run unto thee because of the LORD thy God, and for the Holy One of Israel; for he hath glorified thee.

**Isaiah 55:7** — `אֱלֹהֵ֖ינוּ` (god(s))
> Let the wicked forsake his way, and the unrighteous man his thoughts: and let him return unto the LORD, and he will have mercy upon him; and to our God, for he will abundantly pardon.

> There is no peace, saith my God, to the wicked.

**Isaiah 59:2** — `אֱלֹֽהֵיכֶ֑ם` (god(s))

> Surely the isles shall wait for me, and the ships of Tarshish first, to bring thy sons from far, their silver and their gold with them, unto the name of the LORD thy God, and to the Holy One of Israel, because he hath glorified thee.

**Isaiah 60:19** — `אלֹהַ֖יִךְ` (god(s))
> The sun shall be no more thy light by day; neither for brightness shall the moon give light unto thee: but the LORD shall be unto thee an everlasting light, and thy God thy glory.

> To proclaim the acceptable year of the LORD, and the day of vengeance of our God; to comfort all that mourn;

> I will greatly rejoice in the LORD, my soul shall be joyful in my God; for he hath clothed me with the garments of salvation, he hath covered me with the robe of righteousness, as a bridegroom decketh himself with ornaments, and as a bride adorneth herself with her jewels.
