> He shall put on the holy linen coat, and he shall have the linen breeches upon his flesh, and shall be girded with a linen girdle, and with the linen mitre shall he be attired: these are holy garments; therefore shall he wash his flesh in water, and so put them on.

**Leviticus 16:16** — `קֹּ֗דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And he shall make an atonement for the holy place, because of the uncleanness of the children of Israel, and because of their transgressions in all their sins: and so shall he do for the tabernacle of the congregation, that remaineth among them in the midst of their uncleanness.

**Leviticus 16:17** — `קֹּ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And there shall be no man in the tabernacle of the congregation when he goeth in to make an atonement in the holy place, until he come out, and have made an atonement for himself, and for his household, and for all the congregation of Israel.

**Leviticus 16:20** — `קֹּ֔דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And when he hath made an end of reconciling the holy place, and the tabernacle of the congregation, and the altar, he shall bring the live goat:

**Leviticus 16:23** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And Aaron shall come into the tabernacle of the congregation, and shall put off the linen garments, which he put on when he went into the holy place, and shall leave them there:

**Leviticus 16:27** — `קֹּ֔דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And the bullock for the sin offering, and the goat for the sin offering, whose blood was brought in to make atonement in the holy place, shall one carry forth without the camp; and they shall burn in the fire their skins, and their flesh, and their dung.

**Leviticus 16:32** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> And the priest, whom he shall anoint, and whom he shall consecrate to minister in the priest's office in his father's stead, shall make the atonement, and shall put on the linen clothes, even the holy garments:

**Leviticus 16:33** — `קֹּ֔דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And he shall make an atonement for the holy sanctuary, and he shall make an atonement for the tabernacle of the congregation, and for the altar, and he shall make an atonement for the priests, and for all the people of the congregation.

**Leviticus 19:8** — `קֹ֥דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> Therefore every one that eateth it shall bear his iniquity, because he hath profaned the hallowed thing of the LORD: and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

**Leviticus 19:24** — `קֹ֥דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> But in the fourth year all the fruit thereof shall be holy to praise the LORD withal.

**Leviticus 20:3** — `קָדְשִֽׁי׃` (holiness)
> And I will set my face against that man, and will cut him off from among his people; because he hath given of his seed unto Molech, to defile my sanctuary, and to profane my holy name.

**Leviticus 21:6** — `קֹֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> They shall be holy unto their God, and not profane the name of their God: for the offerings of the LORD made by fire, and the bread of their God, they do offer: therefore they shall be holy.

**Leviticus 21:22** — `קָּדְשֵׁ֖י` (holiness)
> He shall eat the bread of his God, both of the most holy, and of the holy.

**Leviticus 22:2** — `קָּדְשֵׁ֣י` (holiness)
> Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, that they separate themselves from the holy things of the children of Israel, and that they profane not my holy name in those things which they hallow unto me: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 22:3** — `קֳּדָשִׁים֙` (holiness)
> Say unto them, Whosoever he be of all your seed among your generations, that goeth unto the holy things, which the children of Israel hallow unto the LORD, having his uncleanness upon him, that soul shall be cut off from my presence: I am the LORD.

**Leviticus 22:4** — `קֳּדָשִׁים֙` (holiness)
> What man soever of the seed of Aaron is a leper, or hath a running issue; he shall not eat of the holy things, until he be clean. And whoso toucheth any thing that is unclean by the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him;

**Leviticus 22:6** — `קֳּדָשִׁ֔ים` (holiness)
> The soul which hath touched any such shall be unclean until even, and shall not eat of the holy things, unless he wash his flesh with water.

**Leviticus 22:7** — `קֳּדָשִׁ֔ים` (holiness)
> And when the sun is down, he shall be clean, and shall afterward eat of the holy things; because it is his food.

**Leviticus 22:10** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> There shall no stranger eat of the holy thing: a sojourner of the priest, or an hired servant, shall not eat of the holy thing.

**Leviticus 22:12** — `קֳּדָשִׁ֖ים` (holiness)
> If the priest's daughter also be married unto a stranger, she may not eat of an offering of the holy things.

**Leviticus 22:14** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And if a man eat of the holy thing unwittingly, then he shall put the fifth part thereof unto it, and shall give it unto the priest with the holy thing.

**Leviticus 22:15** — `קָדְשֵׁ֖י` (holiness)
> And they shall not profane the holy things of the children of Israel, which they offer unto the LORD;

**Leviticus 22:16** — `קָדְשֵׁיהֶ֑ם` (holiness)
> Or suffer them to bear the iniquity of trespass, when they eat their holy things: for I the LORD do sanctify them.

**Leviticus 22:32** — `קָדְשִׁ֔י` (holiness)
> Neither shall ye profane my holy name; but I will be hallowed among the children of Israel: I am the LORD which hallow you,

**Leviticus 23:2** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, Concerning the feasts of the LORD, which ye shall proclaim to be holy convocations, even these are my feasts.

**Leviticus 23:3** — `קֹ֔דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> Six days shall work be done: but the seventh day is the sabbath of rest, an holy convocation; ye shall do no work therein: it is the sabbath of the LORD in all your dwellings.

**Leviticus 23:4** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> These are the feasts of the LORD, even holy convocations, which ye shall proclaim in their seasons.

**Leviticus 23:7** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> In the first day ye shall have an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.

**Leviticus 23:8** — `קֹ֔דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> But ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD seven days: in the seventh day is an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.

**Leviticus 23:20** — `קֹ֛דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits for a wave offering before the LORD, with the two lambs: they shall be holy to the LORD for the priest.

**Leviticus 23:21** — `קֹ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> And ye shall proclaim on the selfsame day, that it may be an holy convocation unto you: ye shall do no servile work therein: it shall be a statute for ever in all your dwellings throughout your generations.

**Leviticus 23:24** — `קֹֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, In the seventh month, in the first day of the month, shall ye have a sabbath, a memorial of blowing of trumpets, an holy convocation.

**Leviticus 23:27** — `קֹ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> Also on the tenth day of this seventh month there shall be a day of atonement: it shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall afflict your souls, and offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 23:35** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> On the first day shall be an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.

**Leviticus 23:36** — `קֹדֶשׁ֩` (holiness)
> Seven days ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD: on the eighth day shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD: it is a solemn assembly; and ye shall do no servile work therein.

**Leviticus 23:37** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> These are the feasts of the LORD, which ye shall proclaim to be holy convocations, to offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD, a burnt offering, and a meat offering, a sacrifice, and drink offerings, every thing upon his day:

**Leviticus 24:9** — `קֹדֶשׁ֩` (holiness)
> And it shall be Aaron's and his sons'; and they shall eat it in the holy place: for it is most holy unto him of the offerings of the LORD made by fire by a perpetual statute.

**Leviticus 25:12** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> For it is the jubile; it shall be holy unto you: ye shall eat the increase thereof out of the field.

**Leviticus 27:3** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> And thy estimation shall be of the male from twenty years old even unto sixty years old, even thy estimation shall be fifty shekels of silver, after the shekel of the sanctuary.

**Leviticus 27:9** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> And if it be a beast, whereof men bring an offering unto the LORD, all that any man giveth of such unto the LORD shall be holy.

**Leviticus 27:10** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> He shall not alter it, nor change it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good: and if he shall at all change beast for beast, then it and the exchange thereof shall be holy.

**Leviticus 27:14** — `קֹ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> And when a man shall sanctify his house to be holy unto the LORD, then the priest shall estimate it, whether it be good or bad: as the priest shall estimate it, so shall it stand.

**Leviticus 27:21** — `קֹ֛דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> But the field, when it goeth out in the jubile, shall be holy unto the LORD, as a field devoted; the possession thereof shall be the priest's.

**Leviticus 27:23** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> Then the priest shall reckon unto him the worth of thy estimation, even unto the year of the jubile: and he shall give thine estimation in that day, as a holy thing unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 27:25** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And all thy estimations shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs shall be the shekel.

**Leviticus 27:28** — `קֹֽדֶשׁ־` (holiness)
> Notwithstanding no devoted thing, that a man shall devote unto the LORD of all that he hath, both of man and beast, and of the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed: every devoted thing is most holy unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 27:30** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And all the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land, or of the fruit of the tree, is the LORD'S: it is holy unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 27:32** — `קֹּ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And concerning the tithe of the herd, or of the flock, even of whatsoever passeth under the rod, the tenth shall be holy unto the LORD.

**Leviticus 27:33** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> He shall not search whether it be good or bad, neither shall he change it: and if he change it at all, then both it and the change thereof shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.

### Numbers (46 verses)

**Numbers 3:28** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> In the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, were eight thousand and six hundred, keeping the charge of the sanctuary.

**Numbers 3:31** — `קֹּ֔דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And their charge shall be the ark, and the table, and the candlestick, and the altars, and the vessels of the sanctuary wherewith they minister, and the hanging, and all the service thereof.

**Numbers 3:32** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> And Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest shall be chief over the chief of the Levites, and have the oversight of them that keep the charge of the sanctuary.

**Numbers 3:47** — `קֹּ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> Thou shalt even take five shekels apiece by the poll, after the shekel of the sanctuary shalt thou take them: (the shekel is twenty gerahs:)

**Numbers 3:50** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> Of the firstborn of the children of Israel took he the money; a thousand three hundred and threescore and five shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary:

**Numbers 4:4** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> This shall be the service of the sons of Kohath in the tabernacle of the congregation, about the most holy things:

**Numbers 4:12** — `קֹּ֗דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And they shall take all the instruments of ministry, wherewith they minister in the sanctuary, and put them in a cloth of blue, and cover them with a covering of badgers' skins, and shall put them on a bar:

**Numbers 4:15** — `קֹּ֜דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And when Aaron and his sons have made an end of covering the sanctuary, and all the vessels of the sanctuary, as the camp is to set forward; after that, the sons of Kohath shall come to bear it: but they shall not touch any holy thing, lest they die. These things are the burden of the sons of Kohath in the tabernacle of the congregation.

**Numbers 4:16** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And to the office of Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest pertaineth the oil for the light, and the sweet incense, and the daily meat offering, and the anointing oil, and the oversight of all the tabernacle, and of all that therein is, in the sanctuary, and in the vessels thereof.

**Numbers 4:19** — `קֹ֣דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> But thus do unto them, that they may live, and not die, when they approach unto the most holy things: Aaron and his sons shall go in, and appoint them every one to his service and to his burden:

**Numbers 4:20** — `קֹּ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> But they shall not go in to see when the holy things are covered, lest they die.

**Numbers 5:9** — `קָדְשֵׁ֧י` (holiness)
> And every offering of all the holy things of the children of Israel, which they bring unto the priest, shall be his.

**Numbers 5:10** — `קֳדָשָׁ֖יו` (holiness)
> And every man's hallowed things shall be his: whatsoever any man giveth the priest, it shall be his.

**Numbers 6:20** — `קֹ֤דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the LORD: this is holy for the priest, with the wave breast and heave shoulder: and after that the Nazarite may drink wine.

**Numbers 7:9** — `קֹּ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> But unto the sons of Kohath he gave none: because the service of the sanctuary belonging unto them was that they should bear upon their shoulders.

**Numbers 7:13** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And his offering was one silver charger, the weight thereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them were full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:19** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> He offered for his offering one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:25** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:31** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger of the weight of an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:37** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:43** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger of the weight of an hundred and thirty shekels, a silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:49** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:55** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger of the weight of an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:61** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:67** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:73** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:79** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:

**Numbers 7:85** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> Each charger of silver weighing an hundred and thirty shekels, each bowl seventy: all the silver vessels weighed two thousand and four hundred shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary:

**Numbers 7:86** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> The golden spoons were twelve, full of incense, weighing ten shekels apiece, after the shekel of the sanctuary: all the gold of the spoons was an hundred and twenty shekels.

**Numbers 8:19** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and to his sons from among the children of Israel, to do the service of the children of Israel in the tabernacle of the congregation, and to make an atonement for the children of Israel: that there be no plague among the children of Israel, when the children of Israel come nigh unto the sanctuary.

**Numbers 18:3** — `קֹּ֤דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And they shall keep thy charge, and the charge of all the tabernacle: only they shall not come nigh the vessels of the sanctuary and the altar, that neither they, nor ye also, die.

**Numbers 18:5** — `קֹּ֔דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And ye shall keep the charge of the sanctuary, and the charge of the altar: that there be no wrath any more upon the children of Israel.

**Numbers 18:9** — `קֹּ֥דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> This shall be thine of the most holy things, reserved from the fire: every oblation of theirs, every meat offering of theirs, and every sin offering of theirs, and every trespass offering of theirs, which they shall render unto me, shall be most holy for thee and for thy sons.

**Numbers 18:10** — `קֹ֥דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> In the most holy place shalt thou eat it; every male shall eat it: it shall be holy unto thee.

**Numbers 18:16** — `קֹּ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And those that are to be redeemed from a month old shalt thou redeem, according to thine estimation, for the money of five shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary, which is twenty gerahs.

**Numbers 18:17** — `קֹ֣דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> But the firstling of a cow, or the firstling of a sheep, or the firstling of a goat, thou shalt not redeem; they are holy: thou shalt sprinkle their blood upon the altar, and shalt burn their fat for an offering made by fire, for a sweet savour unto the LORD.

**Numbers 18:19** — `קֳּדָשִׁ֗ים` (holiness)
> All the heave offerings of the holy things, which the children of Israel offer unto the LORD, have I given thee, and thy sons and thy daughters with thee, by a statute for ever: it is a covenant of salt for ever before the LORD unto thee and to thy seed with thee.

**Numbers 28:7** — `קֹּ֗דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And the drink offering thereof shall be the fourth part of an hin for the one lamb: in the holy place shalt thou cause the strong wine to be poured unto the LORD for a drink offering.

**Numbers 28:18** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> In the first day shall be an holy convocation; ye shall do no manner of servile work therein:

**Numbers 28:25** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And on the seventh day ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work.

**Numbers 28:26** — `קֹ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> Also in the day of the firstfruits, when ye bring a new meat offering unto the LORD, after your weeks be out, ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work:

**Numbers 29:1** — `קֹ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> And in the seventh month, on the first day of the month, ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work: it is a day of blowing the trumpets unto you.

**Numbers 29:7** — `קֹ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> And ye shall have on the tenth day of this seventh month an holy convocation; and ye shall afflict your souls: ye shall not do any work therein:

**Numbers 29:12** — `קֹ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work, and ye shall keep a feast unto the LORD seven days:

**Numbers 31:6** — `קֹּ֛דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And Moses sent them to the war, a thousand of every tribe, them and Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest, to the war, with the holy instruments, and the trumpets to blow in his hand.

**Numbers 35:25** — `קֹּֽדֶשׁ׃` (holiness)
> And the congregation shall deliver the slayer out of the hand of the revenger of blood, and the congregation shall restore him to the city of his refuge, whither he was fled: and he shall abide in it unto the death of the high priest, which was anointed with the holy oil.

### Deuteronomy (4 verses)

**Deuteronomy 12:26** — `קָֽדָשֶׁ֛יךָ` (holiness)
> Only thy holy things which thou hast, and thy vows, thou shalt take, and go unto the place which the LORD shall choose:

**Deuteronomy 26:13** — `קֹּ֣דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> Then thou shalt say before the LORD thy God, I have brought away the hallowed things out of mine house, and also have given them unto the Levite, and unto the stranger, to the fatherless, and to the widow, according to all thy commandments which thou hast commanded me: I have not transgressed thy commandments, neither have I forgotten them:

**Deuteronomy 26:15** — `קָדְשְׁךָ֜` (holiness)
> Look down from thy holy habitation, from heaven, and bless thy people Israel, and the land which thou hast given us, as thou swarest unto our fathers, a land that floweth with milk and honey.

**Deuteronomy 33:2** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And he said, The LORD came from Sinai, and rose up from Seir unto them; he shined forth from mount Paran, and he came with ten thousands of saints: from his right hand went a fiery law for them.

### Joshua (2 verses)

**Joshua 5:15** — `קֹ֣דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And the captain of the LORD'S host said unto Joshua, Loose thy shoe from off thy foot; for the place whereon thou standest is holy. And Joshua did so.

**Joshua 6:19** — `קֹ֥דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> But all the silver, and gold, and vessels of brass and iron, are consecrated unto the LORD: they shall come into the treasury of the LORD.

### 1 Samuel (3 verses)

**1 Samuel 21:4** — `קֹ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)
> And the priest answered David, and said, There is no common bread under mine hand, but there is hallowed bread; if the young men have kept themselves at least from women.

**1 Samuel 21:5** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And David answered the priest, and said unto him, Of a truth women have been kept from us about these three days, since I came out, and the vessels of the young men are holy, and the bread is in a manner common, yea, though it were sanctified this day in the vessel.

**1 Samuel 21:6** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> So the priest gave him hallowed bread: for there was no bread there but the shewbread, that was taken from before the LORD, to put hot bread in the day when it was taken away.

### 1 Kings (8 verses)

**1 Kings 6:16** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And he built twenty cubits on the sides of the house, both the floor and the walls with boards of cedar: he even built them for it within, even for the oracle, even for the most holy place.

**1 Kings 7:50** — `קֹ֣דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And the bowls, and the snuffers, and the basons, and the spoons, and the censers of pure gold; and the hinges of gold, both for the doors of the inner house, the most holy place, and for the doors of the house, to wit, of the temple.

**1 Kings 7:51** — `קָדְשֵׁ֣י׀` (holiness)
> So was ended all the work that king Solomon made for the house of the LORD. And Solomon brought in the things which David his father had dedicated; even the silver, and the gold, and the vessels, did he put among the treasures of the house of the LORD.

**1 Kings 8:4** — `קֹּ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)
> And they brought up the ark of the LORD, and the tabernacle of the congregation, and all the holy vessels that were in the tabernacle, even those did the priests and the Levites bring up.

**1 Kings 8:6** — `קֹ֣דֶשׁ` (holiness)

**Psalms 110:3** — `קֹ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)

**Psalms 134:2** — `קֹ֑דֶשׁ` (holiness)

**Psalms 138:2** — `קָדְשְׁךָ֡` (holiness)

**Psalms 145:21** — `קָדְשֹׁ֗ו` (holiness)

**Psalms 150:1** — `קָדְשֹׁ֑ו` (holiness)

**Isaiah 35:8** — `קֹּ֨דֶשׁ֙` (holiness)

**Isaiah 52:10** — `קָדְשֹׁ֔ו` (holiness)

**Isaiah 56:7** — `קָדְשִׁ֗י` (holiness)

**Isaiah 57:13** — `קָדְשִֽׁי׃` (holiness)
> When thou criest, let thy companies deliver thee; but the wind shall carry them all away; vanity shall take them: but he that putteth his trust in me shall possess the land, and shall inherit my holy mountain;

**Isaiah 58:13** — `קָדְשִׁ֑י` (holiness)

**Isaiah 62:9** — `קָדְשִֽׁי׃ ס` (holiness)

**Isaiah 62:12** — `קֹּ֖דֶשׁ` (holiness)

**Isaiah 63:10** — `קָדְשֹׁ֑ו` (holiness)

## `H5769` — Olam ("Everlasting / Eternal")

> Used in 'El Olam' (The Everlasting God) — Genesis 21:33.

**413 verses found.**

### Genesis (13 verses)

**Genesis 3:22** — `עֹלָֽם׃` (eternity)
> And the LORD God said, Behold, the man is become as one of us, to know good and evil: and now, lest he put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever:

**Genesis 6:3** — `עֹלָ֔ם` (eternity)
> And the LORD said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for that he also is flesh: yet his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.

**Genesis 6:4** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> There were giants in the earth in those days; and also after that, when the sons of God came in unto the daughters of men, and they bare children to them, the same became mighty men which were of old, men of renown.

**Genesis 9:12** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> And God said, This is the token of the covenant which I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for perpetual generations:

**Genesis 9:16** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> And the bow shall be in the cloud; and I will look upon it, that I may remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is upon the earth.

**Genesis 13:15** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)

**Genesis 17:7** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)

**Genesis 17:13** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)

**Genesis 17:19** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)

> And they shall be upon Aaron, and upon his sons, when they come in unto the tabernacle of the congregation, or when they come near unto the altar to minister in the holy place; that they bear not iniquity, and die: it shall be a statute for ever unto him and his seed after him.

**Exodus 29:9** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> And thou shalt gird them with girdles, Aaron and his sons, and put the bonnets on them: and the priest's office shall be theirs for a perpetual statute: and thou shalt consecrate Aaron and his sons.

**Exodus 29:28** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)
> And it shall be Aaron's and his sons' by a statute for ever from the children of Israel: for it is an heave offering: and it shall be an heave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifice of their peace offerings, even their heave offering unto the LORD.

**Exodus 30:21** — `עֹולָ֛ם` (eternity)
> So they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they die not: and it shall be a statute for ever to them, even to him and to his seed throughout their generations.

**Exodus 31:16** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Wherefore the children of Israel shall keep the sabbath, to observe the sabbath throughout their generations, for a perpetual covenant.

**Exodus 31:17** — `עֹלָ֑ם` (eternity)
> It is a sign between me and the children of Israel for ever: for in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed.

**Exodus 32:13** — `עֹלָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou swarest by thine own self, and saidst unto them, I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have spoken of will I give unto your seed, and they shall inherit it for ever.

**Exodus 40:15** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> And thou shalt anoint them, as thou didst anoint their father, that they may minister unto me in the priest's office: for their anointing shall surely be an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations.

### Leviticus (21 verses)

**Leviticus 3:17** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> It shall be a perpetual statute for your generations throughout all your dwellings, that ye eat neither fat nor blood.

**Leviticus 6:18** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> All the males among the children of Aaron shall eat of it. It shall be a statute for ever in your generations concerning the offerings of the LORD made by fire: every one that toucheth them shall be holy.

**Leviticus 6:22** — `עֹולָ֕ם` (eternity)
> And the priest of his sons that is anointed in his stead shall offer it: it is a statute for ever unto the LORD; it shall be wholly burnt.

**Leviticus 7:34** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> For the wave breast and the heave shoulder have I taken of the children of Israel from off the sacrifices of their peace offerings, and have given them unto Aaron the priest and unto his sons by a statute for ever from among the children of Israel.

**Leviticus 7:36** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Which the LORD commanded to be given them of the children of Israel, in the day that he anointed them, by a statute for ever throughout their generations.

**Leviticus 10:9** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Do not drink wine nor strong drink, thou, nor thy sons with thee, when ye go into the tabernacle of the congregation, lest ye die: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations:

**Leviticus 10:15** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> The heave shoulder and the wave breast shall they bring with the offerings made by fire of the fat, to wave it for a wave offering before the LORD; and it shall be thine, and thy sons' with thee, by a statute for ever; as the LORD hath commanded.

**Leviticus 16:29** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> And this shall be a statute for ever unto you: that in the seventh month, on the tenth day of the month, ye shall afflict your souls, and do no work at all, whether it be one of your own country, or a stranger that sojourneth among you:

**Leviticus 16:31** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> It shall be a sabbath of rest unto you, and ye shall afflict your souls, by a statute for ever.

**Leviticus 16:34** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)
> And this shall be an everlasting statute unto you, to make an atonement for the children of Israel for all their sins once a year. And he did as the LORD commanded Moses.

**Leviticus 17:7** — `עֹולָ֛ם` (eternity)
> And they shall no more offer their sacrifices unto devils, after whom they have gone a whoring. This shall be a statute for ever unto them throughout their generations.

**Leviticus 23:14** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> And ye shall eat neither bread, nor parched corn, nor green ears, until the selfsame day that ye have brought an offering unto your God: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.

**Leviticus 23:21** — `עֹולָ֛ם` (eternity)
> And ye shall proclaim on the selfsame day, that it may be an holy convocation unto you: ye shall do no servile work therein: it shall be a statute for ever in all your dwellings throughout your generations.

**Leviticus 23:31** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> Ye shall do no manner of work: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.

**Leviticus 23:41** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> And ye shall keep it a feast unto the LORD seven days in the year. It shall be a statute for ever in your generations: ye shall celebrate it in the seventh month.

**Leviticus 24:3** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Without the vail of the testimony, in the tabernacle of the congregation, shall Aaron order it from the evening unto the morning before the LORD continually: it shall be a statute for ever in your generations.

**Leviticus 24:8** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Every sabbath he shall set it in order before the LORD continually, being taken from the children of Israel by an everlasting covenant.

**Leviticus 24:9** — `עֹולָֽם׃ ס` (eternity)
> And it shall be Aaron's and his sons'; and they shall eat it in the holy place: for it is most holy unto him of the offerings of the LORD made by fire by a perpetual statute.

**Leviticus 25:32** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Notwithstanding the cities of the Levites, and the houses of the cities of their possession, may the Levites redeem at any time.

**Leviticus 25:34** — `עֹולָ֛ם` (eternity)
> But the field of the suburbs of their cities may not be sold; for it is their perpetual possession.

**Leviticus 25:46** — `עֹלָ֖ם` (eternity)
> And ye shall take them as an inheritance for your children after you, to inherit them for a possession; they shall be your bondmen for ever: but over your brethren the children of Israel, ye shall not rule one over another with rigour.

**2 Chronicles 20:7** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Art not thou our God, who didst drive out the inhabitants of this land before thy people Israel, and gavest it to the seed of Abraham thy friend for ever?

**2 Chronicles 20:21** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> And when he had consulted with the people, he appointed singers unto the LORD, and that should praise the beauty of holiness, as they went out before the army, and to say, Praise the LORD; for his mercy endureth for ever.

**2 Chronicles 30:8** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Now be ye not stiffnecked, as your fathers were, but yield yourselves unto the LORD, and enter into his sanctuary, which he hath sanctified for ever: and serve the LORD your God, that the fierceness of his wrath may turn away from you.

**2 Chronicles 33:4** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Also he built altars in the house of the LORD, whereof the LORD had said, In Jerusalem shall my name be for ever.

### Ezra (2 verses)

**Ezra 3:11** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> And they sang together by course in praising and giving thanks unto the LORD; because he is good, for his mercy endureth for ever toward Israel. And all the people shouted with a great shout, when they praised the LORD, because the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid.

**Ezra 9:12** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> Now therefore give not your daughters unto their sons, neither take their daughters unto your sons, nor seek their peace or their wealth for ever: that ye may be strong, and eat the good of the land, and leave it for an inheritance to your children for ever.

> Then the Levites, Jeshua, and Kadmiel, Bani, Hashabniah, Sherebiah, Hodijah, Shebaniah, and Pethahiah, said, Stand up and bless the LORD your God for ever and ever: and blessed be thy glorious name, which is exalted above all blessing and praise.

> On that day they read in the book of Moses in the audience of the people; and therein was found written, that the Ammonite and the Moabite should not come into the congregation of God for ever;

> I loathe it; I would not live alway: let me alone; for my days are vanity.

> Hast thou marked the old way which wicked men have trodden?

**Job 41:4** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)

### Psalms (138 verses)

**Psalms 5:11** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)

> Thou hast rebuked the heathen, thou hast destroyed the wicked, thou hast put out their name for ever and ever.

**Psalms 9:7** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> But the LORD shall endure for ever: he hath prepared his throne for judgment.

**Psalms 10:16** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 12:7** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)

**Psalms 15:5** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)

> Great deliverance giveth he to his king; and sheweth mercy to his anointed, to David, and to his seed for evermore.

> He asked life of thee, and thou gavest it him, even length of days for ever and ever.

**Psalms 24:7** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)

> Lift up your heads, O ye gates; even lift them up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.

**Psalms 25:6** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Remember, O LORD, thy tender mercies and thy lovingkindnesses; for they have been ever of old.

**Psalms 28:9** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)

**Psalms 29:10** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> The LORD sitteth upon the flood; yea, the LORD sitteth King for ever.

**Psalms 30:6** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> And in my prosperity I said, I shall never be moved.

**Psalms 30:12** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 31:1** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. In thee, O LORD, do I put my trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.

**Psalms 33:11** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> The counsel of the LORD standeth for ever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.

**Psalms 37:18** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> The LORD knoweth the days of the upright: and their inheritance shall be for ever.

**Psalms 37:27** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Depart from evil, and do good; and dwell for evermore.

**Psalms 37:28** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> For the LORD loveth judgment, and forsaketh not his saints; they are preserved for ever: but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.

**Psalms 41:12** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> And as for me, thou upholdest me in mine integrity, and settest me before thy face for ever.

**Psalms 41:13** — `עֹולָם` (eternity)
> Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting, and to everlasting. Amen, and Amen.

**Psalms 44:8** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> In God we boast all the day long, and praise thy name for ever. Selah.

**Psalms 45:2** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Thou art fairer than the children of men: grace is poured into thy lips: therefore God hath blessed thee for ever.

**Psalms 45:6** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever: the sceptre of thy kingdom is a right sceptre.

**Psalms 45:17** — `עֹלָ֥ם` (eternity)
> I will make thy name to be remembered in all generations: therefore shall the people praise thee for ever and ever.

**Psalms 48:8** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God: God will establish it for ever. Selah.

**Psalms 48:14** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> For this God is our God for ever and ever: he will be our guide even unto death.

**Psalms 49:8** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> (For the redemption of their soul is precious, and it ceaseth for ever:)

**Psalms 49:11** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 52:8** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God: I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.

**Psalms 52:9** — `עֹולָם` (eternity)
> I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name; for it is good before thy saints.

**Psalms 55:22** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.

**Psalms 61:4** — `עֹולָמִ֑ים` (eternity)

**Psalms 61:7** — `עֹ֭ולָם` (eternity)
> He shall abide before God for ever: O prepare mercy and truth, which may preserve him.

**Psalms 66:7** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)

> In thee, O LORD, do I put my trust: let me never be put to confusion.

**Psalms 72:17** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 73:26** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.

**Psalms 77:5** — `עֹולָמִֽים׃` (eternity)

**Psalms 79:13** — `עֹ֫ולָ֥ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 81:15** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)

**Psalms 86:12** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)

**Psalms 89:4** — `עֹ֭ולָם` (eternity)

**Psalms 89:28** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 89:36** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 89:37** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)

> Blessed be the LORD for evermore. Amen, and Amen.

**Psalms 90:2** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)

> For the LORD is good; his mercy is everlasting; and his truth endureth to all generations.

**Psalms 102:12** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 103:9** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 103:17** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> But the mercy of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting upon them that fear him, and his righteousness unto children's children;

**Psalms 104:31** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> The glory of the LORD shall endure for ever: the LORD shall rejoice in his works.

**Psalms 105:8** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)

**Psalms 105:10** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> And confirmed the same unto Jacob for a law, and to Israel for an everlasting covenant:

**Psalms 106:1** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Praise ye the LORD. O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 106:31** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)

**Psalms 106:48** — `עֹולָ֨ם׀` (eternity)
> Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting to everlasting: and let all the people say, Amen. Praise ye the LORD.

> O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 110:4** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> The LORD hath sworn, and will not repent, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek.

**Psalms 111:5** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> He hath given meat unto them that fear him: he will ever be mindful of his covenant.

**Psalms 111:8** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> They stand fast for ever and ever, and are done in truth and uprightness.

**Psalms 111:9** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> He sent redemption unto his people: he hath commanded his covenant for ever: holy and reverend is his name.

**Psalms 112:6** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> Surely he shall not be moved for ever: the righteous shall be in everlasting remembrance.

**Psalms 113:2** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Blessed be the name of the LORD from this time forth and for evermore.

**Psalms 115:18** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)
> But we will bless the LORD from this time forth and for evermore. Praise the LORD.

**Psalms 117:2** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)
> For his merciful kindness is great toward us: and the truth of the LORD endureth for ever. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 118:1** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: because his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 118:2** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Let Israel now say, that his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 118:3** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Let the house of Aaron now say, that his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 118:4** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Let them now that fear the LORD say, that his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 118:29** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 119:44** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> So shall I keep thy law continually for ever and ever.

**Psalms 119:52** — `עֹולָ֥ם׀` (eternity)
> I remembered thy judgments of old, O LORD; and have comforted myself.

**Psalms 119:89** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> LAMED. For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven.

**Psalms 119:93** — `עֹולָם` (eternity)
> I will never forget thy precepts: for with them thou hast quickened me.

**Psalms 119:98** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Thou through thy commandments hast made me wiser than mine enemies: for they are ever with me.

**Psalms 119:111** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> Thy testimonies have I taken as an heritage for ever: for they are the rejoicing of my heart.

**Psalms 119:112** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> I have inclined mine heart to perform thy statutes alway, even unto the end.

**Psalms 119:142** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> Thy righteousness is an everlasting righteousness, and thy law is the truth.

**Psalms 119:144** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)
> The righteousness of thy testimonies is everlasting: give me understanding, and I shall live.

**Psalms 119:152** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Concerning thy testimonies, I have known of old that thou hast founded them for ever.

**Psalms 119:160** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)
> Thy word is true from the beginning: and every one of thy righteous judgments endureth for ever.

**Psalms 121:8** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> The LORD shall preserve thy going out and thy coming in from this time forth, and even for evermore.

**Psalms 125:1** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> A Song of degrees. They that trust in the LORD shall be as mount Zion, which cannot be removed, but abideth for ever.

**Psalms 125:2** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> As the mountains are round about Jerusalem, so the LORD is round about his people from henceforth even for ever.

**Psalms 131:3** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Let Israel hope in the LORD from henceforth and for ever.

**Psalms 133:3** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> As the dew of Hermon, and as the dew that descended upon the mountains of Zion: for there the LORD commanded the blessing, even life for evermore.

**Psalms 135:13** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> Thy name, O LORD, endureth for ever; and thy memorial, O LORD, throughout all generations.

**Psalms 136:1** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:2** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> O give thanks unto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:3** — `עֹלָ֣ם` (eternity)
> O give thanks to the Lord of lords: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:4** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> To him who alone doeth great wonders: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:5** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> To him that by wisdom made the heavens: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:6** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> To him that stretched out the earth above the waters: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:7** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> To him that made great lights: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:8** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> The sun to rule by day: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:9** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> The moon and stars to rule by night: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:10** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> To him that smote Egypt in their firstborn: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:11** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> And brought out Israel from among them: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:12** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> With a strong hand, and with a stretched out arm: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:13** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> To him which divided the Red sea into parts: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:14** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> And made Israel to pass through the midst of it: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:15** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> But overthrew Pharaoh and his host in the Red sea: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:16** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> To him which led his people through the wilderness: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:17** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> To him which smote great kings: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:18** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> And slew famous kings: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:19** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Sihon king of the Amorites: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:20** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> And Og the king of Bashan: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:21** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> And gave their land for an heritage: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:22** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Even an heritage unto Israel his servant: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:23** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Who remembered us in our low estate: for his mercy endureth for ever:

**Psalms 136:24** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> And hath redeemed us from our enemies: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:25** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Who giveth food to all flesh: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 136:26** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> O give thanks unto the God of heaven: for his mercy endureth for ever.

**Psalms 138:8** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> The LORD will perfect that which concerneth me: thy mercy, O LORD, endureth for ever: forsake not the works of thine own hands.

**Psalms 139:24** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> And see if there be any wicked way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting.

**Psalms 143:3** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> For the enemy hath persecuted my soul; he hath smitten my life down to the ground; he hath made me to dwell in darkness, as those that have been long dead.

**Psalms 145:1** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> David's Psalm of praise. I will extol thee, my God, O king; and I will bless thy name for ever and ever.

**Psalms 145:2** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> Every day will I bless thee; and I will praise thy name for ever and ever.

**Psalms 145:13** — `עֹֽלָמִ֑ים` (eternity)
> Thy kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and thy dominion endureth throughout all generations.

**Psalms 145:21** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> My mouth shall speak the praise of the LORD: and let all flesh bless his holy name for ever and ever.

**Psalms 146:6** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Which made heaven, and earth, the sea, and all that therein is: which keepeth truth for ever:

**Psalms 146:10** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)
> The LORD shall reign for ever, even thy God, O Zion, unto all generations. Praise ye the LORD.

**Psalms 148:6** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> He hath also stablished them for ever and ever: he hath made a decree which shall not pass.

### Proverbs (6 verses)

**Proverbs 8:23** — `עֹולָם` (eternity)
> I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, or ever the earth was.

**Proverbs 10:25** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> As the whirlwind passeth, so is the wicked no more: but the righteous is an everlasting foundation.

**Proverbs 10:30** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> The righteous shall never be removed: but the wicked shall not inhabit the earth.

**Proverbs 22:28** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> Remove not the ancient landmark, which thy fathers have set.

**Proverbs 23:10** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> Remove not the old landmark; and enter not into the fields of the fatherless:

**Proverbs 27:24** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> For riches are not for ever: and doth the crown endure to every generation?

### Ecclesiastes (7 verses)

**Ecclesiastes 1:4** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> One generation passeth away, and another generation cometh: but the earth abideth for ever.

**Ecclesiastes 1:10** — `עֹֽלָמִ֔ים` (eternity)
> Is there any thing whereof it may be said, See, this is new? it hath been already of old time, which was before us.

**Ecclesiastes 2:16** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> For there is no remembrance of the wise more than of the fool for ever; seeing that which now is in the days to come shall all be forgotten. And how dieth the wise man? as the fool.

**Ecclesiastes 3:11** — `עֹלָם֙` (eternity)
> He hath made every thing beautiful in his time: also he hath set the world in their heart, so that no man can find out the work that God maketh from the beginning to the end.

**Ecclesiastes 3:14** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> I know that, whatsoever God doeth, it shall be for ever: nothing can be put to it, nor any thing taken from it: and God doeth it, that men should fear before him.

**Ecclesiastes 9:6** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Also their love, and their hatred, and their envy, is now perished; neither have they any more a portion for ever in any thing that is done under the sun.

**Ecclesiastes 12:5** — `עֹולָמֹ֔ו` (eternity)
> Also when they shall be afraid of that which is high, and fears shall be in the way, and the almond tree shall flourish, and the grasshopper shall be a burden, and desire shall fail: because man goeth to his long home, and the mourners go about the streets:

### Isaiah (45 verses)

**Isaiah 9:7** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Of the increase of his government and peace there shall be no end, upon the throne of David, and upon his kingdom, to order it, and to establish it with judgment and with justice from henceforth even for ever. The zeal of the LORD of hosts will perform this.

**Isaiah 14:20** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Thou shalt not be joined with them in burial, because thou hast destroyed thy land, and slain thy people: the seed of evildoers shall never be renowned.

**Isaiah 24:5** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> The earth also is defiled under the inhabitants thereof; because they have transgressed the laws, changed the ordinance, broken the everlasting covenant.

**Isaiah 25:2** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> For thou hast made of a city an heap; of a defenced city a ruin: a palace of strangers to be no city; it shall never be built.

**Isaiah 26:4** — `עֹולָמִֽים׃` (eternity)
> Trust ye in the LORD for ever: for in the LORD JEHOVAH is everlasting strength:

**Isaiah 30:8** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Now go, write it before them in a table, and note it in a book, that it may be for the time to come for ever and ever:

**Isaiah 32:14** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Because the palaces shall be forsaken; the multitude of the city shall be left; the forts and towers shall be for dens for ever, a joy of wild asses, a pasture of flocks;

**Isaiah 32:17** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> And the work of righteousness shall be peace; and the effect of righteousness quietness and assurance for ever.

**Isaiah 33:14** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> The sinners in Zion are afraid; fearfulness hath surprised the hypocrites. Who among us shall dwell with the devouring fire? who among us shall dwell with everlasting burnings?

**Isaiah 34:10** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> It shall not be quenched night nor day; the smoke thereof shall go up for ever: from generation to generation it shall lie waste; none shall pass through it for ever and ever.

**Isaiah 34:17** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> And he hath cast the lot for them, and his hand hath divided it unto them by line: they shall possess it for ever, from generation to generation shall they dwell therein.

**Isaiah 35:10** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> And the ransomed of the LORD shall return, and come to Zion with songs and everlasting joy upon their heads: they shall obtain joy and gladness, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.

**Isaiah 40:8** — `עֹולָֽם׃ ס` (eternity)
> The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever.

**Isaiah 40:28** — `עֹולָ֤ם׀` (eternity)
> Hast thou not known? hast thou not heard, that the everlasting God, the LORD, the Creator of the ends of the earth, fainteth not, neither is weary? there is no searching of his understanding.

**Isaiah 42:14** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> I have long time holden my peace; I have been still, and refrained myself: now will I cry like a travailing woman; I will destroy and devour at once.

**Isaiah 44:7** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> And who, as I, shall call, and shall declare it, and set it in order for me, since I appointed the ancient people? and the things that are coming, and shall come, let them shew unto them.

**Isaiah 45:17** — `עֹולָמִ֑ים` (eternity)
> But Israel shall be saved in the LORD with an everlasting salvation: ye shall not be ashamed nor confounded world without end.

**Isaiah 46:9** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> Remember the former things of old: for I am God, and there is none else; I am God, and there is none like me,

**Isaiah 47:7** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> And thou saidst, I shall be a lady for ever: so that thou didst not lay these things to thy heart, neither didst remember the latter end of it.

**Isaiah 51:6** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look upon the earth beneath: for the heavens shall vanish away like smoke, and the earth shall wax old like a garment, and they that dwell therein shall die in like manner: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my righteousness shall not be abolished.

**Isaiah 51:8** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> For the moth shall eat them up like a garment, and the worm shall eat them like wool: but my righteousness shall be for ever, and my salvation from generation to generation.

**Isaiah 51:9** — `עֹולָמִ֑ים` (eternity)
> Awake, awake, put on strength, O arm of the LORD; awake, as in the ancient days, in the generations of old. Art thou not it that hath cut Rahab, and wounded the dragon?

**Isaiah 51:11** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Therefore the redeemed of the LORD shall return, and come with singing unto Zion; and everlasting joy shall be upon their head: they shall obtain gladness and joy; and sorrow and mourning shall flee away.

**Isaiah 54:8** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> In a little wrath I hid my face from thee for a moment; but with everlasting kindness will I have mercy on thee, saith the LORD thy Redeemer.

**Isaiah 55:3** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Incline your ear, and come unto me: hear, and your soul shall live; and I will make an everlasting covenant with you, even the sure mercies of David.

**Isaiah 55:13** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Instead of the thorn shall come up the fir tree, and instead of the brier shall come up the myrtle tree: and it shall be to the LORD for a name, for an everlasting sign that shall not be cut off.

**Isaiah 56:5** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> Even unto them will I give in mine house and within my walls a place and a name better than of sons and of daughters: I will give them an everlasting name, that shall not be cut off.

**Isaiah 57:11** — `עֹלָ֔ם` (eternity)
> And of whom hast thou been afraid or feared, that thou hast lied, and hast not remembered me, nor laid it to thy heart? have not I held my peace even of old, and thou fearest me not?

**Isaiah 57:16** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> For I will not contend for ever, neither will I be always wroth: for the spirit should fail before me, and the souls which I have made.

**Isaiah 58:12** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> And they that shall be of thee shall build the old waste places: thou shalt raise up the foundations of many generations; and thou shalt be called, The repairer of the breach, The restorer of paths to dwell in.

**Isaiah 59:21** — `עֹולָֽם׃ ס` (eternity)
> As for me, this is my covenant with them, saith the LORD; My spirit that is upon thee, and my words which I have put in thy mouth, shall not depart out of thy mouth, nor out of the mouth of thy seed, nor out of the mouth of thy seed's seed, saith the LORD, from henceforth and for ever.

**Isaiah 60:15** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Whereas thou hast been forsaken and hated, so that no man went through thee, I will make thee an eternal excellency, a joy of many generations.

**Isaiah 60:19** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> The sun shall be no more thy light by day; neither for brightness shall the moon give light unto thee: but the LORD shall be unto thee an everlasting light, and thy God thy glory.

**Isaiah 60:20** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Thy sun shall no more go down; neither shall thy moon withdraw itself: for the LORD shall be thine everlasting light, and the days of thy mourning shall be ended.

**Isaiah 60:21** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Thy people also shall be all righteous: they shall inherit the land for ever, the branch of my planting, the work of my hands, that I may be glorified.

**Isaiah 61:4** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> And they shall build the old wastes, they shall raise up the former desolations, and they shall repair the waste cities, the desolations of many generations.

**Isaiah 61:7** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> For your shame ye shall have double; and for confusion they shall rejoice in their portion: therefore in their land they shall possess the double: everlasting joy shall be unto them.

**Isaiah 61:8** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> For I the LORD love judgment, I hate robbery for burnt offering; and I will direct their work in truth, and I will make an everlasting covenant with them.

**Isaiah 63:9** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> In all their affliction he was afflicted, and the angel of his presence saved them: in his love and in his pity he redeemed them; and he bare them, and carried them all the days of old.

**Isaiah 63:11** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Then he remembered the days of old, Moses, and his people, saying, Where is he that brought them up out of the sea with the shepherd of his flock? where is he that put his holy Spirit within him?

**Isaiah 63:12** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> That led them by the right hand of Moses with his glorious arm, dividing the water before them, to make himself an everlasting name?

**Isaiah 63:16** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Doubtless thou art our father, though Abraham be ignorant of us, and Israel acknowledge us not: thou, O LORD, art our father, our redeemer; thy name is from everlasting.

**Isaiah 63:19** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> We are thine: thou never barest rule over them; they were not called by thy name.

**Isaiah 64:4** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> For since the beginning of the world men have not heard, nor perceived by the ear, neither hath the eye seen, O God, beside thee, what he hath prepared for him that waiteth for him.

**Isaiah 64:5** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Thou meetest him that rejoiceth and worketh righteousness, those that remember thee in thy ways: behold, thou art wroth; for we have sinned: in those is continuance, and we shall be saved.

### Jeremiah (31 verses)

**Jeremiah 2:20** — `עֹולָ֞ם` (eternity)
> For of old time I have broken thy yoke, and burst thy bands; and thou saidst, I will not transgress; when upon every high hill and under every green tree thou wanderest, playing the harlot.

**Jeremiah 3:5** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Will he reserve his anger for ever? will he keep it to the end? Behold, thou hast spoken and done evil things as thou couldest.

**Jeremiah 3:12** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Go and proclaim these words toward the north, and say, Return, thou backsliding Israel, saith the LORD; and I will not cause mine anger to fall upon you: for I am merciful, saith the LORD, and I will not keep anger for ever.

**Jeremiah 5:15** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> Lo, I will bring a nation upon you from far, O house of Israel, saith the LORD: it is a mighty nation, it is an ancient nation, a nation whose language thou knowest not, neither understandest what they say.

**Jeremiah 5:22** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Fear ye not me? saith the LORD: will ye not tremble at my presence, which have placed the sand for the bound of the sea by a perpetual decree, that it cannot pass it: and though the waves thereof toss themselves, yet can they not prevail; though they roar, yet can they not pass over it?

**Jeremiah 6:16** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)
> Thus saith the LORD, Stand ye in the ways, and see, and ask for the old paths, where is the good way, and walk therein, and ye shall find rest for your souls. But they said, We will not walk therein.

**Jeremiah 7:7** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Then will I cause you to dwell in this place, in the land that I gave to your fathers, for ever and ever.

**Jeremiah 10:10** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> But the LORD is the true God, he is the living God, and an everlasting king: at his wrath the earth shall tremble, and the nations shall not be able to abide his indignation.

**Jeremiah 17:4** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> And thou, even thyself, shalt discontinue from thine heritage that I gave thee; and I will cause thee to serve thine enemies in the land which thou knowest not: for ye have kindled a fire in mine anger, which shall burn for ever.

**Jeremiah 17:25** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Then shall there enter into the gates of this city kings and princes sitting upon the throne of David, riding in chariots and on horses, they, and their princes, the men of Judah, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem: and this city shall remain for ever.

**Jeremiah 18:15** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Because my people hath forgotten me, they have burned incense to vanity, and they have caused them to stumble in their ways from the ancient paths, to walk in paths, in a way not cast up;

**Jeremiah 18:16** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> To make their land desolate, and a perpetual hissing; every one that passeth thereby shall be astonished, and wag his head.

**Jeremiah 20:11** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> But the LORD is with me as a mighty terrible one: therefore my persecutors shall stumble, and they shall not prevail: they shall be greatly ashamed; for they shall not prosper: their everlasting confusion shall never be forgotten.

**Jeremiah 20:17** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Because he slew me not from the womb; or that my mother might have been my grave, and her womb to be always great with me.

**Jeremiah 23:40** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> And I will bring an everlasting reproach upon you, and a perpetual shame, which shall not be forgotten.

**Jeremiah 25:5** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> They said, Turn ye again now every one from his evil way, and from the evil of your doings, and dwell in the land that the LORD hath given unto you and to your fathers for ever and ever:

**Jeremiah 25:9** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Behold, I will send and take all the families of the north, saith the LORD, and Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon, my servant, and will bring them against this land, and against the inhabitants thereof, and against all these nations round about, and will utterly destroy them, and make them an astonishment, and an hissing, and perpetual desolations.

**Jeremiah 25:12** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> And it shall come to pass, when seventy years are accomplished, that I will punish the king of Babylon, and that nation, saith the LORD, for their iniquity, and the land of the Chaldeans, and will make it perpetual desolations.

**Jeremiah 28:8** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> The prophets that have been before me and before thee of old prophesied both against many countries, and against great kingdoms, of war, and of evil, and of pestilence.

**Jeremiah 31:3** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> The LORD hath appeared of old unto me, saying, Yea, I have loved thee with an everlasting love: therefore with lovingkindness have I drawn thee.

**Jeremiah 31:40** — `עֹולָֽם׃ ס` (eternity)
> And the whole valley of the dead bodies, and of the ashes, and all the fields unto the brook of Kidron, unto the corner of the horse gate toward the east, shall be holy unto the LORD; it shall not be plucked up, nor thrown down any more for ever.

**Jeremiah 32:40** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> And I will make an everlasting covenant with them, that I will not turn away from them, to do them good; but I will put my fear in their hearts, that they shall not depart from me.

**Jeremiah 33:11** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> The voice of joy, and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom, and the voice of the bride, the voice of them that shall say, Praise the LORD of hosts: for the LORD is good; for his mercy endureth for ever: and of them that shall bring the sacrifice of praise into the house of the LORD. For I will cause to return the captivity of the land, as at the first, saith the LORD.

**Jeremiah 35:6** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> But they said, We will drink no wine: for Jonadab the son of Rechab our father commanded us, saying, Ye shall drink no wine, neither ye, nor your sons for ever:

**Jeremiah 49:13** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> For I have sworn by myself, saith the LORD, that Bozrah shall become a desolation, a reproach, a waste, and a curse; and all the cities thereof shall be perpetual wastes.

**Jeremiah 49:33** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> And Hazor shall be a dwelling for dragons, and a desolation for ever: there shall no man abide there, nor any son of man dwell in it.

**Jeremiah 50:5** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> They shall ask the way to Zion with their faces thitherward, saying, Come, and let us join ourselves to the LORD in a perpetual covenant that shall not be forgotten.

**Jeremiah 51:26** — `עֹולָ֛ם` (eternity)
> And they shall not take of thee a stone for a corner, nor a stone for foundations; but thou shalt be desolate for ever, saith the LORD.

**Jeremiah 51:39** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> In their heat I will make their feasts, and I will make them drunken, that they may rejoice, and sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake, saith the LORD.

**Jeremiah 51:57** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> And I will make drunk her princes, and her wise men, her captains, and her rulers, and her mighty men: and they shall sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake, saith the King, whose name is the LORD of hosts.

**Jeremiah 51:62** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Then shalt thou say, O LORD, thou hast spoken against this place, to cut it off, that none shall remain in it, neither man nor beast, but that it shall be desolate for ever.

### Lamentations (3 verses)

**Lamentations 3:6** — `עֹולָֽם׃ ס` (eternity)
> He hath set me in dark places, as they that be dead of old.

**Lamentations 3:31** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> For the Lord will not cast off for ever:

**Lamentations 5:19** — `עֹולָ֣ם` (eternity)
> Thou, O LORD, remainest for ever; thy throne from generation to generation.

### Ezekiel (15 verses)

**Ezekiel 16:60** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Nevertheless I will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth, and I will establish unto thee an everlasting covenant.

**Ezekiel 25:15** — `עֹולָֽם׃` (eternity)
> Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because the Philistines have dealt by revenge, and have taken vengeance with a despiteful heart, to destroy it for the old hatred;

**Ezekiel 26:20** — `עֹולָ֗ם` (eternity)
> When I shall bring thee down with them that descend into the pit, with the people of old time, and shall set thee in the low parts of the earth, in places desolate of old, with them that go down to the pit, that thou be not inhabited; and I shall set glory in the land of the living;

**Ezekiel 26:21** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> I will make thee a terror, and thou shalt be no more: though thou be sought for, yet shalt thou never be found again, saith the Lord GOD.

**Ezekiel 27:36** — `עֹולָֽם׃ ס` (eternity)
> The merchants among the people shall hiss at thee; thou shalt be a terror, and never shalt be any more.

**Ezekiel 28:19** — `עֹולָֽם׃ פ` (eternity)
> All they that know thee among the people shall be astonished at thee: thou shalt be a terror, and never shalt thou be any more.

**Ezekiel 35:5** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Because thou hast had a perpetual hatred, and hast shed the blood of the children of Israel by the force of the sword in the time of their calamity, in the time that their iniquity had an end:

**Ezekiel 35:9** — `עֹולָם֙` (eternity)
> I will make thee perpetual desolations, and thy cities shall not return: and ye shall know that I am the LORD.

**Ezekiel 36:2** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because the enemy hath said against you, Aha, even the ancient high places are ours in possession:

**Ezekiel 37:25** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> And they shall dwell in the land that I have given unto Jacob my servant, wherein your fathers have dwelt; and they shall dwell therein, even they, and their children, and their children's children for ever: and my servant David shall be their prince for ever.

**Ezekiel 37:26** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> Moreover I will make a covenant of peace with them; it shall be an everlasting covenant with them: and I will place them, and multiply them, and will set my sanctuary in the midst of them for evermore.

**Ezekiel 37:28** — `עֹולָֽם׃ ס` (eternity)
> And the heathen shall know that I the LORD do sanctify Israel, when my sanctuary shall be in the midst of them for evermore.

**Ezekiel 43:7** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> And he said unto me, Son of man, the place of my throne, and the place of the soles of my feet, where I will dwell in the midst of the children of Israel for ever, and my holy name, shall the house of Israel no more defile, neither they, nor their kings, by their whoredom, nor by the carcases of their kings in their high places.

**Ezekiel 43:9** — `עֹולָֽם׃ ס` (eternity)
> Now let them put away their whoredom, and the carcases of their kings, far from me, and I will dwell in the midst of them for ever.

**Ezekiel 46:14** — `עֹולָ֖ם` (eternity)
> And thou shalt prepare a meat offering for it every morning, the sixth part of an ephah, and the third part of an hin of oil, to temper with the fine flour; a meat offering continually by a perpetual ordinance unto the LORD.

### Daniel (4 verses)

**Daniel 9:24** — `עֹֽלָמִ֑ים` (eternity)
> Seventy weeks are determined upon thy people and upon thy holy city, to finish the transgression, and to make an end of sins, and to make reconciliation for iniquity, and to bring in everlasting righteousness, and to seal up the vision and prophecy, and to anoint the most Holy.

**Daniel 12:2** — `עֹולָ֔ם` (eternity)
> And many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame and everlasting contempt.

**Daniel 12:3** — `עֹולָ֥ם` (eternity)
> And they that be wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament; and they that turn many to righteousness as the stars for ever and ever.

**Daniel 12:7** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> And I heard the man clothed in linen, which was upon the waters of the river, when he held up his right hand and his left hand unto heaven, and sware by him that liveth for ever that it shall be for a time, times, and an half; and when he shall have accomplished to scatter the power of the holy people, all these things shall be finished.

### Hosea (1 verses)

**Hosea 2:19** — `עֹולָ֑ם` (eternity)
> And I will betroth thee unto me for ever; yea, I will betroth thee unto me in righteousness, and in judgment, and in lovingkindness, and in mercies.

Belteshazzar answered and said, My lord, the dream be to them that hate thee, and the interpretation thereof to thine enemies.

# Part II — Greek (New Testament)

## `G2316` — Theos ("God")

> Standard Greek word for God — used throughout NT for the Father, and in OT quotations.

**1,148 verses found.**

### Matthew (43 verses)

**Matthew 1:23** — `θεός` (God)
> Behold, a virgin shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel, which being interpreted is, God with us.

**Matthew 3:9** — `θεὸς` (God)

> No servant can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.

**Luke 16:15** — `θεὸς` (God)
> And he said unto them, Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God.

**Luke 16:16** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> The law and the prophets were until John: since that time the kingdom of God is preached, and every man presseth into it.

**Luke 17:15** — `θεόν` (God)
> And one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, and with a loud voice glorified God,

**Luke 17:18** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> There are not found that returned to give glory to God, save this stranger.

**Luke 17:20** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And when he was demanded of the Pharisees, when the kingdom of God should come, he answered them and said, The kingdom of God cometh not with observation:

**Luke 17:21** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Neither shall they say, Lo here! or, lo there! for, behold, the kingdom of God is within you.

**Luke 18:2** — `θεὸν` (God)
> Saying, There was in a city a judge, which feared not God, neither regarded man:

**Luke 18:4** — `θεὸν` (God)
> And he would not for a while: but afterward he said within himself, Though I fear not God, nor regard man;

**Luke 18:7** — `θεὸς` (God)
> And shall not God avenge his own elect, which cry day and night unto him, though he bear long with them?

> But Jesus called them unto him, and said, Suffer little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.

**Luke 18:17** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child shall in no wise enter therein.

**Luke 18:19** — `θεός` (God)
> And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? none is good, save one, that is, God.

> And when Jesus saw that he was very sorrowful, he said, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!

**Luke 18:25** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For it is easier for a camel to go through a needle's eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.

**Luke 18:27** — `θεῷ` (God)
> And he said, The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.

**Luke 18:29** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children, for the kingdom of God's sake,

**Luke 18:43** — `θεόν` (God)
> And immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God: and all the people, when they saw it, gave praise unto God.

**Luke 19:11** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And as they heard these things, he added and spake a parable, because he was nigh to Jerusalem, and because they thought that the kingdom of God should immediately appear.

**Luke 20:21** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And they asked him, saying, Master, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, neither acceptest thou the person of any, but teachest the way of God truly:

**Luke 20:25** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And he said unto them, Render therefore unto Cæsar the things which be Cæsar's, and unto God the things which be God's.

> Neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the angels; and are the children of God, being the children of the resurrection.

**Luke 20:37** — `θεὸν` (God)
> Now that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.

**Luke 20:38** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For he is not a God of the dead, but of the living: for all live unto him.

> So likewise ye, when ye see these things come to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is nigh at hand.

> For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.

**Luke 22:18** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For I say unto you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.

> Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand of the power of God.

**Luke 22:70** — `θεοῦ` (of God)

### John (69 verses)

**John 1:1** — `θεόν` (God)

> There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.

> Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.

**John 3:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.

**John 3:16** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.

**John 3:17** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved.

**John 3:18** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.

> But he that doeth truth cometh to the light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.

> He that hath received his testimony hath set to his seal that God is true.

**John 3:34** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For he whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God: for God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him.

**John 3:36** — `θεοῦ` (of God)

> God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.

> But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you.
