**Ephesians 2:19** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Now therefore ye are no more strangers and foreigners, but fellowcitizens with the saints, and of the household of God;

**Ephesians 2:22** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> In whom ye also are builded together for an habitation of God through the Spirit.

**Ephesians 3:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> If ye have heard of the dispensation of the grace of God which is given me to you-ward:

**Ephesians 3:7** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Whereof I was made a minister, according to the gift of the grace of God given unto me by the effectual working of his power.

**Ephesians 3:9** — `θεῷ` (God)
> And to make all men see what is the fellowship of the mystery, which from the beginning of the world hath been hid in God, who created all things by Jesus Christ:

**Ephesians 3:10** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> To the intent that now unto the principalities and powers in heavenly places might be known by the church the manifold wisdom of God,

**Ephesians 3:19** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And to know the love of Christ, which passeth knowledge, that ye might be filled with all the fulness of God.

**Ephesians 4:6** — `θεὸς` (God)
> One God and Father of all, who is above all, and through all, and in you all.

**Ephesians 4:13** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:

**Ephesians 4:18** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:

**Ephesians 4:24** — `θεὸν` (God)
> And that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness.

**Ephesians 4:30** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption.

**Ephesians 4:32** — `θεὸς` (God)
> And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.

**Ephesians 5:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Be ye therefore followers of God, as dear children;

**Ephesians 5:2** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> And walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour.

**Ephesians 5:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For this ye know, that no whoremonger, nor unclean person, nor covetous man, who is an idolater, hath any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God.

**Ephesians 5:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Let no man deceive you with vain words: for because of these things cometh the wrath of God upon the children of disobedience.

**Ephesians 5:20** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Giving thanks always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ;

**Ephesians 6:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but as the servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart;

**Ephesians 6:11** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.

**Ephesians 6:13** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Wherefore take unto you the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.

**Ephesians 6:17** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God:

**Ephesians 6:23** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Peace be to the brethren, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

### Philippians (22 verses)

**Philippians 1:2** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

**Philippians 1:3** — `θεῷ` (God)
> I thank my God upon every remembrance of you,

**Philippians 1:8** — `θεός` (God [is])
> For God is my record, how greatly I long after you all in the bowels of Jesus Christ.

**Philippians 1:11** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Being filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are by Jesus Christ, unto the glory and praise of God.

**Philippians 1:28** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> And in nothing terrified by your adversaries: which is to them an evident token of perdition, but to you of salvation, and that of God.

**Philippians 2:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God:

**Philippians 2:9** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above every name:

**Philippians 2:11** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

**Philippians 2:13** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure.

**Philippians 2:15** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> That ye may be blameless and harmless, the sons of God, without rebuke, in the midst of a crooked and perverse nation, among whom ye shine as lights in the world;

**Philippians 2:27** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For indeed he was sick nigh unto death: but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.

**Philippians 3:3** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For we are the circumcision, which worship God in the spirit, and rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have no confidence in the flesh.

**Philippians 3:9** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> And be found in him, not having mine own righteousness, which is of the law, but that which is through the faith of Christ, the righteousness which is of God by faith:

**Philippians 3:14** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> I press toward the mark for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.

**Philippians 3:15** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Let us therefore, as many as be perfect, be thus minded: and if in any thing ye be otherwise minded, God shall reveal even this unto you.

**Philippians 3:19** — `θεὸς` (God [is])
> Whose end is destruction, whose God is their belly, and whose glory is in their shame, who mind earthly things.)

**Philippians 4:6** — `θεόν` (God)
> Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.

**Philippians 4:7** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.

**Philippians 4:9** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Those things, which ye have both learned, and received, and heard, and seen in me, do: and the God of peace shall be with you.

**Philippians 4:18** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> But I have all, and abound: I am full, having received of Epaphroditus the things which were sent from you, an odour of a sweet smell, a sacrifice acceptable, wellpleasing to God.

**Philippians 4:19** — `θεός` ([the] God)
> But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus.

**Philippians 4:20** — `θεῷ` ([the] God)
> Now unto God and our Father be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

### Colossians (20 verses)

**Colossians 1:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timotheus our brother,

**Colossians 1:2** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ which are at Colosse: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

**Colossians 1:3** — `θεῷ` (God)
> We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,

**Colossians 1:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Which is come unto you, as it is in all the world; and bringeth forth fruit, as it doth also in you, since the day ye heard of it, and knew the grace of God in truth:

**Colossians 1:10** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing, being fruitful in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;

**Colossians 1:15** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature:

**Colossians 1:25** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Whereof I am made a minister, according to the dispensation of God which is given to me for you, to fulfil the word of God;

**Colossians 1:27** — `θεὸς` (God)
> To whom God would make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; which is Christ in you, the hope of glory:

**Colossians 2:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> That their hearts might be comforted, being knit together in love, and unto all riches of the full assurance of understanding, to the acknowledgement of the mystery of God, and of the Father, and of Christ;

**Colossians 2:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Buried with him in baptism, wherein also ye are risen with him through the faith of the operation of God, who hath raised him from the dead.

**Colossians 2:19** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And not holding the Head, from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.

**Colossians 3:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.

**Colossians 3:3** — `θεῷ` (God)
> For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.

**Colossians 3:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For which things' sake the wrath of God cometh on the children of disobedience:

**Colossians 3:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Put on therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, bowels of mercies, kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering;

**Colossians 3:16** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.

**Colossians 3:17** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.

**Colossians 4:3** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Withal praying also for us, that God would open unto us a door of utterance, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds:

**Colossians 4:11** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And Jesus, which is called Justus, who are of the circumcision. These only are my fellowworkers unto the kingdom of God, which have been a comfort unto me.

**Colossians 4:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, saluteth you, always labouring fervently for you in prayers, that ye may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.

### 1 Thessalonians (30 verses)

**1 Thessalonians 1:1** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians which is in God the Father and in the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.

**1 Thessalonians 1:2** — `θεῷ` (God)
> We give thanks to God always for you all, making mention of you in our prayers;

**1 Thessalonians 1:3** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Remembering without ceasing your work of faith, and labour of love, and patience of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, in the sight of God and our Father;

**1 Thessalonians 1:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Knowing, brethren beloved, your election of God.

**1 Thessalonians 1:8** — `θεὸν` (God)
> For from you sounded out the word of the Lord not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith to God-ward is spread abroad; so that we need not to speak any thing.

**1 Thessalonians 1:9** — `θεὸν` (God)
> For they themselves shew of us what manner of entering in we had unto you, and how ye turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God;

**1 Thessalonians 2:2** — `θεῷ` (God)
> But even after that we had suffered before, and were shamefully entreated, as ye know, at Philippi, we were bold in our God to speak unto you the gospel of God with much contention.

**1 Thessalonians 2:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> But as we were allowed of God to be put in trust with the gospel, even so we speak; not as pleasing men, but God, which trieth our hearts.

**1 Thessalonians 2:5** — `θεὸς` (God [is])
> For neither at any time used we flattering words, as ye know, nor a cloke of covetousness; God is witness:

**1 Thessalonians 2:8** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> So being affectionately desirous of you, we were willing to have imparted unto you, not the gospel of God only, but also our own souls, because ye were dear unto us.

**1 Thessalonians 2:9** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For ye remember, brethren, our labour and travail: for labouring night and day, because we would not be chargeable unto any of you, we preached unto you the gospel of God.

**1 Thessalonians 2:10** — `θεός` (God)
> Ye are witnesses, and God also, how holily and justly and unblameably we behaved ourselves among you that believe:

**1 Thessalonians 2:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> That ye would walk worthy of God, who hath called you unto his kingdom and glory.

**1 Thessalonians 2:13** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> For this cause also thank we God without ceasing, because, when ye received the word of God which ye heard of us, ye received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God, which effectually worketh also in you that believe.

**1 Thessalonians 2:14** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For ye, brethren, became followers of the churches of God which in Judaea are in Christ Jesus: for ye also have suffered like things of your own countrymen, even as they have of the Jews:

**1 Thessalonians 2:15** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Who both killed the Lord Jesus, and their own prophets, and have persecuted us; and they please not God, and are contrary to all men:

**1 Thessalonians 3:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And sent Timotheus, our brother, and minister of God, and our fellowlabourer in the gospel of Christ, to establish you, and to comfort you concerning your faith:

**1 Thessalonians 3:9** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> For what thanks can we render to God again for you, for all the joy wherewith we joy for your sakes before our God;

**1 Thessalonians 3:11** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Now God himself and our Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way unto you.

**1 Thessalonians 3:13** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> To the end he may stablish your hearts unblameable in holiness before God, even our Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his saints.

**1 Thessalonians 4:1** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Furthermore then we beseech you, brethren, and exhort you by the Lord Jesus, that as ye have received of us how ye ought to walk and to please God, so ye would abound more and more.

**1 Thessalonians 4:3** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For this is the will of God, even your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication:

**1 Thessalonians 4:5** — `θεόν` (God)
> Not in the lust of concupiscence, even as the Gentiles which know not God:

**1 Thessalonians 4:7** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For God hath not called us unto uncleanness, but unto holiness.

**1 Thessalonians 4:8** — `θεὸν` (God)
> He therefore that despiseth, despiseth not man, but God, who hath also given unto us his holy Spirit.

**1 Thessalonians 4:14** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so them also which sleep in Jesus will God bring with him.

**1 Thessalonians 4:16** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:

**1 Thessalonians 5:9** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For God hath not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,

**1 Thessalonians 5:18** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.

**1 Thessalonians 5:23** — `θεὸς` (God)
> And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

### 2 Thessalonians (14 verses)

**2 Thessalonians 1:1** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:

**2 Thessalonians 1:2** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Grace unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

**2 Thessalonians 1:3** — `θεῷ` (God)
> We are bound to thank God always for you, brethren, as it is meet, because that your faith groweth exceedingly, and the charity of every one of you all toward each other aboundeth;

**2 Thessalonians 1:4** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> So that we ourselves glory in you in the churches of God for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that ye endure:

**2 Thessalonians 1:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Which is a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God, that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which ye also suffer:

**2 Thessalonians 1:6** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Seeing it is a righteous thing with God to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you;

**2 Thessalonians 1:8** — `θεὸν` (God)
> In flaming fire taking vengeance on them that know not God, and that obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ:

**2 Thessalonians 1:11** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Wherefore also we pray always for you, that our God would count you worthy of this calling, and fulfil all the good pleasure of his goodness, and the work of faith with power:

**2 Thessalonians 1:12** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and ye in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.

**2 Thessalonians 2:4** — `θεὸν` (god)
> Who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God.

**2 Thessalonians 2:11** — `θεὸς` (God)
> And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion, that they should believe a lie:

**2 Thessalonians 2:13** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> But we are bound to give thanks alway to God for you, brethren beloved of the Lord, because God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth:

**2 Thessalonians 2:16** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God, even our Father, which hath loved us, and hath given us everlasting consolation and good hope through grace,

**2 Thessalonians 3:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God, and into the patient waiting for Christ.

### 1 Timothy (20 verses)

**1 Timothy 1:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour, and Lord Jesus Christ, which is our hope;

**1 Timothy 1:2** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Unto Timothy, my own son in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.

**1 Timothy 1:4** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies, which minister questions, rather than godly edifying which is in faith: so do.

**1 Timothy 1:11** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.

**1 Timothy 1:17** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.

**1 Timothy 2:3** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;

**1 Timothy 2:5** — `θεός` (God [there is])
> For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus;

**1 Timothy 3:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For if a man know not how to rule his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?)

**1 Timothy 3:15** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.

**1 Timothy 4:3** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth.

**1 Timothy 4:4** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving:

**1 Timothy 4:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.

**1 Timothy 4:10** — `θεῷ` (God)
> For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.

**1 Timothy 5:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> But if any widow have children or nephews, let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.

**1 Timothy 5:5** — `θεὸν` (God)
> Now she that is a widow indeed, and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.

**1 Timothy 5:21** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> I charge thee before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the elect angels, that thou observe these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality.

**1 Timothy 6:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Let as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and his doctrine be not blasphemed.

**1 Timothy 6:11** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness.

**1 Timothy 6:13** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> I give thee charge in the sight of God, who quickeneth all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate witnessed a good confession;

**1 Timothy 6:17** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy;

### 2 Timothy (12 verses)

**2 Timothy 1:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the promise of life which is in Christ Jesus,

**2 Timothy 1:2** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Timothy, my dearly beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

**2 Timothy 1:3** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> I thank God, whom I serve from my forefathers with pure conscience, that without ceasing I have remembrance of thee in my prayers night and day;

**2 Timothy 1:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands.

**2 Timothy 1:7** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.

**2 Timothy 1:8** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of God;

**2 Timothy 2:9** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound.

**2 Timothy 2:15** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.

**2 Timothy 2:19** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity.

**2 Timothy 2:25** — `θεὸς` (God)
> In meekness instructing those that oppose themselves; if God peradventure will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth;

**2 Timothy 3:17** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> That the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works.

**2 Timothy 4:1** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;

### Titus (12 verses)

**Titus 1:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God's elect, and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness;

**Titus 1:2** — `θεὸς` (God)
> In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie, promised before the world began;

**Titus 1:3** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;

**Titus 1:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> To Titus, mine own son after the common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.

**Titus 1:7** — `θεοῦ` (God’s)
> For a bishop must be blameless, as the steward of God; not selfwilled, not soon angry, not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre;

**Titus 1:16** — `θεὸν` (God)
> They profess that they know God; but in works they deny him, being abominable, and disobedient, and unto every good work reprobate.

**Titus 2:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> To be discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.

**Titus 2:10** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Not purloining, but shewing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.

**Titus 2:11** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,

**Titus 2:13** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;

**Titus 3:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,

**Titus 3:8** — `θεῷ` (God)
> This is a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable unto men.

### Philemon (2 verses)

**Philemon 1:3** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

**Philemon 1:4** — `θεῷ` (God)
> I thank my God, making mention of thee always in my prayers,

### Hebrews (63 verses)

**Hebrews 1:1** — `θεὸς` (God)
> God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets,

**Hebrews 1:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And again, when he bringeth in the firstbegotten into the world, he saith, And let all the angels of God worship him.

**Hebrews 1:8** — `θεὸς` (God)
> But unto the Son he saith, Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever: a sceptre of righteousness is the sceptre of thy kingdom.

**Hebrews 1:9** — `θεός` (God)
> Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, even thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.

**Hebrews 2:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> God also bearing them witness, both with signs and wonders, and with divers miracles, and gifts of the Holy Ghost, according to his own will?

**Hebrews 2:9** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels for the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour; that he by the grace of God should taste death for every man.

**Hebrews 2:13** — `θεός` (God)
> And again, I will put my trust in him. And again, Behold I and the children which God hath given me.

**Hebrews 2:17** — `θεόν` (God)
> Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto his brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make reconciliation for the sins of the people.

**Hebrews 3:4** — `θεός` (God)
> For every house is builded by some man; but he that built all things is God.

**Hebrews 3:12** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief, in departing from the living God.

**Hebrews 4:4** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For he spake in a certain place of the seventh day on this wise, And God did rest the seventh day from all his works.

**Hebrews 4:9** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God.

**Hebrews 4:10** — `θεός` (God [did])
> For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God did from his.

**Hebrews 4:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For the word of God is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.

**Hebrews 4:14** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Seeing then that we have a great high priest, that is passed into the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast our profession.

**Hebrews 5:1** — `θεόν` (God)
> For every high priest taken from among men is ordained for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins:

**Hebrews 5:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> And no man taketh this honour unto himself, but he that is called of God, as was Aaron.

**Hebrews 5:10** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Called of God an high priest after the order of Melchisedec.

**Hebrews 5:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For when for the time ye ought to be teachers, ye have need that one teach you again which be the first principles of the oracles of God; and are become such as have need of milk, and not of strong meat.

**Hebrews 6:1** — `θεόν` (God)
> Therefore leaving the principles of the doctrine of Christ, let us go on unto perfection; not laying again the foundation of repentance from dead works, and of faith toward God,

**Hebrews 6:3** — `θεός` (God)
> And this will we do, if God permit.

**Hebrews 6:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God’s)
> And have tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the world to come,

**Hebrews 6:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> If they shall fall away, to renew them again unto repentance; seeing they crucify to themselves the Son of God afresh, and put him to an open shame.

**Hebrews 6:7** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> For the earth which drinketh in the rain that cometh oft upon it, and bringeth forth herbs meet for them by whom it is dressed, receiveth blessing from God:

**Hebrews 6:10** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For God is not unrighteous to forget your work and labour of love, which ye have shewed toward his name, in that ye have ministered to the saints, and do minister.

**Hebrews 6:13** — `θεός` (God)
> For when God made promise to Abraham, because he could swear by no greater, he sware by himself,

**Hebrews 6:17** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Wherein God, willing more abundantly to shew unto the heirs of promise the immutability of his counsel, confirmed it by an oath:

**Hebrews 6:18** — `θεόν` ([for] God)
> That by two immutable things, in which it was impossible for God to lie, we might have a strong consolation, who have fled for refuge to lay hold upon the hope set before us:

**Hebrews 7:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, priest of the most high God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him;

**Hebrews 7:3** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the Son of God; abideth a priest continually.

**Hebrews 7:19** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> For the law made nothing perfect, but the bringing in of a better hope did; by the which we draw nigh unto God.

**Hebrews 7:25** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> Wherefore he is able also to save them to the uttermost that come unto God by him, seeing he ever liveth to make intercession for them.

**Hebrews 8:10** — `θεόν` (God)
> For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, saith the Lord; I will put my laws into their mind, and write them in their hearts: and I will be to them a God, and they shall be to me a people:

**Hebrews 9:14** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> How much more shall the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without spot to God, purge your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?

**Hebrews 9:20** — `θεός` (God)
> Saying, This is the blood of the testament which God hath enjoined unto you.

**Hebrews 9:24** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For Christ is not entered into the holy places made with hands, which are the figures of the true; but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us:

**Hebrews 10:7** — `θεός` (O God)
> Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.

**Hebrews 10:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> But this man, after he had offered one sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God;

**Hebrews 10:21** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And having an high priest over the house of God;

**Hebrews 10:29** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace?

**Hebrews 10:31** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

**Hebrews 10:36** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.

**Hebrews 11:3** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Through faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.

**Hebrews 11:4** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> By faith Abel offered unto God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, by which he obtained witness that he was righteous, God testifying of his gifts: and by it he being dead yet speaketh.

**Hebrews 11:5** — `θεός` (God)
> By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and was not found, because God had translated him: for before his translation he had this testimony, that he pleased God.

**Hebrews 11:6** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.

**Hebrews 11:10** — `θεός` (God)
> For he looked for a city which hath foundations, whose builder and maker is God.

**Hebrews 11:16** — `θεὸς` (God)
> But now they desire a better country, that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.

**Hebrews 11:19** — `θεός` (God)
> Accounting that God was able to raise him up, even from the dead; from whence also he received him in a figure.

**Hebrews 11:25** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;

**Hebrews 11:40** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> God having provided some better thing for us, that they without us should not be made perfect.

**Hebrews 12:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.

**Hebrews 12:7** — `θεός` (God)
> If ye endure chastening, God dealeth with you as with sons; for what son is he whom the father chasteneth not?

**Hebrews 12:15** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and thereby many be defiled;

**Hebrews 12:22** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> But ye are come unto mount Sion, and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to an innumerable company of angels,

**Hebrews 12:23** — `θεῷ` (God)
> To the general assembly and church of the firstborn, which are written in heaven, and to God the Judge of all, and to the spirits of just men made perfect,

**Hebrews 12:28** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Wherefore we receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved, let us have grace, whereby we may serve God acceptably with reverence and godly fear:

**Hebrews 12:29** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For our God is a consuming fire.

**Hebrews 13:4** — `θεός` (God)
> Marriage is honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge.

**Hebrews 13:7** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Remember them which have the rule over you, who have spoken unto you the word of God: whose faith follow, considering the end of their conversation.

**Hebrews 13:15** — `θεῷ` (God)
> By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of our lips giving thanks to his name.

**Hebrews 13:16** — `θεός` (God)
> But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

**Hebrews 13:20** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Now the God of peace, that brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great shepherd of the sheep, through the blood of the everlasting covenant,

### James (13 verses)

**James 1:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are scattered abroad, greeting.

**James 1:5** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him.

**James 1:13** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Let no man say when he is tempted, I am tempted of God: for God cannot be tempted with evil, neither tempteth he any man:

**James 1:20** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.

**James 1:27** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Pure religion and undefiled before God and the Father is this, To visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, and to keep himself unspotted from the world.

**James 2:5** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Hearken, my beloved brethren, Hath not God chosen the poor of this world rich in faith, and heirs of the kingdom which he hath promised to them that love him?

**James 2:19** — `θεός` (God)
> Thou believest that there is one God; thou doest well: the devils also believe, and tremble.

**James 2:23** — `θεῷ` (God)
> And the scripture was fulfilled which saith, Abraham believed God, and it was imputed unto him for righteousness: and he was called the Friend of God.

**James 3:9** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Therewith bless we God, even the Father; and therewith curse we men, which are made after the similitude of God.

**James 4:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Ye adulterers and adulteresses, know ye not that the friendship of the world is enmity with God? whosoever therefore will be a friend of the world is the enemy of God.

**James 4:6** — `θεὸς` (God)
> But he giveth more grace. Wherefore he saith, God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble.

**James 4:7** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.

**James 4:8** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you. Cleanse your hands, ye sinners; and purify your hearts, ye double minded.

### 1 Peter (34 verses)

**1 Peter 1:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, through sanctification of the Spirit, unto obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ: Grace unto you, and peace, be multiplied.

**1 Peter 1:3** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which according to his abundant mercy hath begotten us again unto a lively hope by the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,

**1 Peter 1:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Who are kept by the power of God through faith unto salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.

**1 Peter 1:21** — `θεὸν` (God)
> Who by him do believe in God, that raised him up from the dead, and gave him glory; that your faith and hope might be in God.

**1 Peter 1:23** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Being born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever.

**1 Peter 2:4** — `θεῷ` (God)
> To whom coming, as unto a living stone, disallowed indeed of men, but chosen of God, and precious,

**1 Peter 2:5** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> Ye also, as lively stones, are built up a spiritual house, an holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God by Jesus Christ.

**1 Peter 2:10** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Which in time past were not a people, but are now the people of God: which had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy.

**1 Peter 2:12** — `θεὸν` (God)
> Having your conversation honest among the Gentiles: that, whereas they speak against you as evildoers, they may by your good works, which they shall behold, glorify God in the day of visitation.

**1 Peter 2:15** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For so is the will of God, that with well doing ye may put to silence the ignorance of foolish men:

**1 Peter 2:16** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> As free, and not using your liberty for a cloke of maliciousness, but as the servants of God.

**1 Peter 2:17** — `θεὸν` (God)
> Honour all men. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honour the king.

**1 Peter 2:19** — `θεοῦ` (toward God)
> For this is thankworthy, if a man for conscience toward God endure grief, suffering wrongfully.

**1 Peter 2:20** — `θεῷ` (God)
> For what glory is it, if, when ye be buffeted for your faults, ye shall take it patiently? but if, when ye do well, and suffer for it, ye take it patiently, this is acceptable with God.

**1 Peter 3:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> But let it be the hidden man of the heart, in that which is not corruptible, even the ornament of a meek and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God of great price.

**1 Peter 3:5** — `θεὸν` (God)
> For after this manner in the old time the holy women also, who trusted in God, adorned themselves, being in subjection unto their own husbands:

**1 Peter 3:17** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For it is better, if the will of God be so, that ye suffer for well doing, than for evil doing.

**1 Peter 3:18** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> For Christ also hath once suffered for sins, the just for the unjust, that he might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh, but quickened by the Spirit:

**1 Peter 3:20** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Which sometime were disobedient, when once the longsuffering of God waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was a preparing, wherein few, that is, eight souls were saved by water.

**1 Peter 3:21** — `θεόν` (God)
> The like figure whereunto even baptism doth also now save us (not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God,) by the resurrection of Jesus Christ:

**1 Peter 3:22** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Who is gone into heaven, and is on the right hand of God; angels and authorities and powers being made subject unto him.

**1 Peter 4:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> That he no longer should live the rest of his time in the flesh to the lusts of men, but to the will of God.

**1 Peter 4:6** — `θεὸν` (God)
> For for this cause was the gospel preached also to them that are dead, that they might be judged according to men in the flesh, but live according to God in the spirit.

**1 Peter 4:10** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> As every man hath received the gift, even so minister the same one to another, as good stewards of the manifold grace of God.

**1 Peter 4:11** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> If any man speak, let him speak as the oracles of God; if any man minister, let him do it as of the ability which God giveth: that God in all things may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom be praise and dominion for ever and ever. Amen.

**1 Peter 4:14** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> If ye be reproached for the name of Christ, happy are ye; for the spirit of glory and of God resteth upon you: on their part he is evil spoken of, but on your part he is glorified.

**1 Peter 4:16** — `θεὸν` (God)
> Yet if any man suffer as a Christian, let him not be ashamed; but let him glorify God on this behalf.

**1 Peter 4:17** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For the time is come that judgment must begin at the house of God: and if it first begin at us, what shall the end be of them that obey not the gospel of God?

**1 Peter 4:19** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Wherefore let them that suffer according to the will of God commit the keeping of their souls to him in well doing, as unto a faithful Creator.

**1 Peter 5:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Feed the flock of God which is among you, taking the oversight thereof, not by constraint, but willingly; not for filthy lucre, but of a ready mind;

**1 Peter 5:5** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Likewise, ye younger, submit yourselves unto the elder. Yea, all of you be subject one to another, and be clothed with humility: for God resisteth the proud, and giveth grace to the humble.

**1 Peter 5:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time:

**1 Peter 5:10** — `θεὸς` (God)
> But the God of all grace, who hath called us unto his eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after that ye have suffered a while, make you perfect, stablish, strengthen, settle you.

**1 Peter 5:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> By Silvanus, a faithful brother unto you, as I suppose, I have written briefly, exhorting, and testifying that this is the true grace of God wherein ye stand.

### 2 Peter (7 verses)

**2 Peter 1:1** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Simon Peter, a servant and an apostle of Jesus Christ, to them that have obtained like precious faith with us through the righteousness of God and our Saviour Jesus Christ:

**2 Peter 1:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Grace and peace be multiplied unto you through the knowledge of God, and of Jesus our Lord,

**2 Peter 1:17** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> For he received from God the Father honour and glory, when there came such a voice to him from the excellent glory, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.

**2 Peter 1:21** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> For the prophecy came not in old time by the will of man: but holy men of God spake as they were moved by the Holy Ghost.

**2 Peter 2:4** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For if God spared not the angels that sinned, but cast them down to hell, and delivered them into chains of darkness, to be reserved unto judgment;

**2 Peter 3:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For this they willingly are ignorant of, that by the word of God the heavens were of old, and the earth standing out of the water and in the water:

**2 Peter 3:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Looking for and hasting unto the coming of the day of God, wherein the heavens being on fire shall be dissolved, and the elements shall melt with fervent heat?

### 1 John (40 verses)

**1 John 1:5** — `θεὸς` (God)
> This then is the message which we have heard of him, and declare unto you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.

**1 John 2:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> But whoso keepeth his word, in him verily is the love of God perfected: hereby know we that we are in him.

**1 John 2:14** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> I have written unto you, fathers, because ye have known him that is from the beginning. I have written unto you, young men, because ye are strong, and the word of God abideth in you, and ye have overcome the wicked one.

**1 John 2:17** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And the world passeth away, and the lust thereof: but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever.

**1 John 3:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Behold, what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called the sons of God: therefore the world knoweth us not, because it knew him not.

**1 John 3:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that, when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is.

**1 John 3:8** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> He that committeth sin is of the devil; for the devil sinneth from the beginning. For this purpose the Son of God was manifested, that he might destroy the works of the devil.

**1 John 3:9** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Whosoever is born of God doth not commit sin; for his seed remaineth in him: and he cannot sin, because he is born of God.

**1 John 3:10** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> In this the children of God are manifest, and the children of the devil: whosoever doeth not righteousness is not of God, neither he that loveth not his brother.

**1 John 3:17** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> But whoso hath this world's good, and seeth his brother have need, and shutteth up his bowels of compassion from him, how dwelleth the love of God in him?

**1 John 3:20** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For if our heart condemn us, God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things.

**1 John 3:21** — `θεόν` (God)
> Beloved, if our heart condemn us not, then have we confidence toward God.

**1 John 4:1** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Beloved, believe not every spirit, but try the spirits whether they are of God: because many false prophets are gone out into the world.

**1 John 4:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Hereby know ye the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is of God:

**1 John 4:3** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> And every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is not of God: and this is that spirit of antichrist, whereof ye have heard that it should come; and even now already is it in the world.

**1 John 4:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Ye are of God, little children, and have overcome them: because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world.

**1 John 4:6** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> We are of God: he that knoweth God heareth us; he that is not of God heareth not us. Hereby know we the spirit of truth, and the spirit of error.

**1 John 4:7** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Beloved, let us love one another: for love is of God; and every one that loveth is born of God, and knoweth God.

**1 John 4:8** — `θεόν` (God)
> He that loveth not knoweth not God; for God is love.

**1 John 4:9** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> In this was manifested the love of God toward us, because that God sent his only begotten Son into the world, that we might live through him.

**1 John 4:10** — `θεόν` (God)
> Herein is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins.

**1 John 4:11** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Beloved, if God so loved us, we ought also to love one another.

**1 John 4:12** — `θεὸν` (God)
> No man hath seen God at any time. If we love one another, God dwelleth in us, and his love is perfected in us.

**1 John 4:15** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him, and he in God.

**1 John 4:16** — `θεὸς` (God)
> And we have known and believed the love that God hath to us. God is love; and he that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God, and God in him.

**1 John 4:20** — `θεόν` (God)
> If a man say, I love God, and hateth his brother, he is a liar: for he that loveth not his brother whom he hath seen, how can he love God whom he hath not seen?

**1 John 4:21** — `θεὸν` (God)
> And this commandment have we from him, That he who loveth God love his brother also.

**1 John 5:1** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Whosoever believeth that Jesus is the Christ is born of God: and every one that loveth him that begat loveth him also that is begotten of him.

**1 John 5:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God, and keep his commandments.

**1 John 5:3** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> For this is the love of God, that we keep his commandments: and his commandments are not grievous.

**1 John 5:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> For whatsoever is born of God overcometh the world: and this is the victory that overcometh the world, even our faith.

**1 John 5:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Who is he that overcometh the world, but he that believeth that Jesus is the Son of God?

**1 John 5:9** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater: for this is the witness of God which he hath testified of his Son.

**1 John 5:10** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> He that believeth on the Son of God hath the witness in himself: he that believeth not God hath made him a liar; because he believeth not the record that God gave of his Son.

**1 John 5:11** — `θεὸς` (God)
> And this is the record, that God hath given to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.

**1 John 5:12** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> He that hath the Son hath life; and he that hath not the Son of God hath not life.

**1 John 5:13** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> These things have I written unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal life, and that ye may believe on the name of the Son of God.

**1 John 5:18** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> We know that whosoever is born of God sinneth not; but he that is begotten of God keepeth himself, and that wicked one toucheth him not.

**1 John 5:19** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> And we know that we are of God, and the whole world lieth in wickedness.

**1 John 5:20** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And we know that the Son of God is come, and hath given us an understanding, that we may know him that is true, and we are in him that is true, even in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God, and eternal life.

### 2 John (2 verses)

**2 John 1:3** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Grace be with you, mercy, and peace, from God the Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.

**2 John 1:9** — `θεὸν` (God)
> Whosoever transgresseth, and abideth not in the doctrine of Christ, hath not God. He that abideth in the doctrine of Christ, he hath both the Father and the Son.

### 3 John (2 verses)

**3 John 1:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Which have borne witness of thy charity before the church: whom if thou bring forward on their journey after a godly sort, thou shalt do well:

**3 John 1:11** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Beloved, follow not that which is evil, but that which is good. He that doeth good is of God: but he that doeth evil hath not seen God.

### Jude (4 verses)

**Jude 1:1** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Jude, the servant of Jesus Christ, and brother of James, to them that are sanctified by God the Father, and preserved in Jesus Christ, and called:

**Jude 1:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> For there are certain men crept in unawares, who were before of old ordained to this condemnation, ungodly men, turning the grace of our God into lasciviousness, and denying the only Lord God, and our Lord Jesus Christ.

**Jude 1:21** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Keep yourselves in the love of God, looking for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ unto eternal life.

**Jude 1:25** — `θεῷ` (God)
> To the only wise God our Saviour, be glory and majesty, dominion and power, both now and ever. Amen.

### Revelation (87 verses)

**Revelation 1:1** — `θεὸς` (God)
> The Revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave unto him, to shew unto his servants things which must shortly come to pass; and he sent and signified it by his angel unto his servant John:

**Revelation 1:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Who bare record of the word of God, and of the testimony of Jesus Christ, and of all things that he saw.

**Revelation 1:6** — `θεῷ` (God)
> And hath made us kings and priests unto God and his Father; to him be glory and dominion for ever and ever. Amen.

**Revelation 1:8** — `θεός` (God)
> I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the ending, saith the Lord, which is, and which was, and which is to come, the Almighty.

**Revelation 1:9** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> I John, who also am your brother, and companion in tribulation, and in the kingdom and patience of Jesus Christ, was in the isle that is called Patmos, for the word of God, and for the testimony of Jesus Christ.

**Revelation 2:7** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches; To him that overcometh will I give to eat of the tree of life, which is in the midst of the paradise of God.

**Revelation 2:18** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And unto the angel of the church in Thyatira write; These things saith the Son of God, who hath his eyes like unto a flame of fire, and his feet are like fine brass;

**Revelation 3:1** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And unto the angel of the church in Sardis write; These things saith he that hath the seven Spirits of God, and the seven stars; I know thy works, that thou hast a name that thou livest, and art dead.

**Revelation 3:2** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Be watchful, and strengthen the things which remain, that are ready to die: for I have not found thy works perfect before God.

**Revelation 3:12** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Him that overcometh will I make a pillar in the temple of my God, and he shall go no more out: and I will write upon him the name of my God, and the name of the city of my God, which is new Jerusalem, which cometh down out of heaven from my God: and I will write upon him my new name.

**Revelation 3:14** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And unto the angel of the church of the Laodiceans write; These things saith the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of the creation of God;

**Revelation 4:5** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And out of the throne proceeded lightnings and thunderings and voices: and there were seven lamps of fire burning before the throne, which are the seven Spirits of God.

**Revelation 4:8** — `θεός` (God)
> And the four beasts had each of them six wings about him; and they were full of eyes within: and they rest not day and night, saying, Holy, holy, holy, Lord God Almighty, which was, and is, and is to come.

**Revelation 4:11** — `θεὸς` (God)
> Thou art worthy, O Lord, to receive glory and honour and power: for thou hast created all things, and for thy pleasure they are and were created.

**Revelation 5:6** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And I beheld, and, lo, in the midst of the throne and of the four beasts, and in the midst of the elders, stood a Lamb as it had been slain, having seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God sent forth into all the earth.

**Revelation 5:9** — `θεῷ` (to God)
> And they sung a new song, saying, Thou art worthy to take the book, and to open the seals thereof: for thou wast slain, and hast redeemed us to God by thy blood out of every kindred, and tongue, and people, and nation;

**Revelation 5:10** — `θεῷ` (God)
> And hast made us unto our God kings and priests: and we shall reign on the earth.

**Revelation 6:9** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And when he had opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of them that were slain for the word of God, and for the testimony which they held:

**Revelation 7:2** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> And I saw another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God: and he cried with a loud voice to the four angels, to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea,

**Revelation 7:3** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> Saying, Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads.

**Revelation 7:10** — `θεῷ` (God)
> And cried with a loud voice, saying, Salvation to our God which sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb.

**Revelation 7:11** — `θεῷ` (God)
> And all the angels stood round about the throne, and about the elders and the four beasts, and fell before the throne on their faces, and worshipped God,

**Revelation 7:12** — `θεῷ` (God)
> Saying, Amen: Blessing, and glory, and wisdom, and thanksgiving, and honour, and power, and might, be unto our God for ever and ever. Amen.

**Revelation 7:15** — `θεοῦ` (of God)
> Therefore are they before the throne of God, and serve him day and night in his temple: and he that sitteth on the throne shall dwell among them.

**Revelation 7:17** — `θεὸς` (God)
> For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them, and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters: and God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes.

**Revelation 8:2** — `θεοῦ` (God)
> And I saw the seven angels which stood before God; and to them were given seven trumpets.

**Revelation 8:4** — `θεοῦ` (God)

---

## `G2962` — Kyrios ("Lord")

> Greek for 'Lord/Master' — applied to both God the Father and to Jesus. Equivalent of YHWH in LXX quotations.

**657 verses found.**

### Matthew (73 verses)

### Acts (101 verses)

**Acts 1:6** — `Κύριε` (Lord)

> And he was with them coming in and going out at Jerusalem.

> The word which God sent unto the children of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ: (he is Lord of all:)

> said, O full of all subtilty and all mischief, thou child of the devil, thou enemy of all righteousness, wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?

> And now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun for a season. And immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkness; and he went about seeking some to lead him by the hand.

> Then the deputy, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the doctrine of the Lord.

> But we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ we shall be saved, even as they.

> Men that have hazarded their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

> And Paul chose Silas, and departed, being recommended by the brethren unto the grace of God.

> And this was known to all the Jews and Greeks also dwelling at Ephesus; and fear fell on them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified.

**Acts 19:20** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> So mightily grew the word of God and prevailed.

**Acts 20:19** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Serving the Lord with all humility of mind, and with many tears, and temptations, which befell me by the lying in wait of the Jews:

> Testifying both to the Jews, and also to the Greeks, repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ.

> But none of these things move me, neither count I my life dear unto myself, so that I might finish my course with joy, and the ministry, which I have received of the Lord Jesus, to testify the gospel of the grace of God.

> I have shewed you all things, how that so labouring ye ought to support the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he said, It is more blessed to give than to receive.
