> And I said, Who art thou, Lord? And he said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest.

**Acts 28:31** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Preaching the kingdom of God, and teaching those things which concern the Lord Jesus Christ, with all confidence, no man forbidding him.

### Romans (38 verses)

**Romans 1:4** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> And declared to be the Son of God with power, according to the spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead:

**Romans 1:7** — `κυρίου` ([the] Lord)
> To all that be in Rome, beloved of God, called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.

**Romans 4:8** — `κύριος` ([the] Lord)
> Blessed is the man to whom the Lord will not impute sin.

**Romans 4:24** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> But for us also, to whom it shall be imputed, if we believe on him that raised up Jesus our Lord from the dead;

**Romans 5:1** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Therefore being justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ:

**Romans 5:11** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> And not only so, but we also joy in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom we have now received the atonement.

**Romans 5:21** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> That as sin hath reigned unto death, even so might grace reign through righteousness unto eternal life by Jesus Christ our Lord.

**Romans 6:23** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.

**Romans 7:25** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> I thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord. So then with the mind I myself serve the law of God; but with the flesh the law of sin.

**Romans 8:39** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

**Romans 9:28** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> For he will finish the work, and cut it short in righteousness: because a short work will the Lord make upon the earth.

**Romans 9:29** — `κύριος` ([the] Lord)
> And as Esaias said before, Except the Lord of Sabaoth had left us a seed, we had been as Sodoma, and been made like unto Gomorrha.

**Romans 10:9** — `κύριον` ([The] Lord [is])
> That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.

**Romans 10:12** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> For there is no difference between the Jew and the Greek: for the same Lord over all is rich unto all that call upon him.

**Romans 10:13** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> For whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved.

**Romans 10:16** — `Κύριε` (Lord)
> But they have not all obeyed the gospel. For Esaias saith, Lord, who hath believed our report?

**Romans 11:3** — `Κύριε` (Lord)
> Lord, they have killed thy prophets, and digged down thine altars; and I am left alone, and they seek my life.

**Romans 11:34** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> For who hath known the mind of the Lord? or who hath been his counsellor?

**Romans 12:11** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;

**Romans 12:19** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves, but rather give place unto wrath: for it is written, Vengeance is mine; I will repay, saith the Lord.

**Romans 13:14** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof.

**Romans 14:4** — `κυρίῳ` (master)
> Who art thou that judgest another man's servant? to his own master he standeth or falleth. Yea, he shall be holden up: for God is able to make him stand.

**Romans 14:6** — `κυρίῳ` (to [the] Lord)
> He that regardeth the day, regardeth it unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard it. He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.

**Romans 14:8** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we die unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord's.

**Romans 14:11** — `κύριος` ([the] Lord)
> For it is written, As I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.

**Romans 14:14** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> I know, and am persuaded by the Lord Jesus, that there is nothing unclean of itself: but to him that esteemeth any thing to be unclean, to him it is unclean.

**Romans 15:6** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> That ye may with one mind and one mouth glorify God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

**Romans 15:11** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> And again, Praise the Lord, all ye Gentiles; and laud him, all ye people.

**Romans 15:30** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Now I beseech you, brethren, for the Lord Jesus Christ's sake, and for the love of the Spirit, that ye strive together with me in your prayers to God for me;

**Romans 16:2** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> That ye receive her in the Lord, as becometh saints, and that ye assist her in whatsoever business she hath need of you: for she hath been a succourer of many, and of myself also.

**Romans 16:8** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Greet Amplias my beloved in the Lord.

**Romans 16:11** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Salute Herodion my kinsman. Greet them that be of the household of Narcissus, which are in the Lord.

**Romans 16:12** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Salute Tryphena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute the beloved Persis, which laboured much in the Lord.

**Romans 16:13** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Salute Rufus chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.

**Romans 16:18** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> For they that are such serve not our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by good words and fair speeches deceive the hearts of the simple.

**Romans 16:20** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> And the God of peace shall bruise Satan under your feet shortly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

**Romans 16:22** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you in the Lord.

**Romans 16:24** — `κυρίου` (lord: God)
> The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

### 1 Corinthians (56 verses)

**1 Corinthians 1:2** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Unto the church of God which is at Corinth, to them that are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, with all that in every place call upon the name of Jesus Christ our Lord, both theirs and ours:

**1 Corinthians 1:3** — `κυρίου` ([the] Lord)
> Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

**1 Corinthians 1:7** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> So that ye come behind in no gift; waiting for the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ:

**1 Corinthians 1:8** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Who shall also confirm you unto the end, that ye may be blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.

**1 Corinthians 1:9** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> God is faithful, by whom ye were called unto the fellowship of his Son Jesus Christ our Lord.

**1 Corinthians 1:10** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Now I beseech you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you; but that ye be perfectly joined together in the same mind and in the same judgment.

**1 Corinthians 1:31** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> That, according as it is written, He that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.

**1 Corinthians 2:8** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> Which none of the princes of this world knew: for had they known it, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.

**1 Corinthians 2:16** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> For who hath known the mind of the Lord, that he may instruct him? But we have the mind of Christ.

**1 Corinthians 3:5** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> Who then is Paul, and who is Apollos, but ministers by whom ye believed, even as the Lord gave to every man?

**1 Corinthians 3:20** — `Κύριος` ([The] Lord)
> And again, The Lord knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are vain.

**1 Corinthians 4:4** — `κύριός` ([the] Lord)
> For I know nothing by myself; yet am I not hereby justified: but he that judgeth me is the Lord.

**1 Corinthians 4:5** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord come, who both will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and will make manifest the counsels of the hearts: and then shall every man have praise of God.

**1 Corinthians 4:17** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> For this cause have I sent unto you Timotheus, who is my beloved son, and faithful in the Lord, who shall bring you into remembrance of my ways which be in Christ, as I teach every where in every church.

**1 Corinthians 4:19** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord will, and will know, not the speech of them which are puffed up, but the power.

**1 Corinthians 5:4** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, when ye are gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,

**1 Corinthians 5:5** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> To deliver such an one unto Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.

**1 Corinthians 6:11** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> And such were some of you: but ye are washed, but ye are sanctified, but ye are justified in the name of the Lord Jesus, and by the Spirit of our God.

**1 Corinthians 6:13** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Meats for the belly, and the belly for meats: but God shall destroy both it and them. Now the body is not for fornication, but for the Lord; and the Lord for the body.

**1 Corinthians 6:14** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> And God hath both raised up the Lord, and will also raise up us by his own power.

**1 Corinthians 6:17** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> But he that is joined unto the Lord is one spirit.

**1 Corinthians 7:10** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> And unto the married I command, yet not I, but the Lord, Let not the wife depart from her husband:

**1 Corinthians 7:12** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> But to the rest speak I, not the Lord: If any brother hath a wife that believeth not, and she be pleased to dwell with him, let him not put her away.

**1 Corinthians 7:17** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> But as God hath distributed to every man, as the Lord hath called every one, so let him walk. And so ordain I in all churches.

**1 Corinthians 7:22** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> For he that is called in the Lord, being a servant, is the Lord's freeman: likewise also he that is called, being free, is Christ's servant.

**1 Corinthians 7:25** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> Now concerning virgins I have no commandment of the Lord: yet I give my judgment, as one that hath obtained mercy of the Lord to be faithful.

**1 Corinthians 7:32** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> But I would have you without carefulness. He that is unmarried careth for the things that belong to the Lord, how he may please the Lord:

**1 Corinthians 7:34** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> There is difference also between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman careth for the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in spirit: but she that is married careth for the things of the world, how she may please her husband.

**1 Corinthians 7:35** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> And this I speak for your own profit; not that I may cast a snare upon you, but for that which is comely, and that ye may attend upon the Lord without distraction.

**1 Corinthians 7:39** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> The wife is bound by the law as long as her husband liveth; but if her husband be dead, she is at liberty to be married to whom she will; only in the Lord.

**1 Corinthians 8:5** — `κύριοι` (lords)
> For though there be that are called gods, whether in heaven or in earth, (as there be gods many, and lords many,)

**1 Corinthians 8:6** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> But to us there is but one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we in him; and one Lord Jesus Christ, by whom are all things, and we by him.

**1 Corinthians 9:1** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> Am I not an apostle? am I not free? have I not seen Jesus Christ our Lord? are not ye my work in the Lord?

**1 Corinthians 9:2** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> If I be not an apostle unto others, yet doubtless I am to you: for the seal of mine apostleship are ye in the Lord.

**1 Corinthians 9:5** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Have we not power to lead about a sister, a wife, as well as other apostles, and as the brethren of the Lord, and Cephas?

**1 Corinthians 9:14** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> Even so hath the Lord ordained that they which preach the gospel should live of the gospel.

**1 Corinthians 10:21** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord's table, and of the table of devils.

**1 Corinthians 10:22** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?

**1 Corinthians 10:26** — `κυρίου` (Lord’s)
> For the earth is the Lord's, and the fulness thereof.

**1 Corinthians 11:11** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Nevertheless neither is the man without the woman, neither the woman without the man, in the Lord.

**1 Corinthians 11:23** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, That the Lord Jesus the same night in which he was betrayed took bread:

**1 Corinthians 11:26** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> For as often as ye eat this bread, and drink this cup, ye do shew the Lord's death till he come.

**1 Corinthians 11:27** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Wherefore whosoever shall eat this bread, and drink this cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.

**1 Corinthians 11:32** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.

**1 Corinthians 12:3** — `Κύριος` (Lord [is])
> Wherefore I give you to understand, that no man speaking by the Spirit of God calleth Jesus accursed: and that no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, but by the Holy Ghost.

**1 Corinthians 12:5** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> And there are differences of administrations, but the same Lord.

**1 Corinthians 14:21** — `κύριος` ([the] Lord)
> In the law it is written, With men of other tongues and other lips will I speak unto this people; and yet for all that will they not hear me, saith the Lord.

**1 Corinthians 14:37** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> If any man think himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him acknowledge that the things that I write unto you are the commandments of the Lord.

**1 Corinthians 15:31** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> I protest by your rejoicing which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.

**1 Corinthians 15:57** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> But thanks be to God, which giveth us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.

**1 Corinthians 15:58** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye stedfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.

**1 Corinthians 16:7** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> For I will not see you now by the way; but I trust to tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.

**1 Corinthians 16:10** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also do.

**1 Corinthians 16:19** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> The churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.

**1 Corinthians 16:22** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maranatha.

**1 Corinthians 16:23** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

### 2 Corinthians (26 verses)

**2 Corinthians 1:2** — `κυρίου` ([the] Lord)
> Grace be to you and peace from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

**2 Corinthians 1:3** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Blessed be God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort;

**2 Corinthians 1:14** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> As also ye have acknowledged us in part, that we are your rejoicing, even as ye also are ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.

**2 Corinthians 2:12** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Furthermore, when I came to Troas to preach Christ's gospel, and a door was opened unto me of the Lord,

**2 Corinthians 3:16** — `κύριον` ([the] Lord)
> Nevertheless when it shall turn to the Lord, the vail shall be taken away.

**2 Corinthians 3:17** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> Now the Lord is that Spirit: and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.

**2 Corinthians 3:18** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> But we all, with open face beholding as in a glass the glory of the Lord, are changed into the same image from glory to glory, even as by the Spirit of the Lord.

**2 Corinthians 4:5** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> For we preach not ourselves, but Christ Jesus the Lord; and ourselves your servants for Jesus' sake.

**2 Corinthians 5:6** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Therefore we are always confident, knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:

**2 Corinthians 5:8** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> We are confident, I say, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.

**2 Corinthians 5:11** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord, we persuade men; but we are made manifest unto God; and I trust also are made manifest in your consciences.

**2 Corinthians 6:17** — `κύριος` ([the] Lord)
> Wherefore come out from among them, and be ye separate, saith the Lord, and touch not the unclean thing; and I will receive you,

**2 Corinthians 6:18** — `κύριος` ([the] Lord)
> And will be a Father unto you, and ye shall be my sons and daughters, saith the Lord Almighty.

**2 Corinthians 8:5** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> And this they did, not as we hoped, but first gave their own selves to the Lord, and unto us by the will of God.

**2 Corinthians 8:9** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> For ye know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that ye through his poverty might be rich.

**2 Corinthians 8:19** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> And not that only, but who was also chosen of the churches to travel with us with this grace, which is administered by us to the glory of the same Lord, and declaration of your ready mind:

**2 Corinthians 8:21** — `κυρίου` ([the] Lord)
> Providing for honest things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.

**2 Corinthians 10:8** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> For though I should boast somewhat more of our authority, which the Lord hath given us for edification, and not for your destruction, I should not be ashamed:

**2 Corinthians 10:17** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> But he that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.

**2 Corinthians 10:18** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> For not he that commendeth himself is approved, but whom the Lord commendeth.

**2 Corinthians 11:17** — `κύριον` ([the] Lord)
> That which I speak, I speak it not after the Lord, but as it were foolishly, in this confidence of boasting.

**2 Corinthians 11:31** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which is blessed for evermore, knoweth that I lie not.

**2 Corinthians 12:1** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> It is not expedient for me doubtless to glory. I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord.

**2 Corinthians 12:8** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> For this thing I besought the Lord thrice, that it might depart from me.

**2 Corinthians 13:10** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> Therefore I write these things being absent, lest being present I should use sharpness, according to the power which the Lord hath given me to edification, and not to destruction.

**2 Corinthians 13:13** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> All the saints salute you.

### Galatians (6 verses)

**Galatians 1:3** — `κυρίου` (the Lord)
> Grace be to you and peace from God the Father, and from our Lord Jesus Christ,

**Galatians 1:19** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord's brother.

**Galatians 4:1** — `κύριος` ([though] owner)
> Now I say, That the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all;

**Galatians 5:10** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> I have confidence in you through the Lord, that ye will be none otherwise minded: but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.

**Galatians 6:14** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.

**Galatians 6:18** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

### Ephesians (25 verses)

**Ephesians 1:2** — `κυρίου` ([the] Lord)
> Grace be to you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

**Ephesians 1:3** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who hath blessed us with all spiritual blessings in heavenly places in Christ:

**Ephesians 1:15** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Wherefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus, and love unto all the saints,

**Ephesians 1:17** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him:

**Ephesians 2:21** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> In whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple in the Lord:

**Ephesians 3:11** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> According to the eternal purpose which he purposed in Christ Jesus our Lord:

**Ephesians 4:1** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you that ye walk worthy of the vocation wherewith ye are called,

**Ephesians 4:5** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> One Lord, one faith, one baptism,

**Ephesians 4:17** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind,

**Ephesians 5:8** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> For ye were sometimes darkness, but now are ye light in the Lord: walk as children of light:

**Ephesians 5:10** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Proving what is acceptable unto the Lord.

**Ephesians 5:17** — `κυρίου` (Lord [is])
> Wherefore be ye not unwise, but understanding what the will of the Lord is.

**Ephesians 5:19** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord;

**Ephesians 5:20** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Giving thanks always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ;

**Ephesians 5:22** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.

**Ephesians 6:1** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Children, obey your parents in the Lord: for this is right.

**Ephesians 6:4** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> And, ye fathers, provoke not your children to wrath: but bring them up in the nurture and admonition of the Lord.

**Ephesians 6:5** — `κυρίοις` (masters)
> Servants, be obedient to them that are your masters according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as unto Christ;

**Ephesians 6:7** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> With good will doing service, as to the Lord, and not to men:

**Ephesians 6:8** — `κυρίου` ([the] Lord)
> Knowing that whatsoever good thing any man doeth, the same shall he receive of the Lord, whether he be bond or free.

**Ephesians 6:9** — `κύριοι` (masters)
> And, ye masters, do the same things unto them, forbearing threatening: knowing that your Master also is in heaven; neither is there respect of persons with him.

**Ephesians 6:10** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might.

**Ephesians 6:21** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> But that ye also may know my affairs, and how I do, Tychicus, a beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord, shall make known to you all things:

**Ephesians 6:23** — `κυρίου` ([the] Lord)
> Peace be to the brethren, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

**Ephesians 6:24** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> Grace be with all them that love our Lord Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen.

### Philippians (15 verses)

**Philippians 1:2** — `κυρίου` ([the] Lord)
> Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

**Philippians 1:14** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> And many of the brethren in the Lord, waxing confident by my bonds, are much more bold to speak the word without fear.

**Philippians 2:11** — `κύριος` ([is] Lord)
> And that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

**Philippians 2:19** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timotheus shortly unto you, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know your state.

**Philippians 2:24** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> But I trust in the Lord that I also myself shall come shortly.

**Philippians 2:29** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Receive him therefore in the Lord with all gladness; and hold such in reputation:

**Philippians 3:1** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Finally, my brethren, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not grievous, but for you it is safe.

**Philippians 3:8** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Yea doubtless, and I count all things but loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord: for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and do count them but dung, that I may win Christ,

**Philippians 3:20** — `κύριον` ([the] Lord)
> For our conversation is in heaven; from whence also we look for the Saviour, the Lord Jesus Christ:

**Philippians 4:1** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Therefore, my brethren dearly beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, so stand fast in the Lord, my dearly beloved.

**Philippians 4:2** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> I beseech Euodias, and beseech Syntyche, that they be of the same mind in the Lord.

**Philippians 4:4** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice.

**Philippians 4:5** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> Let your moderation be known unto all men. The Lord is at hand.

**Philippians 4:10** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly, that now at the last your care of me hath flourished again; wherein ye were also careful, but ye lacked opportunity.

**Philippians 4:23** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

### Colossians (13 verses)

**Colossians 1:3** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,

**Colossians 1:10** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing, being fruitful in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;

**Colossians 2:6** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him:

**Colossians 3:13** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye.

**Colossians 3:17** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.

**Colossians 3:18** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord.

**Colossians 3:20** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Children, obey your parents in all things: for this is well pleasing unto the Lord.

**Colossians 3:22** — `κυρίοις` (masters)
> Servants, obey in all things your masters according to the flesh; not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but in singleness of heart, fearing God:

**Colossians 3:23** — `κυρίῳ` (Lord)
> And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men;

**Colossians 3:24** — `κυρίου` ([the] Lord)
> Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.

**Colossians 4:1** — `κύριοι` (Masters)
> Masters, give unto your servants that which is just and equal; knowing that ye also have a Master in heaven.

**Colossians 4:7** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you, who is a beloved brother, and a faithful minister and fellowservant in the Lord:

**Colossians 4:17** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> And say to Archippus, Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it.

### 1 Thessalonians (22 verses)

**1 Thessalonians 1:1** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians which is in God the Father and in the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.

**1 Thessalonians 1:3** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Remembering without ceasing your work of faith, and labour of love, and patience of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, in the sight of God and our Father;

**1 Thessalonians 1:6** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> And ye became followers of us, and of the Lord, having received the word in much affliction, with joy of the Holy Ghost:

**1 Thessalonians 1:8** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> For from you sounded out the word of the Lord not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith to God-ward is spread abroad; so that we need not to speak any thing.

**1 Thessalonians 2:15** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> Who both killed the Lord Jesus, and their own prophets, and have persecuted us; and they please not God, and are contrary to all men:

**1 Thessalonians 2:19** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Are not even ye in the presence of our Lord Jesus Christ at his coming?

**1 Thessalonians 3:8** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> For now we live, if ye stand fast in the Lord.

**1 Thessalonians 3:11** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> Now God himself and our Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way unto you.

**1 Thessalonians 3:12** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> And the Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another, and toward all men, even as we do toward you:

**1 Thessalonians 3:13** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> To the end he may stablish your hearts unblameable in holiness before God, even our Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his saints.

**1 Thessalonians 4:1** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> Furthermore then we beseech you, brethren, and exhort you by the Lord Jesus, that as ye have received of us how ye ought to walk and to please God, so ye would abound more and more.

**1 Thessalonians 4:2** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> For ye know what commandments we gave you by the Lord Jesus.

**1 Thessalonians 4:6** — `κύριος` ([the] Lord)
> That no man go beyond and defraud his brother in any matter: because that the Lord is the avenger of all such, as we also have forewarned you and testified.

**1 Thessalonians 4:15** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, that we which are alive and remain unto the coming of the Lord shall not prevent them which are asleep.

**1 Thessalonians 4:16** — `κύριος` (Lord)
> For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:

**1 Thessalonians 4:17** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air: and so shall we ever be with the Lord.

**1 Thessalonians 5:2** — `κυρίου` (of [the] Lord)
> For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.

**1 Thessalonians 5:9** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> For God hath not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,

**1 Thessalonians 5:12** — `κυρίῳ` ([the] Lord)
> And we beseech you, brethren, to know them which labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you;

**1 Thessalonians 5:23** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

**1 Thessalonians 5:27** — `κύριον` (Lord)
> I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren.

**1 Thessalonians 5:28** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

### 2 Thessalonians (20 verses)

**2 Thessalonians 1:1** — `κυρίῳ` (the Lord)
> Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:

**2 Thessalonians 1:2** — `κυρίου` (the Lord)
> Grace unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

**2 Thessalonians 1:7** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> And to you who are troubled rest with us, when the Lord Jesus shall be revealed from heaven with his mighty angels,

**2 Thessalonians 1:8** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> In flaming fire taking vengeance on them that know not God, and that obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ:

**2 Thessalonians 1:9** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> Who shall be punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power;

**2 Thessalonians 1:12** — `κυρίου` (Lord)
> That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and ye in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.

## `G1203` — Despotēs ("Master / Sovereign Lord")

> Emphasizes absolute ownership and authority — used for God in Luke 2:29, Acts 4:24, Rev 6:10.

**10 verses found.**

### Luke (1 verses)

**Luke 2:29** — `δέσποτα` (Lord)
> Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:

### Acts (1 verses)

**Acts 4:24** — `Δέσποτα` (Sovereign Lord)
> And when they heard that, they lifted up their voice to God with one accord, and said, Lord, thou art God, which hast made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all that in them is:

### 1 Timothy (2 verses)

**1 Timothy 6:1** — `δεσπότας` (masters)
> Let as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and his doctrine be not blasphemed.

**1 Timothy 6:2** — `δεσπότας` (masters)
> And they that have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are brethren; but rather do them service, because they are faithful and beloved, partakers of the benefit. These things teach and exhort.

### 2 Timothy (1 verses)

**2 Timothy 2:21** — `δεσπότῃ` (Master)
> If a man therefore purge himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honour, sanctified, and meet for the master's use, and prepared unto every good work.

### Titus (1 verses)

**Titus 2:9** — `δεσπόταις` (masters)
> Exhort servants to be obedient unto their own masters, and to please them well in all things; not answering again;

### 1 Peter (1 verses)

**1 Peter 2:18** — `δεσπόταις` (masters)
> Servants, be subject to your masters with all fear; not only to the good and gentle, but also to the froward.

### 2 Peter (1 verses)

**2 Peter 2:1** — `δεσπότην` (Master)
> But there were false prophets also among the people, even as there shall be false teachers among you, who privily shall bring in damnable heresies, even denying the Lord that bought them, and bring upon themselves swift destruction.

### Jude (1 verses)

**Jude 1:4** — `δεσπότην` (master)
> For there are certain men crept in unawares, who were before of old ordained to this condemnation, ungodly men, turning the grace of our God into lasciviousness, and denying the only Lord God, and our Lord Jesus Christ.

### Revelation (1 verses)

**Revelation 6:10** — `δεσπότης` (Lord)
> And they cried with a loud voice, saying, How long, O Lord, holy and true, dost thou not judge and avenge our blood on them that dwell on the earth?

> And they sing the song of Moses the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and marvellous are thy works, Lord God Almighty; just and true are thy ways, thou King of saints.

**Revelation 16:14** — `παντοκράτορος` (Almighty)
> For they are the spirits of devils, working miracles, which go forth unto the kings of the earth and of the whole world, to gather them to the battle of that great day of God Almighty.

**Revelation 19:6** — `παντοκράτωρ` (Almighty)

## `G5310` — Hypsistos ("Most High")

> Greek equivalent of Elyon — 'the Most High God'. Used in Luke, Acts, Hebrews.

**13 verses found.**
